She reached out and affectionately ruffled his hair, a teasing smile playing on her lips. "Well, it seems there are some things even you can't master perfectly. That actually makes me feel a bit better about my own shortcomings!"

A small, reluctant smile touched Alexander's lips at her playful jab.

"You're right. I shouldn't create more work," he conceded, looking down at his food-stained clothes. "I should go and change."

"Alright," Evelyn said softly.

Just then, a faint click came from the kitchen doorway.

Alexander began to maneuver his wheelchair, but stopped short when he saw Sebastian standing there, phone in hand, having just taken a picture.

Caught in the act, Sebastian tried to quickly hide his phone, a mischievous grin spreading across his face.

"Sebastian, what are you doing?" Alexander asked, his tone laced with clear annoyance.

Sebastian felt a flicker of irritation. During the car ride over, Evelyn had assured him that Alexander wasn't as difficult as he seemed. Yet here was Alexander, treating him with the same old disdain.

He silently lamented how Alexander always seemed to have one set of rules for Evelyn and another, much stricter set, for him. He wished Evelyn could see this side of him—Alexander was never this sharp with her.

Alexander narrowed his eyes and wheeled closer, fixing Sebastian with a stern glare. "I saw you take that picture. Delete it. Right now. Or you'll regret making me angry."

Sebastian's eyes darted around, searching for an escape route.

He was used to seeing Alexander as the epitome of composure, always untouchable and impeccably put together. This disheveled, flustered version of the man was a rare sight.

Thinking of the photo now saved on his phone, Sebastian felt a strong desire to keep it. It was a snapshot of a truly unguarded moment.

Perhaps, he mused, this little piece of blackmail could come in handy someday.
###Chapter 220

Sebastian grinned mischievously. "Alexander, this is your first time cooking. It's a historic moment. I'm just preserving it for posterity!"

Alexander's expression darkened further.

It was true this was his inaugural attempt at cooking, but it had ended in spectacular failure, humiliating him in front of Evelyn. What was there to commemorate?

Sebastian seemed determined to antagonize him.

The more Alexander thought about it, the more his anger simmered.

Just as he was about to erupt, Evelyn, standing beside him, spoke up casually. "Mr. Hayes, may I see the photo you just took?"

Hearing her voice instantly soothed Alexander's rising temper.

He glanced at Evelyn, his curiosity piqued, and the demand to delete the photo died on his lips.

Without a second thought, Sebastian eagerly presented his phone to Evelyn.

"See? I have a real talent for this. The composition, the lighting... anyone would agree it's masterful."

Evelyn studied the image. It captured Alexander looking less rigid than usual, his hair slightly tousled, which lent him an unexpected warmth and appeal.

She thought he looked more attractive in this unguarded kitchen moment than he ever did in his perfectly tailored suits.

A smile touched her lips. "So this is what they mean by finding beauty in the unexpected."

The longer Evelyn looked, the more she appreciated the photo.

A realization struck her: she had no pictures of Alexander on her own phone.

"Mr. Hayes, could you send this photo to me? I'd like to keep it," she requested, feeling a sudden pang of regret for not having captured any memories of him herself.

She had no photos of Alexander, nor any of them together.

Sebastian agreed readily. "Of course! Let's exchange WhatsApp details."

Watching them swap contact information sparked a sharp, unexpected jealousy in Alexander.

Sebastian's smug grin was becoming intolerable.

Why was he still here, anyway?

Alexander grasped Evelyn's wrist, his voice a low murmur. "Evelyn, that's not necessary. I can take many photos for you."

Evelyn blinked, then smiled. "Alright. You'll have to promise to take lots of pictures with me then."

Alexander let out a quiet breath of relief. He was about to suggest she didn't need to add Sebastian on WhatsApp, but they had already connected.

Sebastian caught the resigned look on Alexander's face and bit back a laugh.

"You look really handsome in this one." Evelyn opened the photo, saved it, and then showed the screen to Alexander.

"Look. Mr. Hayes has a gift for candid shots. We have him to thank for this keepsake." Evelyn smiled, tucked her phone away securely, and declared with earnest determination, "I'm going to safeguard this photo. Even if I change phones."
###Chapter 221

You're Truly A Devoted Husband

Evelyn remembered the promise she had made to Sebastian earlier in his car.

She had agreed to speak well of him in front of Alexander.

This small act would hopefully make Alexander more receptive to future meetings with Sebastian.

Knowing how rarely Alexander formed genuine friendships, Evelyn wanted to prevent a momentary impulse from damaging their relationship.

Alexander, however, viewed the situation differently.

He noticed Evelyn's sincere appreciation for the photograph.

This prompted him to reluctantly extend an olive branch toward Sebastian.

After agreeing to keep the photograph, Alexander issued a stern warning. "This is a one-time exception. Do not take photos without permission again."

Evelyn gently patted Alexander's shoulder. "Please, don't be so harsh."

Alexander paused, seeming ready to defend himself. "I wasn't being harsh. Just stating a necessary reminder."

Sebastian observed the exchange with quiet amazement.

He realized seeking Evelyn's help had been the correct decision.

The change in Alexander's attitude was both immediate and remarkable.

It made him wonder—had he ever noticed how skilled Alexander was at modulating his emotions?

Simultaneously, Sebastian felt a sense of shock.

Alexander, usually so decisive and unyielding, was now heeding a woman's advice.

Was this a testament to Evelyn's influence or Alexander's newfound adaptability?

Evelyn laughed softly, brushing some dust from Alexander's jacket.

Alexander raised his hand to stop her. "It's dirty. Don't touch it."

"Then go change quickly. I'll have the staff clean the kitchen," Evelyn said, turning to find the maids.

Alexander moved toward Sebastian. "What did you want to discuss?"

Sebastian adopted a serious tone. "It's an important matter."

Alexander seemed unimpressed. "I need to change first, then have dinner with Evelyn. She's been on her feet all day and hasn't eaten properly. I've already inconvenienced her enough..."

As Alexander continued to elaborate on his concern for Evelyn, Sebastian interrupted. "Alright, I understand. You care about your wife. No need for further explanation. This is truly urgent. Let's go to the study."

Evelyn, having just finished speaking with the maids, heard Sebastian's plea and glanced at Alexander.

To her surprise, Alexander immediately refused. "I'm busy. Come back another time."

Evelyn quickly intervened. "Alexander, please go to the study and speak with Mr. Hayes. I'll manage things here with the staff."

She approached him, leaned down, and whispered softly near his ear. "Mr. Hayes is your friend and our guest. Remember your manners."

Alexander's expression darkened with a frown. He seemed poised to argue, but the firm look in Evelyn's eyes stopped him.

He paused, then offered her a conciliatory smile and gently took her hand.

"Alright, I understand. Don't overexert yourself. Let the staff handle everything and ensure they prepare something you'll enjoy," Alexander conceded.

Evelyn nodded in agreement and gestured for Alexander's assistant to come forward and push his wheelchair.

After changing clothes, Alexander joined Sebastian in the study.

The moment the door closed, Alexander noticed Sebastian giving him a peculiar look.

Alexander scowled. "What's with that expression?"

Sebastian shook his head, sat on the sofa, and let out a heavy sigh. "How did I not see it before? You're truly a devoted husband. It seems you follow your wife's suggestions without any resistance."
###Chapter 222

Alexander remained perfectly composed, his tone even. "What's wrong with following my wife's suggestions without question? Evelyn genuinely cares for me. I'm perfectly happy to let her take the lead."

He then turned the tables on Sebastian. "That's more than I can say for you. Have you even found a woman who can handle you? You've drifted through one shallow affair after another, never settling down."

Sebastian's face tightened at the direct hit.

It was an undeniable truth. For years, Sebastian had floated from one meaningless romance to the next. None had ever developed into something real. They were all drawn to his fortune and status, hoping to secure his favor.

This pattern had bred a deep cynicism in him about love, ultimately leading to his decision to remain a bachelor.

In stark contrast, Evelyn's love for Alexander was pure. She saw past his wealth and his physical challenges, focusing solely on being the supportive wife he needed.

Truly, Alexander was a fortunate man.

The more Sebastian thought about it, the more irritated he became. He shot back, "Alexander, are you really so confident that Evelyn will always be yours?"

He let out a hollow laugh. "Be careful. I might just charm her away from you."

The words were spoken impulsively, but the immediate change in Alexander's demeanor was chilling. His voice dropped to an icy tone. "You dare?"

Sebastian froze under Alexander's piercing gaze, a cold shiver running down his spine. He quickly backtracked.

"No, no! It was just a joke!"

He knew a confrontation with Alexander could escalate dangerously.

Trying to defuse the tension, Sebastian hastily added, "Remember, Alexander, Evelyn asked you to be nice to me, your guest. She'd be upset to know you were being harsh. She's put so much effort into this family, and you're not even cooperating. She might end up crying over this!"

Alexander took a deep breath, forcing his anger down. After a moment, he replied coldly, "I'll let it slide this time."

But before Sebastian could feel relief, Alexander added with stern finality, "But remember this, Sebastian. Evelyn is not a trophy to be won. Never make a joke like that again, or you will regret it."

Sebastian was taken aback. He hadn't expected his offhand comment to provoke such a severe reaction.

Looking back, he acknowledged his own recklessness.

Evelyn was Alexander's cherished partner, not an object to be discussed so lightly.

Sebastian wiped the cold sweat from his brow, a creeping sense of fear overwhelming him.

Alexander had masked his true nature so completely around Evelyn that even Sebastian had nearly forgotten how intimidating he could be.

It was common knowledge in Crestwood that provoking the founder of Aethelgard Industries was a grave mistake.

Sebastian exhaled slowly, reminding himself to be more cautious in the future.

Seeing the depth of Alexander's affection for Evelyn, Sebastian understood that while he might risk angering Alexander, he could never afford to alienate Evelyn.

Deciding to abandon the dangerous topic entirely, Sebastian swiftly changed the subject. "Right, right, I almost forgot. I came to see you today for something important. Seriously important."

Alexander gave him a wary look. "Out with it."

Sebastian's expression turned grave. "Theodore is returning to Crestwood."

Alexander paused. "Theodore has been overseas for years. What brings him back now?"

###Chapter 223

Sebastian leaned forward, a mysterious grin spreading across his face. "I heard Theodore is coming back to find a woman."

"A woman? Who?" Surprise flickered in Alexander's eyes as he looked up, eager for details.

Sebastian chuckled. "Remember two years ago? Theodore was ambushed overseas. He was critically injured, on the verge of death. A woman saved him. After he recovered, he spent two years searching for her but never found a trace." Sebastian paused, reflecting. "His return is undoubtedly connected to finding his savior."

Alexander responded calmly, "Anyone brave enough to save Theodore in such a situation must be quite remarkable."

But Sebastian, with a sly grin, was already several steps ahead. "Alexander, think about it. Theodore has tried to kill you multiple times. Now that we know his motive, why don't we locate this woman first? If we find her, we gain significant leverage."

Without hesitation, Alexander dismissed the idea. "No," he ordered firmly. "This is a personal matter between Theodore and me. We will not involve an innocent person."

Sebastian sighed, clearly disappointed by the rebuttal.

Once Alexander made a decision, he rarely wavered. Still, Sebastian tried another angle.

"We don't need to hurt her. Just finding her and making contact could turn her into an unexpected ally," Sebastian suggested.

He continued to press his point. "Consider Theodore's ruthlessness. With him back in the city, his plans are unknown. We must stay ahead of him."

Alexander's expression remained stoic, showing no sign of agreement. Seeing this, Sebastian played his final card, the one thing guaranteed to sway his friend.

"If you won't consider your own safety, then think of Evelyn. Your marriage to her is public knowledge in Crestwood. If Theodore discovers your true identity, he will not hesitate to target her. While you're busy protecting bystanders, Evelyn could already be in danger. What will you do then?"

The mention of Evelyn's name instantly altered Alexander's expression.

He could handle Theodore's schemes against him, but the thought of Evelyn in danger was unbearable.

Frowning, Alexander conceded, "You're right. Theodore will stop at nothing to get to me."

"Exactly!" Sebastian clapped his hands together, his face lighting up with satisfaction.

The mention of Evelyn had clearly hit its mark.

Alexander paused, considering his options. After a moment, he relented. "Fine. Look into the woman's identity and whereabouts. But my order stands: she is not to be harmed. We merely ask for her cooperation."

"Understood, understood," Sebastian replied, getting to his feet and heading for the door.

Meanwhile, Evelyn had just finished helping the maids clean the kitchen, who were now busy preparing dinner.

Noticing Sebastian leaving, Evelyn called out, her curiosity piqued. "Mr. Hayes, you're leaving already? Won't you stay for dinner with us?"
###Chapter 224

Your Friend Is Quite The Narcissist

Sebastian glanced back at Evelyn.

He had urgent business to handle and couldn't afford to delay by sharing a meal with the couple.

Considering Alexander's possessive nature, he decided it was wiser not to stay.

A playful grin spread across Sebastian's face. "Honestly, it's better if I don't. I'm far too irresistible. If I linger, you might find yourself captivated. Alexander is a dear friend, and I'd hate to create an uncomfortable situation."

With that, he made a swift exit, leaving Evelyn sitting on the sofa, completely stunned.

Alexander wheeled into the room and noticed Evelyn's bewildered expression. "Is everything alright?" he asked.

Evelyn offered a faint smile. "Your friend has an incredibly high opinion of himself. I completely misjudged his character."

Alexander raised an eyebrow. "Pay him no mind."

"Alright, let's eat. I'm absolutely starving." Evelyn stood up and helped guide Alexander's wheelchair to the dining table.

They enjoyed their meal together, sharing light conversation and laughter throughout.

Evelyn arrived at the office exactly on time the following morning.

To her great surprise, Cassandra was already there, waiting much earlier than expected.

"Cassandra, what are you doing here so early?" Evelyn inquired.

Cassandra responded with a radiant smile. "You told me last night the gown was finished. I was so excited I could barely sleep. Please, can we go see it now? I simply can't wait another moment."

"Of course." Evelyn agreed. After setting down her things, she escorted Cassandra to the studio where the dress was stored.

As they reached the display cabinet, Evelyn unlocked it and carefully removed the mannequin inside.

"Be careful with it," Cassandra advised softly from behind her.

Unseen by Evelyn, a sly expression flickered across Cassandra's face.

Evelyn then gently lifted the protective cloth, fully revealing the completed gown to Cassandra.

Cassandra examined it with sheer delight, her face lighting up. "Oh, Evelyn! It's absolutely breathtaking!"

Just then, Nathanial entered and saw the dress Evelyn had created. He nodded in approval. "Truly remarkable. Evelyn continues to impress Beatrice, proving once again what an exceptionally talented designer she is."

Cassandra turned to Evelyn with a satisfied smile. "I'm so incredibly glad I chose to work with you."

Evelyn blushed slightly. "Cassandra, would you like to try it on now?"

"Absolutely, please help me," Cassandra agreed.

Together, they carefully lifted the dress from the mannequin and moved to the nearby fitting room.

Once Cassandra was wearing the gown, she admired her reflection in the mirror.

Evelyn stood behind her, making minor adjustments.

"How does it feel? Are you happy with it?" Evelyn asked. "If anything needs altering, I can fix it immediately."

Cassandra touched the fabric at her waist. "The waist feels a little tight. Could you let it out just a bit? I may have indulged a little too much since coming back home."

Evelyn nodded. "Of course, I'll loosen it slightly for you."

Cassandra suggested a few other minor changes.

"When the day of the red carpet arrives, I'll wear this dress and take plenty of photos for promotion online," Cassandra beamed. "We must connect on Twitter then."

"Okay." Evelyn nodded in agreement.
###Chapter 225

Evelyn Won't Reign Supreme Indefinitely

After Cassandra Sterling left, Nathanial Reed couldn't stop praising Evelyn.

"Evelyn, I initially thought you were just another beginner. But you've consistently exceeded my expectations. Keep this up."

"It's only a matter of time before you lead our design department. I wouldn't be surprised if the entire fashion industry soon knows your name!"

Nathanial beamed. "And when that day comes, I'll benefit too—think promotions and bonuses."

Evelyn offered a humble smile. "I owe it all to your guidance, Nathanial. You made it possible for me to complete this gown so flawlessly. Thank you for everything."

Her sincere gratitude made Nathanial's smile widen. He seized the moment to praise her publicly, urging their colleagues to learn from her example.

While most staff went to the cafeteria for lunch, Evelyn remained in the studio, refining the gown's final details.

"Evelyn's dedication is truly remarkable. No wonder she's so highly regarded."

"I admit, I was initially envious of her seemingly quick success. But seeing her work ethic, it feels deserved. She truly earns her accolades."

"Exactly. I heard she's been the first to arrive and last to leave for two weeks straight. Working tirelessly."

"Evelyn is definitely making her mark. With Cassandra's upcoming red carpet appearance, she's bound to get more orders." A colleague mused aloud, curiosity evident. "What does the gown Evelyn designed for Cassandra look like? We haven't seen it. Cassandra was here for an early fitting. Judging by her and Nathanial's reactions, it must be spectacular."

"Absolutely. It's going to stun everyone. We'll all see it at the red carpet event soon. I can't wait!"

Just then, Genevieve Prescott walked by with her lunch, overhearing the praise.

She scoffed sarcastically. "Oh, please. The event hasn't even happened. For all we know, Evelyn's design could be a disaster. If it fails, it will embarrass all of us."

Her comment caused an immediate stir.

"Genevieve, maybe save the negativity. Focus on improving your own skills. You've missed your sales targets for two consecutive months. Fall short again this month, and Nathanial might have to let you go."

Genevieve, visibly irritated, had no retort. She shot back resentfully before storming off, "Just wait. Evelyn won't be on top forever."

The others exchanged knowing glances, shaking their heads before resuming their conversation.

"She despises Evelyn more than anyone else here."

"Let's just ignore her and enjoy our lunch."

Finally, the day of the red carpet event arrived. Aethelgard Industries and Cassandra Sterling jointly released promotional photos on social media, featuring Cassandra in the breathtaking gown.

The moment the photos went live online, excitement exploded.

"Is that really Cassandra Sterling?"

"Look! Your favorite actress is absolutely radiant tonight!"

"This gown is a masterpiece."

"Cassandra's usual soft, gentle style is completely transformed. This avant-garde design makes her look powerful and striking!"

"The futuristic aesthetic suits her perfectly, giving her an aura of mystery. I'm completely captivated!"
###Chapter 226

Evelyn, You're Our Shining Star!

"The gown is a masterpiece. It perfectly captures Cassandra's iconic essence while introducing a bold, new vision. It's breathtaking!"

"I've been a fan for a decade. Cassandra, you have my unwavering support!"

"Cassandra's beauty is transcendent. She owns every style. Who dares call her predictable now?"

"Does anyone know the designer? The credits list someone from Aethelgard Industries."

"Aethelgard's designers haven't explored this aesthetic before. Is there a new prodigy in their ranks?"

Intrigued, countless netizens scoured Aethelgard's design team roster but found no answers.

As speculation grew, Cassandra posted a comment and tagged Evelyn's account. "The guessing game is over, everyone. Meet the brilliant mind behind this creation—an exceptional new designer!"

The online crowd was stunned.

"A newcomer designed this? That's incredible!"

"Evelyn, your name is now unforgettable."

"She just joined Aethelgard and has already created a masterpiece. What an achievement!"

"Evelyn, please design for our favorite stars. We're so tired of the mediocre fashion choices."

Instantly, Evelyn's social media profile exploded with new followers.

Fans and netizens flooded her page, praising her work. Many begged her to design for other celebrities.

The energy was electric and full of excitement.

Aethelgard Industries was inundated with inquiries, many specifically requesting Evelyn.

Nathanial couldn't contain his joy and hurried to Evelyn's desk. "Evelyn, you're our shining star! We're throwing a celebration for your success after this red carpet event."

Her colleagues gathered, offering their congratulations.

"Evelyn, congratulations! Your reputation has skyrocketed. We're all so happy for you!"

Evelyn smiled warmly. "Thank you."

Nathanial chuckled. "This dress's success isn't just a win for Evelyn."

The entire office buzzed with excitement.

"Really? Wasn't this Evelyn's design? Why are they asking about us?" a colleague inquired curiously.

Nathanial smiled. "Online chatter suggests that if a newcomer like Evelyn can produce such phenomenal work, then the experienced designers at Aethelgard must be even more extraordinary. Naturally, they want to collaborate. In short, the universal praise for Evelyn's design is a massive victory for our entire team!"

Colleagues laughed joyfully, thanking Evelyn as they shook her hands.

"Evelyn, we owe you so much!"

Evelyn was both amused and touched by their gratitude. "You're all incredibly talented—you just needed the right opportunity. I'm happy to have helped."

Away from the celebration, Genevieve stood quietly at a distance, observing.

There was no resentment on her face.

Instead, she smiled slyly and let out a soft, cold chuckle.
###Chapter 227

You'll Find Out Soon Enough

Genevieve understood perfectly that Evelyn's sudden popularity was merely a temporary phenomenon.

The more praise Evelyn gathered at this moment, the harder and faster her eventual fall would be.

A smug smile played on Genevieve's lips as she savored the thought of Evelyn's impending disgrace.

Soon, everyone would turn on Evelyn. They would criticize her, shun her. The admiration would vanish completely.

Taking a deep breath, Genevieve could scarcely contain her excitement. She pulled out her phone and typed a quick message.

"Begin now."

A reply appeared on her screen moments later. "Understood."

With a triumphant smirk, Genevieve walked to the lounge to get some water, her steps light and airy.

The mental image of Evelyn's imminent public shaming filled Genevieve with intense satisfaction.

She was so engrossed in her pleasant thoughts that she started humming a tune.

Several colleagues entered the lounge, surprised by Genevieve's unusually cheerful mood.

They exchanged confused glances.

They had expected Genevieve to be furious about Evelyn's recent acclaim. Finding her not just calm, but actually happy and humming, was baffling.

Had she lost her mind?

One colleague, overcome with curiosity, finally asked, "Genevieve, what has you in such high spirits?"

Genevieve offered a mysterious smile. "You'll find out soon enough."

Meanwhile, online excitement for that evening's red carpet event was intensifying. People were already applauding Evelyn's innovative designs.

However, the situation shifted dramatically with the arrival of a post from the renowned designer, Julian Thorne.

Julian's tweet, which tagged both Cassandra and Evelyn, instantly captured the attention of countless netizens.

"Today began as an ordinary day. I was at home, immersed in my new designs, when a friend contacted me. They pointed out that Cassandra's red carpet gown bore a startling resemblance to a design I released previously. Shocked, I logged into Twitter, a platform I haven't checked in some time. I saw the trending topics immediately. I must question Aethelgard Industries, a company with years of history and a roster of talented designers. Why would you need to copy the work of a designer you might consider outdated? Has your organization grown complacent with its influence? And Evelyn, you are young, full of potential, with a bright future ahead. You could easily rely on your own talent. So, why resort to plagiarism? I urge all netizens to judge for themselves. Visit the Crestwood Design platform's official website. I uploaded this design just two weeks ago. The dress Evelyn created for Cassandra is nearly identical, with only minor alterations. I am not seeking publicity. I simply demand a public apology. Without one, I will use the evidence I have to ensure the plagiarist faces the full consequences."

This post sent a shockwave across the internet.

Cassandra, a rising actress rebuilding her career, was already attracting significant attention.

Aethelgard Industries, a giant in Crestwood, had maintained a flawless reputation for years.

The allegation of plagiarism against their designer propelled the post to the top of the trends, sparking widespread outrage.
###Chapter 228

Let's Not Condemn An Innocent

Julian Thorne was once the undisputed star of the design world.
His unique creations placed him at the industry's pinnacle.
Top celebrities once fought for the privilege of wearing his gowns.
But his star had faded in recent years.
A prolonged creative drought had seen his presence diminish.

"The internet is exploding! Twitter hasn't seen drama like this in ages."
"Can you believe it? Cassandra Sterling is wearing a dress by a known plagiarist. This is insane."
"Leave Cassandra out of this! She's a victim here. The blame lies entirely with that inexperienced designer. Cassandra was just trying to support a newcomer, and this is the thanks she gets? Why is she receiving this backlash?"
"I'm furious! As a loyal fan of Cassandra, I've been counting down to this red carpet. How could it go so wrong? Aethelgard Industries, you need to take responsibility. Stop hiding! Drag that naive designer out and force her to apologize to Cassandra. Immediately!"
"Evelyn, you should be ashamed for copying an established designer's work. You owe everyone an apology. Perhaps you should just leave the industry for good."
"The Sterling family, enough with the damage control. It's exhausting watching your blindly loyal fans defend you. Clearly, that rookie designer was just a pawn in Cassandra's larger scheme."
"Let me be clear. Cassandra has been anticipating this red carpet for a full month. She knows the caliber of designers at Aethelgard Industries. She commissioned her gown well in advance, fully aware of the competition she'd face. Would she really gamble her entire reputation by knowingly choosing a plagiarist? Open your eyes, people! Cassandra has been set up! She's just trying to reclaim her status among the domestic elite after her time abroad."
"Evelyn, you need to come forward and explain yourself. Are you trying to sabotage Cassandra? After she went out of her way to promote you, this is your repayment? Bravo. We will be boycotting Aethelgard Industries' designers from now on."

Within thirty minutes, Julian Thorne posted a new update.

"I started the initial sketches for this design six months ago.
I completed the final piece just one month ago.
After several refinements, I officially released it on my platform.
I am utterly baffled as to how Evelyn could have copied my work.
Even more shocking, she convinced Cassandra Sterling to wear it on the red carpet.
This is a profound insult to the art of design itself.
I demand strict action be taken!
Furthermore, having collaborated with Cassandra in the past, I know her to be a person of integrity.
I sincerely doubt she would stoop to such levels.
There is undoubtedly more to this story.
A thorough investigation is necessary to clear Cassandra's name.
Let's not condemn an innocent person."

Julian's latest post effectively confirmed the growing suspicion.
It suggested Evelyn had concealed the fact that the gown was plagiarized.

"Evelyn has some nerve, daring to imitate Julian Thorne's design. His experience far surpasses hers."
"It's truly brazen. Cassandra always commands the spotlight. To plagiarize for such a high-profile event is like playing with fire."
"Maybe Evelyn ran out of original ideas and thought Julian, being past his prime, wouldn't attract much attention."
"Wait, is it possible that Evelyn didn't actually copy Julian's designs?"
"Whose side are you on? The release timeline clearly points to plagiarism."
"Absolutely. Julian has a stellar reputation. Why would he falsely accuse an unknown newcomer?"
###Chapter 229

229 Can You Provide Any Clarification

"I agree with the previous comment. The name Evelyn was completely unknown to me before this."

"Ever since Cassandra announced she would wear a red carpet gown by a new designer, I had my doubts. How could someone with no experience create something so stunning? I stayed quiet while everyone praised it, but it never felt right."

Within hours, the initial admiration for Evelyn on Twitter twisted into vicious accusations of plagiarism.

"Are you ignoring this mess? Are you trying to ruin Cassandra? Did a rival send you?"

"Cassandra has won countless awards. It seemed strange she would pick an unknown like Evelyn when she has access to the world's top designers."

"How dare you, a beginner, so brazenly steal from an established artist like Julian Thorne? Your career is over."

"I found a picture of her. She's attractive. She seemed nice at first, but you can't trust appearances."

The internet was ablaze with the scandal of Cassandra's dress and the plagiarism claims.

"What is wrong with you people? Why can't you get Cassandra a reliable designer?"

"It's time for a complete management shake-up! Cassandra is back to reclaim her throne. Shouldn't you be working harder? How could you let this happen for her big red carpet return? Does anyone there actually care about their job?"

"I'm speechless. Cassandra deserves so much better than this chaos. When will she finally be treated right?"

"Remember, your paychecks come from Cassandra! Could you please focus? What's the plan for tonight's event?"

Instead of addressing the concerns, Cassandra's studio quickly deleted all photos of her in the gown Evelyn designed from their social media platforms.

This move was widely interpreted as a confession of guilt, solidifying the belief that Evelyn had copied the design.

Amid the turmoil, Cassandra's manager, Genevieve Hartman, called Nathanial.

"Nathanial, what in the world is going on? Can you provide any clarification? Your designer's actions have severely damaged Cassandra's reputation. How is she supposed to show her face tonight? Cassandra is too afraid to even look at her phone, she breaks down crying every time she sees a message from a fan! Nathanial, what is your plan to fix this?"

Nathanial, stunned by the rapid escalation, was equally concerned. "Don't worry, I'm handling it."

But Genevieve was relentless. "There's no time for a lengthy investigation! It's obvious your novice designer, Evelyn, copied Julian Thorne's work. The dress is unusable. We need a replacement immediately, even something off-the-rack will do. Send it over now so Cassandra can change!"
###Chapter 230

I Didn't Plagiarize Anything!

Nathanial Reed rubbed his temples, the strain clear on his face.
"Genevieve, please, this has to be a misunderstanding."
"Perhaps Evelyn didn't actually plagiarize."
"We can still fix this."

He had been counting on Evelyn securing Cassandra Sterling's design order.
It was supposed to mean a promotion and a raise for him.
Now, with this scandal breaking, it felt like a curse.

Genevieve Hartman, Cassandra's manager, was having none of it.
"Don't give me that rubbish."
"Plagiarism is plagiarism."
"Cassandra is about to walk the red carpet."
"The only logical choice is to change designers immediately!"
"Nathanial, listen carefully."
"If you ignore my advice now, Cassandra will cut all ties with your agency."
"Your next move decides everything."

Nathanial's heart dropped like a stone.
Losing Cassandra as a client because of Evelyn was a nightmare scenario.

"Genevieve, I understand."
"I'll do exactly as you say," Nathanial replied, forcing a placating smile.
"We have many talented designers."
"But given the red carpet is so soon, they might only be able to offer pre-made gowns."

"That's acceptable," Genevieve stated flatly.
"As long as it suits Cassandra's style and hasn't been seen online, it will work."
"We are out of time."

"Of course, no problem," Nathanial assured her.
"Has Cassandra mentioned any preference for a specific designer?"
"We will make sure she is happy."

Genevieve answered, "Cassandra suggested her cousin, Genevieve Prescott, a designer in your department."
"Start with her."
"See if she has any suitable ready-to-wear pieces."
"If not, any competent designer will suffice."

"Understood, completely understood," Nathanial said, feeling a wave of relief.
He then ventured cautiously, "And what should we do about Evelyn?"

After a short silence, Genevieve let out a cold, dismissive snort.
"Evelyn's mistake is severe."
"She must face strict disciplinary action."
"Nathanial, keeping someone like her will drag your entire agency down."
"You risk damaging relationships with other clients."
"It would be a catastrophe."

Nathanial hesitated for a moment before responding gravely, "I see."
"I'll handle it."

Meanwhile, Evelyn was completely unaware of the firestorm online.
She was absorbed in her work, focused on refining new design techniques.

Earlier, Cassandra had contacted her, inviting her to attend the red carpet event after work.

Evelyn's phone had been vibrating softly for some time.
She ignored it, assuming the alerts were just notifications about the gown's official reveal that morning.

Suddenly, a colleague approached her desk, her expression deeply concerned.
"Evelyn, why are you still working?"
"Haven't you seen what's happening online?"
"You're in serious trouble."

Evelyn looked up, her face a mask of startled confusion.
"What?"

Her colleague paused, clearly searching for the right words.
"You really haven't seen it?"
"A famous designer has publicly accused you of plagiarism on the internet..."

Evelyn's eyes widened in shock, her brows furrowing in disbelief.
"What?"
"That's impossible!"
"I didn't plagiarize anything!"

"You should check Twitter immediately," the colleague urged, her voice filled with sympathy.

Where there might once have been envy among her peers, now there was only pity.

Even though the plagiarism accusation was unproven, public opinion had already turned.
It strongly suggested Cassandra would not be wearing Evelyn's design.

A heavy sense of dread settled in Evelyn's stomach.
She quickly snatched her phone and opened Twitter.

The moment she logged in, a flood of harsh comments and accusations bombarded her.

Her face lost all color as she scrolled.
The full gravity of the situation crashed down upon her.
###Chapter 231

Evelyn, You're So Naive

Evelyn just discovered that Julian Thorne, a celebrated name in the design world, had publicly accused her of plagiarism.

The accusation was a lie. The gown was entirely her own creation.

Understanding the severity of the scandal, she rose quickly and rushed to Nathanial's office.

"Nathanial, we need to talk. It's urgent," she said, her voice tight.

Nathanial looked up from his desk, observing her distressed state. A faint smile touched his lips. "I know why you're here. The online firestorm. Sit. Have some coffee. There's no need to panic."

Coffee was the last thing on Evelyn's mind. She leaned forward, her hands gripping the edge of his desk. "Nathanial, I swear I didn't plagiarize. You must believe me."

He nodded calmly. "I do believe you."

His expression then turned grave. "But you've seen the public reaction. This situation is volatile. It won't be resolved quickly. We need time to manage it. For now, your profile is too high. Don't come into the office. Go home and stay out of sight."

Evelyn had braced for blame, fearing Nathanial would hold her responsible for the negative attention.

His supportive words washed over her, bringing a wave of relief.

At least he was in her corner.

After a moment, she ventured, "What is the company's plan to address this?"

"Leave that to me and upper management," Nathanial stated firmly. He gave her a pointed look. "Your only task is to compile every piece of evidence that proves the design is yours. Bring it all to me."

Evelyn stood straighter, resolve hardening her features. "I'll do it immediately."

Every sketch, every note, every draft that documented the gown's creation was hers.

She gathered the materials swiftly. Returning to his office, she placed the thick folder on his desk. "Is this sufficient?"

Nathanial flipped through the documents, his eyes scanning the pages. He gave a decisive nod. "This is perfect. With this, we should be able to clear your name soon."

Evelyn released a breath she didn't realize she'd been holding.

Then Nathanial added, his tone authoritative, "We'll also take control of your social media accounts. The company will issue an official statement. Until then, ignore all the noise. Focus on resting and preparing for your next project."

Evelyn listened intently to Nathanial's instructions, agreeing to cooperate fully.

She looked at him with profound gratitude. "Nathanial, thank you for trusting me."

He offered a reassuring smile. "Of course. I've witnessed your talent and dedication since you joined Aethelgard Industries. I know your character."

Feeling reassured, Evelyn left the office, convinced of Nathanial's support. The moment the door closed behind her, his smile vanished.

His face darkened with cold contempt.

Shaking his head, he muttered under his breath, "Evelyn, you're so naive. Do you truly think we will expend resources to defend you? You are just a junior designer. Why would we risk alienating Cassandra Sterling and Julian Thorne for your sake? I won't waste my energy on such a futile endeavor."
###Chapter 232

Evelyn Is Immensely Talented

Nathanial Reed relaxed in his chair.

He felt no concern for Evelyn's difficult situation.

Sacrificing her seemed a reasonable cost to secure his own position.

It also offered a perfect chance to gain favor with the famous Cassandra Sterling.

To Nathanial, the decision was obvious.

How could Evelyn, a mere beginner, ever hope to rival someone of Cassandra's caliber?

With this logic, he meticulously erased all digital traces related to Evelyn.

Elsewhere, Cassandra was preparing for the evening's red carpet event.

The day's plagiarism scandal appeared to have little effect on her.

She maintained her composure, trying on a gown designed by Genevieve Prescott.

Once the dress was on, Genevieve immediately offered praise. "Cassandra, you look breathtaking. This dress was made for you."

Cassandra stared at her reflection, her face impassive.

Noticing the silence, Genevieve approached carefully. "Is something wrong? Does it not fit correctly?"

Cassandra released a soft sigh. She touched the fabric of the hem, her interest clearly fading. "Genevieve, your work simply doesn't compare to Evelyn's."

Genevieve's expression stiffened.

Cassandra continued, unaware of the sting her words carried. "After wearing one of Evelyn's creations, this feels... ordinary."

A sharp pain clenched in Genevieve's chest. She forced a tight smile. "My apologies, Cassandra. I will strive to create a design more worthy of you next time."

Since secretly agreeing to work with Cassandra, Genevieve had poured all her effort into this single dress.

She believed she had succeeded, but Cassandra's reaction told a different story.

This realization fueled Genevieve's resentment, making it burn brighter.

Cassandra remained indifferent to Genevieve's pledge. She stated calmly, "Evelyn possesses a rare gift. You could work for ten more years and never attain her level."

Genevieve's face nearly fractured. She gritted her teeth, fighting for a response.

"Yet, precisely because Evelyn is so exceptionally talented, I must ensure she is completely removed from the picture," Cassandra murmured, a slow smile spreading across her lips.

The thought of Evelyn being permanently disgraced, perhaps even earning Alexander Blackwood's contempt because of the allegations, filled Cassandra with a deep, satisfying thrill.

As evening fell, the red carpet event commenced. Cassandra, one of the main attractions, made a dramatic entrance.

The recent controversy had guaranteed that all eyes were on her the moment she appeared.

The press immediately swiveled their cameras toward her, noting the new gown.

"Cassandra is wearing a different dress. It seems the accusations against that new designer might have merit," one journalist observed.

"Audacious. She's associated with Aethelgard Industries and still makes such a bold statement. Truly impressive," another commented.

After her walk, reporters surged forward, surrounding Cassandra with a flood of urgent questions.
###Chapter 233

I Believe She Won't Make That Error Again

"Cassandra, why the last-minute dress change? Does this confirm that Evelyn plagiarized Julian Thorne's work?"

"Cassandra, what initially made you decide to collaborate with Evelyn?"

"Cassandra, who is the designer of your current gown?"

Cassandra maintained a polite, distant smile. "I apologize, but these questions are unrelated to tonight's red carpet event."

She turned and walked away without another word.

The reporters surged forward, eager for more, but were immediately blocked by Genevieve, who had been shadowing Cassandra closely.

"Please, no further questions." Genevieve let out a carefully measured sigh. "Cassandra is understandably distressed by today's events. Thankfully, the red carpet proceeded without issue. She needs time and space to process everything."

A reporter eyed her skeptically. "And you are?"

Genevieve offered a confident smile. "Good evening. I'm Genevieve Prescott, a designer at Aethelgard Industries. The gown Cassandra wore earlier was my design."

A ripple of surprise went through the media crowd. A momentary silence was followed by a wave of hushed whispers.

"Aethelgard actually replaced the designer? That practically proves Evelyn's guilt."

"Even her own company isn't backing her. Her career is finished."

Genevieve released another theatrical sigh. "As Evelyn is also a designer at Aethelgard Industries, I sincerely hope everyone can find it in their hearts to give her another opportunity. I believe she won't make that error again. From now on, she will undoubtedly focus on creating original work."

Her words, framed as a plea for mercy, served as the final condemnation. The verdict was delivered.

The reporters exchanged knowing glances before pressing harder. "Ms. Prescott, are you confirming that Evelyn did, in fact, plagiarize?"

Genevieve feigned shock, bringing a hand to her mouth. "Oh! That's not what I meant at all. Let's not speculate. We should really be focusing on Cassandra's upcoming film projects."

She then turned and strode away, a smirk of satisfaction finally breaking through her composed facade.

Within minutes, the interview clips spread across the internet, fueling the scandal surrounding Evelyn's alleged plagiarism anew.

Fans of Cassandra and Julian Thorne flooded the official social media accounts of Aethelgard Industries, demanding action.

"How can you stay silent? Your own designer just admitted it! Evelyn harmed Cassandra! Evelyn needs to come forward and apologize immediately!"

"The audacity of Evelyn! Did she truly believe Julian's fans wouldn't recognize his work? We've followed his career for years! For a novice designer to pull this stunt is an insult to the entire industry!"

Despite the barrage of angry comments and questions, Aethelgard Industries remained silent.

Frustrated and seeking an outlet for their rage, the crowd turned their attention to Evelyn's personal accounts, ready to unleash their fury.

"It's been days. Still no admission of guilt. How can you be so shameless?"

"Your own colleague has confirmed the plagiarism. What possible excuse do you have left?"

"Your silence won't protect you forever. After this, no design house will ever hire you."

"Evelyn's photo and details are everywhere now. What company would be foolish enough to associate with her after this disaster?"
###Chapter 234

You're Home Early

"I never liked Cassandra, but this? It's disgraceful. Pure plagiarism!"

Soon, people realized they couldn't comment on Isabella's account anymore.

Some refreshed the page. Others logged out and back in. They all found the same thing. Her profile was gone.

"Wow. Isabella actually deleted her account."

"She couldn't take the pressure."

"Isabella, do you think deleting your account makes this disappear? Think again. Everyone, keep sharing! The world needs to know she's a thief!"

Meanwhile, Evelyn had already left the office. She was home, completely unaware of the online firestorm.

She trusted her supervisor and the company to manage the situation. She deliberately avoided reading the comments. Why stress if it was being handled?

In his study, Alexander was deep in thought. The sound of the front door opening startled him. It was too early.

He wheeled himself out to the foyer. "Evelyn? You're home early. Is everything alright at work?"

Concern tinged his voice.

He knew her. She was dedicated. Leaving early was unusual.

She paused, surprised he noticed so quickly.

But she didn't want to worry him. If the company was handling it, there was no need for drama.

She smiled, setting down her bag. "Yes, I finished my work early. They gave me the rest of the day off. That's all. You look shocked. Aren't you happy to see me?"

Alexander had been preoccupied with Theodore's affairs. He hadn't checked the news.

He took her words at face value. He was glad she could rest.

"You've been working too hard lately. It's good you have a break," he said softly. "Since you're free, let's go out. Get some fresh air."

Evelyn blinked, then smiled. "I'd like that."

The next morning, she was ready for their outing.

"Where are we going today?" she asked, lacing her fingers with his. "We've visited every park nearby. The mall, perhaps?"

Alexander smiled and squeezed her hand. "It's a surprise. Just trust me."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow, intrigued. She followed him across the grounds. Then she stopped dead.

A helicopter sat before them. Its sleek body gleamed in the sun. She blinked, her mind struggling to catch up.

"This..." Her voice faded. She had never been this close to a helicopter before.

She turned to Alexander, her eyes wide.

He had said they were going for a walk. A simple outing.

But he had arranged a helicopter.

Evelyn didn't know whether to laugh or shake her head. "Where are you taking me?"

Alexander only smiled, his expression playful.

"If I told you, it wouldn't be a surprise." He reached into his pocket. He pulled out a blindfold. "Now, be good. Crouch down a little so I can put this on."

Evelyn let out a laugh. She shook her head but complied. She crouched slightly. He gently tied the blindfold over her eyes.
###Chapter 235

Everything went black for Evelyn.

The darkness was absolute.

Every sound around her became intensely sharp and clear.

A wave of discomfort washed over her.
She instinctively moved closer to Alexander.
"Don't leave me alone here, please," she whispered, her voice soft and warm.

Blindfolded, her lips parted slightly as she spoke.
This subtle movement caught Alexander's attention.

Watching her, he felt a sudden tightness in his chest.
The urge to kiss her was powerful, almost overwhelming.

Yet, he held himself back.

It wasn't the right moment.

Guided by his voice, Evelyn carefully climbed into the helicopter.

Time lost all meaning as they flew.
With each passing minute, her curiosity grew.
She was desperate to know where they were going.

Finally, the helicopter began its descent.
Her excitement surged.

Once they landed, Alexander's calm voice cut through the silence.
"You can remove the blindfold now."

Evelyn quickly tore the fabric from her eyes.

Her vision adjusted to the bright light.
Astonishment gripped her.

An island.
They were on an island!

She had imagined a quiet countryside or a mountain retreat near Crestwood.
But an island was a complete surprise.

A gentle breeze played with the hem of her dress, lifting it lightly.

"Alexander! What island is this?" Evelyn scanned the stunning landscape. "I've never seen anything like this online."

He let out a soft laugh. "It's a private island. We can be completely alone here. No interruptions from the outside world."

Her wonder deepened. "A private island? How did you possibly get permission to come here?"

He almost told her the truth, that the island was his.
But he stopped himself.
Adopting a composed expression, he simply said, "I borrowed it."

A knowing smile spread across Evelyn's face. "Let me guess. Sebastian helped you, didn't he?"

After all, Sebastian was from the influential Hayes family.
It wasn't surprising he might own a private island.

Alexander remained silent.

As Evelyn continued to admire the island's beauty, her smile widened. "Sebastian can be arrogant, but he really does look out for you."

She noticed a subtle shift in Alexander's expression then.

This jealous man!

Amused, Evelyn stepped forward and pressed a gentle kiss to his cheek.

"But in my heart, it's only you. Only you would think to bring me somewhere like this. Thank you, Alexander."

A flicker of emotion shone in his eyes.
A faint, genuine smile touched his lips.

He then guided her towards the villa on the island.

Entering the villa, Evelyn couldn't hide her amazement. "Sebastian built a villa here too? The rich really do have more money than sense."

The island's true owner, walking quietly behind her, said nothing.

He wished he could tell her the truth about his wealth.
He longed to be honest with her.

He would give her anything she desired.

The villa was exquisitely appointed, with every amenity they could need.
It even had a swimming pool in the backyard.

Evelyn's face lit up with pure joy. "I haven't been swimming in ages."
###Chapter 236

236 This Swimsuit Is All Wrong

Alexander looked at her. "Why not go for a swim?"

Evelyn frowned, looking troubled. "I didn't pack a swimsuit."

She hadn't brought anything else either. This outing had been a complete surprise, planned entirely by him.

Hearing her concern, Alexander raised an eyebrow. "Don't worry about that. I'll have someone bring one."

"Really?" Evelyn asked, surprised.

True to his word, within half an hour, a bag containing a swimsuit was delivered.

Evelyn quickly opened it. But the moment she saw the contents, her cheeks burned crimson.

Her heart hammered loudly in her chest.

Acting on impulse, she shoved the swimsuit back into the bag, her expression one of pure shock.

Alexander, who hadn't seen it, was baffled by her reaction. "What's wrong? Changed your mind about swimming?"

He was about to suggest an alternative activity when Evelyn bit her lip, visibly torn. "Maybe there was a mistake? This isn't the kind of swimsuit I wear."

She searched for the right words. "This swimsuit is all wrong."

Puzzled, Alexander pressed for clarity. "What's the problem with it?"

Evelyn blushed even deeper. She hesitated before confessing, "It's a bikini. It's far too revealing. I'm not comfortable wearing something like that."

Understanding dawned on him, and a teasing glint appeared in his eyes. "We're completely alone here. What is there to be shy about?"

Evelyn shot him a playful glare, not expecting his jest. "Absolutely not! Why should I be the only one dressed like that? It's not fair!"

Intrigued, Alexander suggested, "Oh? Should I join you in the water, then?"

Evelyn glanced at the wheelchair beside him. "Can you even swim?"

He replied in a casual, offhand tone. "Not very well. I might need you to rescue me if I start swallowing water."

Evelyn was amused yet speechless. She frowned and crossed her arms. "You should definitely stay out of the water. You're too heavy for me to pull out. We'd both end up drowning."

Alexander nodded solemnly. "A shame. I suppose I'll have to content myself with cheering you on from the sidelines."

However, he immediately asked someone to fetch a different swimsuit for her, a more conservative one-piece.

Despite their many intimate moments, Evelyn remained surprisingly timid in certain situations.

Alexander knew better than to push her too far on something like this.

Once she had changed into the new swimsuit, Evelyn stepped into the pool.

At first, her movements were a little rusty; it had been a while since she'd last swum.

But within minutes, she was gliding smoothly through the water, swimming laps from one end of the pool to the other.

Alexander, unable to join her, watched contentedly from his spot nearby.

Seeing Evelyn move with such freedom and joy in the water brought a genuine, soft smile to his face. He shared in her happiness as if it were his own.

In that moment, everything felt perfectly peaceful.

It was just the two of them, enjoying their own private world, completely untouched by any outside interruptions.
###Chapter 237

Did I Startle You?

A brief flicker of joy warmed Alexander's heart.
It vanished instantly, replaced by a cold dread as he remembered.
Theodore Vance was returning to Crestwood.

His presence never brought anything good.
Only complications.
Only deliberate attempts to disrupt Alexander's carefully constructed peace.

This was why Alexander monitored Theodore's movements so closely.
Why he planned for every contingency.
His primary goal was always the same: to keep Evelyn safely out of the fray.

These dark thoughts clouded his mind.
His brow furrowed.
His expression grew stern and distant.

Evelyn noticed the change from her place in the pool.
He was lost in thought again.
Something was troubling him, something heavy.

She stopped swimming and pushed her wet hair back from her face.

Lately, Alexander was always at his desk.
He worked relentlessly.
When Evelyn returned from her design studio, she often found him still in the study.
Hours would pass without him seeming to notice.

If she didn't intervene, he would work straight through the night.
He would forget to sleep entirely.

A wave of concern washed over her.
She needed to pull him away, even for a moment.
Since he couldn't join her in the water...

A mischievous idea sparked in her mind.

She grinned, took a deep breath, and dove beneath the surface.

Alexander finally snapped out of his brooding.
He realized he hadn't seen Evelyn come up for air.
A knot of panic tightened in his chest.

"Evelyn!" he called out, wheeling himself closer to the pool's edge.

Silence.
He called her name again, louder this time.
His eyes scanned the still, undisturbed water.
A cold fear gripped him.

Had something happened?

Driven by sheer alarm, his hands gripped the arms of his wheelchair.
He tried to push himself up.

Splash!

Evelyn erupted from the water right beside him, laughing.

Alexander jolted back, his eyes wide with shock.

"Alexander! Did I startle you?" she teased, her face glowing with playful triumph.

Relief flooded through him so intensely it left him breathless. "You're alright."

"Of course I'm alright!" she said, blinking water from her lashes. "I'm an excellent swimmer. Nothing ever happens to me here."

She laughed again, a bright, clear sound. "I knew I could get a reaction from you! See? You're not as unshakable as you pretend to be."

Understanding dawned. She had staged this whole scene just to scare him.
His initial shock melted into amused exasperation.

He shook his head slowly. "You're incredibly childish."

His voice held a faint note of scolding, but his eyes were soft, full of undisguised affection.

Completely unrepentant, Evelyn splashed a wave of water directly at him. "So what if I am?"

The water hit his shirt and trousers, soaking them instantly.

Alexander looked down at his drenched clothes, then back at her.
His eyes narrowed, his voice dropping to a low, dangerous purr. "Evelyn, are you sure you want to provoke me? Aren't you afraid of the consequences?"

Confident in her advantage, she grinned victoriously. "You can't even get in the water. How exactly are you going to catch me?"

A slow, predatory smile spread across his face. "What if I told you I have my ways?"

"I wouldn't believe you," she challenged, her confidence unshaken. "If you can, then you can do whatever you want."

"Really? Anything I want?" His gaze intensified, locking with hers. A playful challenge danced in their depths.
###Chapter 238

A smug smile played on Evelyn's lips.

Alexander had only meant to give her a small scare. He reached out to pull her from the water.

But Evelyn swam backward with a swift motion, easily evading his grasp. A delighted laugh bubbled from her as she put more distance between them.

"Alexander, you can try again when you're fast enough to catch me!"

Confined to his wheelchair, Alexander watched her glide toward the deep end. Frustration was clear on his face.

If he weren't committed to this charade of being disabled, he would have already swept her into his arms.

Still, Alexander knew other ways to make her come to him.

A mischievous glint sparked in his eyes. A slow, cunning smirk spread across his features.

He lifted a hand to his temple. A soft, pained groan escaped his lips.

"Ah..." Alexander winced, exaggerating his expression of discomfort.

Evelyn saw his act from the pool. Her brow furrowed with immediate concern. She swam back quickly.

"Alexander, what is it?" she asked, her voice laced with worry. "A headache? Did you get a chill from the water earlier?"

Alexander kept his face partially turned away. His tone was deliberately distant. "It's nothing. Don't trouble yourself. I should just go to my room and rest."

Seeing his apparent pain, Evelyn's anxiety grew.

She hurriedly climbed out of the pool. Water dripped from her as she rushed to his side.

"Alexander, wait..." she began, wanting to check on him properly.

Before she could finish, his hand shot out and captured her wrist. "See? I caught you after all," he said, one eyebrow arched triumphantly.

Evelyn stood frozen for a second. The realization that she'd been tricked dawned on her.

This devious, cunning man! And she had been genuinely worried!

She had fallen right into his trap.

Irritated, she bit her lip and tried to pull her hand back.

Alexander gave her no chance to escape. With a firm tug, he pulled her onto his lap. His arms locked around her, holding her securely in place.

"Still thinking of running?" Alexander's whisper was warm against her ear. It sent a shiver across her skin.

Evelyn glared at him. "You're completely shameless!"

Alexander chuckled, utterly unbothered by her accusation. "You said if I caught you, I could have anything I want. Are you going back on your word now?"

Evelyn shot him an exasperated look. She immediately recognized the playful, predatory gleam in his eyes.

What a miscalculation. How had she not seen this sly side of him before?

Then again, Alexander would never truly hurt her... would he?

After a brief internal debate, Evelyn decided to play along, albeit reluctantly. "Fine, I'll honor the bet. What is it you want me to do?"

Alexander's expression softened. A faint, knowing smile touched his lips. "Just promise me one thing. I haven't decided what it is yet. So you'll owe me for now."

Evelyn agreed without a second thought. "Alright."

After all, whether she actually followed through later was her decision. If she refused, what could he really do about it?

Having agreed, she instinctively tried to stand up. To her surprise, Alexander's grip tightened. He held her even closer, making her position feel increasingly intimate.

Evelyn frowned. She shifted slightly in his lap to relieve the pressure.

Suddenly, her leg brushed against something hard...
###Chapter 239

239 This Is Just Petty, Alexander!

Alexander's expression hardened instantly.
A low, displeased sound rumbled in his chest.
His intense gaze locked onto Evelyn. "Was that on purpose, Evelyn?" he demanded.

Heat flooded her cheeks at the accidental contact.
She felt a wave of embarrassment.
She averted her eyes, unable to hold his stare.

Her voice was laced with defiance when she spoke. "Alexander, you're the one twisting things!"

Feeling more confident, she met his eyes directly.
She stated firmly, "You're the one who refused to let me go in the first place. That's why this happened—"

She trailed off, searching for the right phrase.
Frustrated, she bit her lower lip.
"Let me go right now!" she finally demanded.

She was completely drenched.
Her swimsuit was plastered to her skin.

By now, Alexander's clothes were equally soaked.

He let out a cold, short laugh. "You were faking distress in the pool. You made me think you were in trouble. We haven't settled that score, and now you want me to free you? Not happening."

Evelyn was stunned. "What are you talking about? I thought we resolved that! You said if I agreed to one request, we'd be even. You still haven't even told me what you want!"

"That's a separate issue," Alexander countered, his tone leaving no room for argument. "You agreed to the request as compensation for intentionally splashing me. The pool deception is a completely different matter."

Evelyn could only stare, shocked by his meticulous pettiness. "This is just petty, Alexander!" she exclaimed.

Completely unfazed, he raised a single eyebrow. "Only just realizing how petty I can be? You started this game. Now your luck has run out. It's a shame. Even if you regret it now, it's far too late. I'm not letting you go."

Evelyn tried to pull her wrist free again.
It was useless.

Despite his physical limitations, his grip was like iron.

Feeling exhausted and cornered, she finally asked, "Fine. If this is a separate issue, what is your retaliation? Are you going to hit me or something?"

"Just kiss me," Alexander replied simply.
His eyes dropped to her lips.
A faint, knowing smile played on his mouth.

Evelyn was taken aback by the straightforward request.

Her mind was instantly flooded with vivid, unspoken memories.
She and Alexander had already been intimate.

Could a simple kiss really be all he wanted now?

Despite the seeming simplicity, a sense of underlying danger lingered.

"Is that really all?" she questioned, her tone heavy with skepticism. "I'm worried you'll just keep escalating your demands. There will be no end to it."

Alexander gave a soft snort. "Fine. Then I suppose I'm not letting you go."

"You!" Evelyn stared at him, bewildered by his sheer audacity.

To make matters worse, she could feel that he was still aroused.

Her entire body tensed.
She froze completely, terrified the situation might spiral further out of control.

Taking a deep, steadying breath, Evelyn slowly raised her hands.
She gently cupped Alexander's face.
Leaning forward, she pressed her lips to his in a soft, tentative kiss.
She hesitated to deepen the contact.
###Chapter 240

You're Just Letting Me Go

Her lips touched Alexander's.

Evelyn closed her eyes nervously.

Her long eyelashes fluttered.

His warm breath hit her skin.

A sudden panic surged inside her.

She instinctively wanted to pull back.

But Alexander seemed to read her mind.

His hand moved quickly to the small of her back.

He held her firmly, preventing any escape.

Evelyn's eyes widened in surprise.

Before she could speak, he deepened the kiss.

A soft sound escaped her lips.

She held her breath without realizing it.

The intensity quickly left her breathless.

Alexander noticed her struggle.

He loosened his grip slightly.

His rough fingertips brushed a tear from her lip.

"Is this really your first sober kiss?" he murmured. "You still forget to breathe."

His teasing made her blush deeper.

She gave him a look of mock anger.

It held no real threat.

She didn't know how she looked.

Her flushed cheeks and bright, wet eyes captivated him.

Her lips were soft, pink, and inviting.

They stirred a deep desire in Alexander.

He swallowed hard.

His Adam's apple moved visibly.

His gaze darkened with want.

Evelyn's heart pounded wildly.

Her breathing quickened.

She shyly looked away.

She expected him to kiss her again.

Instead, he pressed his lips together.

He moved his wheelchair back slightly.

"You should change out of those wet clothes," he said calmly. "You'll get sick."

Evelyn stared at him, stunned.

Her eyes were wide with disbelief.

He looked completely composed.

Had she imagined the passion of moments ago?

"What did you say?" she stammered. "You're just letting me go?"

Shock made her almost bite her tongue.

She searched his face for any hint of emotion.

Had their kiss meant nothing to him?

Alexander chuckled softly at her reaction.

"What else should I do?" he asked.

He watched her with curious amusement.

"If you don't want this, I won't force you," he added.

What?

When had she ever said that?

Evelyn pointed at herself, confused.

"I don't want to?"

She was just being shy!

That was all!

Frustration bubbled inside her.

She mentally called him clueless and dense.

Then a realization hit her.

He was trying to be a gentleman.

He was respecting her feelings.

Feeling a little braver, she challenged him.

"Who said I didn't want to?"

But her courage faltered as her eyes met his.

Alexander raised an eyebrow.

He gently pinched her chin.

"Is that so?" he teased.

He paused for a moment.

Then he sighed.

"I don't want to pressure you," he said. "Last time... you were drunk. I shouldn't have taken advantage."

He helped her stand up.

He began to turn his wheelchair to leave.

Panic seized Evelyn.

She grabbed his arm.

"That's not true!" she insisted. "Even if I was sober, I would have wanted it!"

Alexander turned back.

He shook his head.

"You don't have to say that to make me feel better."

But she was telling the truth!

Frustrated, words failed her.

She acted on pure impulse.

She threw her arms around his neck.

She crushed her lips against his in a desperate kiss.

Without thinking, her hands went to the buttons of his wet shirt.

She started to undress him.
###Chapter 241

Alexander, Just Be Quiet

Evelyn tore at Alexander's tie, leaving it hanging loosely around his neck.

Alexander, seated in his wheelchair, went completely still. His eyes locked onto Evelyn, wide with shock at her sudden action.

Seeing his stunned expression, Evelyn paused, her lips hovering just a breath away from his. "What's wrong?" she whispered softly. "Are you distracted now?"

His voice was a low, gentle rumble. "I just don't want to make you uncomfortable," he said, even as his arms tightened around her waist, pulling her flush against him.

A faint frown touched Evelyn's brow. She wasn't interested in talking. This wasn't about conversation.

"Alexander, just be quiet," she instructed, pressing a finger to his lips to stop any further words. "From here, you just need to follow my lead."

She was determined to prove that their first time wasn't just a drunken accident. Her feelings for him were real. She wanted to close the distance between them, to truly connect.

Alexander let out a shaky breath, a soft "okay" escaping him.

Later, Evelyn was completely spent, collapsing into a deep sleep in Alexander's arms as exhaustion finally claimed her.

He held her close, his own breathing ragged as he fought for control.

Once he was sure she was asleep, Alexander carefully rose from his wheelchair. He scooped her up gently and carried her to the bed, tucking the blankets around her with infinite care.

He sat on the edge of the bed, his fingers softly brushing the damp strands of hair from Evelyn's forehead.

His mind raced, replaying the events of the evening.

Evelyn's boldness hadn't satisfied the hunger inside him. It had only made it sharper, more demanding.

He had to keep up the act of being disabled in front of her. That meant letting her take the lead while he forced himself to hold back.

A frustrated sigh escaped him. The only solution right now was a cold shower.

In all his twenty-eight years, Alexander had never felt so thoroughly tormented.

He thought that if Sebastian ever found out about this, he would never hear the end of it.

After his cold shower, a measure of calm finally returned to Alexander.

He slipped quietly into bed beside Evelyn.

Just then, she murmured in her sleep, shifting slightly. "Mmm... cold."

Alexander hesitated for only a second before gathering her into his arms, pulling the duvet tightly around them both.

Instinctively, Evelyn curled into the warmth of his broad chest, her breathing evening out once more into peaceful slumber.

Alexander watched her sleeping face, the desire to stand tall before her, to reveal his true self, burning stronger than ever.

He ached to hold her openly, to give her the complete security she deserved.

But danger still circled them.

To protect Evelyn, he couldn't afford to be reckless.

His arms tightened around her. He silently vowed to remove every obstacle, and quickly.

No matter what, he had to find that woman before Theodore Vance did.

Elsewhere, a tall, imposing man stood on the balcony of a luxurious villa, his gaze fixed on the distant city lights, a storm of emotion in his eyes.

He slowly looked down at the butterfly pendant resting in his palm, his warm fingertips tracing its delicate contours with a strange tenderness.

This man was Theodore Vance, the very source of Alexander's high alert.
###Chapter 242

Theodore Vance held the delicate butterfly pendant.

His stern features softened into an uncharacteristic tenderness.

A flood of memories washed over him.

It was a scene he had replayed in his mind a thousand times.

He had been hunted, cornered by enemies.

He barely escaped with his life.

Injured and bleeding, he stumbled into a remote fishing village.

His consciousness faded with each passing moment.

He remembered collapsing on the desolate beach.

The cold sea water lapped at his wounds, washing the blood away.

He was ready to give up.

Then, a blurry figure appeared through his dimming sight.

She saved him.

She tended his injuries with gentle hands.

She whispered for him to hold on, to fight.

She was his lifeline, his second chance.

His loyal men found him soon after and smuggled him out.

When he returned to the village, she was gone.

The only clue was that she was a foreigner.

The only proof was this butterfly pendant.

For years, he searched the world for her.

He found nothing.

Until now.

A slow smile spread across his face.

The woman was from Crestwood.

He pulled a worn photograph from his pocket.

It showed a woman with a kind smile and graceful demeanor.

"Evelyn," Theodore whispered, his voice low. "I've found you at last. I will not lose you again."

Meanwhile, Evelyn was trapped in a dream.

It was a memory from long ago.

She was traveling, seeking inspiration in a small fishing village abroad.

One morning, she walked along the beach.

She saw a man lying on the sand.

His body was covered in blood.

She didn't think. She acted.

She dragged him to safety.

She checked his pulse. It was faint.

He was dying.

She called for an ambulance, but the village was too remote.

Help would take too long.

The local villagers warned her to stay away.

"He's lost too much blood," they said. "He won't survive. Don't waste your time."

"We see death here often. It's normal."

But Evelyn refused to let a man die in front of her.

She took him to her rented cottage.

She found what medicine she could to stop the bleeding.

She managed to stabilize him, but then a fever set in.

With no doctor, she cared for him herself.

She stayed by his side for days.

She talked to him, urging him to live.

She felt he could hear her.

Miraculously, he held on. His fever broke.

Days later, she heard a doctor was nearby.

She brought gifts and begged for his help.

She pleaded all day.

Finally, the doctor agreed.

But when they returned to the cottage, the man was gone.

The neighbors said people came for him.

She tried to find out more, but she hit a dead end.

She didn't know his name. She had to let go.
###Chapter 243

I'm Utterly Spent

The morning light felt harsh against Evelyn's eyelids.

A strange dream lingered in her mind, a fragment from a past she rarely visited.

Why had it chosen to resurface now?

Her fingers pressed against her temple, a dull throb answering her touch.

The fatigue from the previous night was a heavy blanket.

A desperate thirst clawed at her throat.

She tried to sit up, but a solid weight held her down.

Alexander's arm was draped possessively over her waist.

Her slight movement roused him.

A low, sleep-roughened voice murmured near her ear. "Awake already? I thought you'd sleep longer." His tone was laced with amusement. "After last night's... exertions."

Heat flooded Evelyn's cheeks at the memory.

She couldn't believe her own boldness.

Every muscle in her body screamed in protest. She felt hollowed out. But she refused to show weakness. "I'm fine," she insisted, gritting her teeth. "Let me up."

Alexander's eyebrow arched. A smirk played on his lips.

His gaze swept over the faint marks on her neck, his eyes darkening momentarily before he loosened his hold.

Evelyn pushed herself up, but her legs buckled instantly.

She gasped, falling back onto the mattress.

In a flash, Alexander's arms were around her again, pulling her securely against his chest.

"Alexander, you're crushing me! Release me!" she demanded, frustration etching lines on her forehead.

She struggled briefly, then froze.

Her eyes widened as she looked up at his face. "Alexander! You... you can't be serious."

He let out a slow breath, his thumb tracing the line of her jaw.

A flicker of something—awkwardness?—passed over his features, replaced by a soft chuckle. "Is there a problem?"

"Is there a problem?" she echoed, frowning deeply. "After last night... how are you still...? Aren't you ever satisfied?"

Instead of denial, he shifted, his body pressing more firmly against hers. His gaze was intense, unwavering. "Evelyn, I'm a healthy man. This is perfectly normal." He stated it as simple fact. "Besides, since our marriage, opportunities have been scarce. Did you truly think one night would be sufficient?"

His blunt logic left her momentarily speechless. She swallowed. "Alexander, please. I'm utterly spent."

The image of his wheelchair from yesterday flashed in her mind, and a pang of guilt silenced further protest.

Taking the initiative even once had required all her courage and energy.

Alexander's laughter was a low rumble. His fingers trailed from her ear down her neck.

"Relax. I promise, this time I'll do all the work. You won't have to lift a finger."

Evelyn's eyes shot open wide. "Why didn't you lead with that crucial piece of information last night?"

All that exhaustion, just because of him! This impossible, infuriating man.
###Chapter 244

It's Acceptable To Rely On Me Occasionally

Witnessing Evelyn's visible fury, Alexander halted, an awkward gesture as he rubbed the bridge of his nose. "You appeared thoroughly engrossed last night," he stated. "I refrained from commenting to preserve the atmosphere."

Evelyn shot him a scorching glare, her irritation hitting its zenith. "Alexander Blackwood, you are utterly insufferable! How can you behave like this?"

She was unaware that her rage had minimal impact on him.

Alexander countered with a faint, teasing smile and a lifted brow.

Provoked and humiliated, Evelyn's hand flew up, delivering a sharp slap against his cheek.

Recognizing her genuine distress, Alexander's demeanor softened. After a brief pause for thought, he spoke in a gentle tone. "Very well, I concede my error. I am here to rectify the situation. Simply inform me of what you require."

Her anger surged anew, and she sank her teeth firmly into his shoulder.

It was barely visible, especially when contrasted with the more distinct marks from the previous night.

Alexander's eyes darkened, his voice dropping to a husky murmur. "The shoulder is manageable. However, there is another location causing significant discomfort."

Evelyn stiffened, comprehension dawning. A deep blush spread across her cheeks as she averted her gaze, biting her lip. Despite her anger, a part of her was impressed. "Alexander, how are you so... resilient? It defies your condition."

It was fortunate she was on leave from work.

In her present condition, surviving a full workday seemed an impossibility.

By the time they settled to eat, the clock showed it was well past noon, a direct consequence of their prolonged morning diversion.

During the meal, Alexander noticed Evelyn's pronounced fatigue. "Allow me to assist you," he offered gently.

Evelyn shook her head. "No, it's fine. I'm not completely helpless."

She reached for her knife and fork.
