With chilling calm, Cassandra glanced at the lead bodyguard and gave a slight nod. Her voice dropped to a venomous whisper. "A snake like Julian doesn't deserve to live. The only way to ensure silence... is permanent silence."

Horror washed over Julian's face. His whole body shook as the reality hit him.

Was Cassandra really going to kill him? Just like that?

How could she?

What was she thinking?

Killing someone as if it were nothing—she was insane!

"Cassandra! You can't! If I die, it won't stay hidden! You'll be caught!" he shouted, fear evident in every syllable.

Seeing her unwavering resolve, Julian finally broke down sobbing. "Cassandra, I was wrong! Forget the money! Just get me out of the country! I'll do anything!" his voice cracked in a desperate plea.

Cassandra's expression didn't flicker. "It's far too late for regrets. But before I decide, I need to be sure you'll never talk."

Her tone was ice. A sinister glint sparkled in her eyes.

Out of the corner of her eye, Genevieve, who had been silently watching, jolted. She never imagined Cassandra would go this far.

"Cassandra... you can't mean... kill him? We don't have to! Just exile him!" Genevieve's voice trembled uncontrollably with fear.

She had allied with Cassandra to outmaneuver Evelyn and gain positive press. This had spiraled into something dark and terrifying.

"Silence!" Cassandra's shout cut through the air, her glare instantly silencing Genevieve.

Genevieve recoiled, her pulse racing as a cold, heavy dread settled over her.

###Chapter 270


Witnessing this side of Cassandra terrified Genevieve.

"Proceed!" Cassandra snapped, her voice sharp with impatience as the lead bodyguard hesitated.

Without delay, the lead bodyguard drew a knife, advancing toward Julian with deliberate steps.

Panic surged through Julian. He shook his head frantically, struggling against the crushing grip holding him down. There was no escape.

Terror choked him. His eyes remained fixed on the blade, every glint of metal a silent threat.

Meanwhile, Evelyn and Alexander watched the scene unfold on the monitor.

Evelyn's breath hitched. Disbelief clouded her features as she instinctively grabbed Alexander's hand. "She's not actually going to kill him... is she?"

Alexander's brow furrowed, equally surprised by Cassandra's cold resolve.

"Relax, she won't succeed," he murmured, squeezing her hand reassuringly. Irritation tightened his jaw—Cassandra's cruelty had shaken Evelyn, and he didn't like it.

Evelyn looked up, confused. Before she could ask, the sharp wail of police sirens pierced through the audio feed. "The police are here!" a shaky voice shouted.

The lead bodyguard jolted. Panic flashed across his face as the knife clattered to the floor.

Cassandra's fury twisted into something dangerous. Her eyes narrowed to icy slits as she glared at Julian. "So, you had the nerve to call the police, Julian? You never intended to cooperate," she sneered, her voice dripping with contempt.

Julian's heart hammered. Relief flooded him at the sirens, though confusion nagged at him.

How could he have called them?

It didn't matter. The police were here.

Seizing the chance, he broke free and sprinted for the door, desperation fueling his steps. "Help! They're trying to kill me!" he screamed, his voice raw.

Cassandra gasped. Rage surged. She barked an order, desperate to catch him before he escaped.

Julian bolted like lightning, vanishing before anyone could react.

Though he had escaped, Cassandra knew the real threat was the recording.

It had to be destroyed.

She lunged to smash the device in her hand.

But she was too late. A wall of police officers surrounded her.

"Cassandra Sterling, you need to come with us," an officer stated firmly. "We have matters to discuss."

Before she or Genevieve could protest, the officers seized them, dragging them away.

The device fell from Cassandra's grasp as it was confiscated. The laptop screen abruptly went black.

Evelyn froze, shock jolting through her.

She stared at the blank screen, mind reeling. Slowly, she turned to Alexander, realization dawning in her eyes.

"So, it was you?" she asked, her voice low with awe and disbelief.

No wonder he had been so confident—he had orchestrated everything from the start.

"Wasn't it obvious?" Alexander replied smoothly, his voice calm. His gaze softened as he looked at her, a gentle smile touching his lips. "I did some research on Julian Thorne. His history—like leaving someone in a vegetative state—doesn't exactly scream 'trustworthy'."

His words were measured. His arms remained around her waist, protective and possessive.

"I played along, letting him think I needed his help to expose the conspirators. I knew once he realized Cassandra and Genevieve planned to pin everything on him, he wouldn't take it lying down. He'd retaliate—and try to extort them."

Alexander's lips curled into a smirk, his eyes glinting with mockery.

"And knowing those two, they don't respond well to blackmail," he said, his voice low with quiet amusement. "A confrontation was inevitable."

###Chapter 271

Evelyn connected the dots swiftly. "So you had Julian use recording equipment linked to us. It wasn't just for clear evidence, but also to switch the feed to the police at the right moment. They could intervene immediately and catch them in the act."

Alexander chuckled and affectionately ruffled her hair. "You're brilliant, my love."

Evelyn blushed at the compliment but brushed it aside. "You're exaggerating."

He planted a kiss on her cheek. "The arrests are made. We have compelling evidence. It won't be long before your name is publicly cleared."

As Alexander predicted, events unfolded seamlessly.

News of Cassandra's arrest spread like wildfire online.

A frenzy erupted.

Cassandra's loyal fans stormed social media, outraged. "Why arrest Cassandra? Police brutality!" "This is an outrage! I'm reporting this illegal arrest!" "The Crestwood PD needs to explain!" "How could they arrest someone so kind?" "I can't imagine her fear. What did she even do?" "She has no powerful backing, yet she's always targeted."

The overwhelming public outcry forced the police, who had planned a quiet investigation, to disclose case details.

The revelations sent shockwaves through the online community.

"Wait, Cassandra orchestrated the plagiarism scandal? She put Julian up to it?"

"It wasn't just Julian—Cassandra even bribed Genevieve Prescott, the designer at Aethelgard Industries, to frame Evelyn."

"This is like a thriller plot twist! The innocent one was the real schemer."

"Cassandra went so far as to bribe Nathanial, Evelyn's boss, to destroy evidence."

"Evelyn uncovered the plot by spotting her own signature, thwarting their entire plan. Cornered, Cassandra tried to silence Julian."

"Does that mean she contemplated murder? Did she really go that far?"

"Thank God the police intervened. A reputation can be fixed, a life cannot."

"How could Cassandra discuss murder so casually? Has she done this before? It's terrifying."

"This ordeal lasted so long, and Cassandra was behind it all? She's pure evil!"

"Those brainwashed fans who attacked Evelyn owe her an apology! You defended a criminal."

Cassandra's career ground to a halt.

Production companies and brands associated with her scrambled to sever ties, terminating contracts and issuing statements to distance themselves, while also pursuing damages.

The scandal's magnitude brought unprecedented scrutiny to law enforcement.

Genevieve and Nathanial, as accomplices, were fired from Aethelgard Industries. The company's official account issued a public apology.

"We sincerely apologize to our designer, Evelyn Blackwood. Protecting her was our duty, and we failed. We commit to enhanced oversight to prevent recurrence..."

The most astonishing part was the signature at the bottom—it was from the legendary, reclusive founder of Aethelgard Industries himself.
###Chapter 272

The entire office was in a state of shock.

The name signed at the bottom of the apology letter sent waves of disbelief through everyone.

No one had anticipated that Evelyn's situation would draw the personal attention of the reclusive founder of Aethelgard Industries.

"Is this for real? Did someone at Aethelgard mess up? The founder actually intervened personally?"

"How serious is Evelyn's problem, really?"

"You must be new here. Evelyn first gained public recognition as the designer behind actress Cassandra Sterling's iconic gown. Just as her career was taking off, a senior designer named Julian Thorne accused her of plagiarism. She was silenced, unable to defend herself, and endured weeks of brutal online attacks. When she finally gathered evidence to clear her name, it was revealed that Cassandra and several Aethelgard employees had orchestrated the entire scheme."

"At the very least, Aethelgard owes Evelyn significant compensation for this mess."

"It seems the founder is just trying to protect the company's reputation."

"But the founder of Aethelgard has always avoided the public eye. No matter the scandal, they've never stepped in personally before. Now, they're issuing a personal apology to a newly hired designer?"

"None of this adds up."

Evelyn herself was utterly stunned.

The online commenters had a point—she was just a designer. Why would the founder go to such lengths to apologize to her personally?

She voiced her confusion to Alexander.

Alexander, ever her steadfast supporter, dismissed her concerns. "So what? My wife is the most remarkable woman in the world. It's high time Aethelgard acknowledged their error and apologized."

His words made Evelyn laugh softly. "You're exaggerating. I'm not all that special."

But Alexander's expression remained intensely serious. "No, I mean it. You deserve nothing less than the utmost respect."

His gaze was tender, yet a flicker of regret passed through his eyes.

If he had known the full extent of the troubles Evelyn faced, he would have intervened much sooner.

He was also filled with gratitude—grateful that she had chosen to join his company. He vowed to himself that from now on, he would use all his power to shield her from harm.

Once the commotion died down, Evelyn finally returned to her workplace.

Entering the office, she ran into Nathanial Reed.

He was carrying a box filled with his personal items, his face a mask of dejection. He was clearly leaving the company.

The moment he saw Evelyn, his expression darkened.

He mistakenly assumed she had come to revel in his misfortune.

As this thought took hold, his anger flared. He shot her a bitter, mocking sneer. "Evelyn, you really had us all fooled. First, you cozy up to Beatrice Sterling, and now it turns out you've had a direct line to the founder all along. I was completely kept in the dark. Well, I concede. I suppose I was just unlucky to have crossed paths with you."

Nathanial had once believed he could use Evelyn—who seemed to lack powerful connections—as a stepping stone for his own career advancement.

He had gravely miscalculated.

Now, no amount of regret could change the consequences of his actions.

Nathanial's warped sense of victimhood left Evelyn completely speechless.
###Chapter 273

"I'm only a designer at Aethelgard Industries. The founder intervened because he witnessed an injustice. It's not the scandal you're implying," Evelyn stated.

Nathanial narrowed his gaze. "Evelyn, stop pretending. It's pointless. If you weren't already acquainted with the founder, why would he issue a personal apology to you?"

He released a sharp, frustrated breath. "For years, massive deals and public scandals never prompted his involvement. Yet you, a mere designer, managed it? What gives you that kind of significance?"

Evelyn opened her mouth to retort, but after a moment's hesitation, she closed it. Arguing was futile.

Seeing her refusal to engage, Nathanial let out a dry, humorless laugh. "You likely haven't heard yet, but the official notice just came down. I've been terminated. My role as head of the Fashion Design Department is now vacant. And the new appointment? It's you."

He scoffed derisively and turned on his heel, striding away without a backward glance.

Evelyn stood rooted to the spot, her eyes wide with shock.

What? She was replacing Nathanial?

The idea was so ludicrous she almost believed she'd misheard him.

Before she could fully process the information, her colleagues swarmed her, offering excited congratulations.

"Evelyn, congratulations!"

"We knew you'd be back, Evelyn. Someone with your talent was never going to stay away for long."

"You're definitely treating us to dinner tonight to celebrate!"

Their enthusiastic chatter only amplified her confusion.

She frowned. "What are you all talking about? I don't understand."

A colleague looked at her in surprise. "You haven't seen the company-wide email?"

Evelyn quickly pulled out her phone and accessed her inbox, realizing she must have missed a critical notification. At the very top was an official announcement.

Her breath caught. The promotion was real.

She was officially the new Head of the Fashion Design Department.

The confirmation struck her with full force, leaving her momentarily speechless.

Hadn't she joined Aethelgard Industries relatively recently?

She was still finding her footing. How could she possibly be leading the entire department?

Her mind whirled, struggling to reconcile the situation.

It felt surreal. She refreshed the email repeatedly, half-expecting it to be an error.

Perhaps it was a minor promotion.

But jumping from a junior designer directly to department head? The leap was astronomical.

Yet, no matter how many times she refreshed, the message remained unchanged.

"Look at her, she's too stunned to speak!"

"Evelyn, seriously, congratulations! You've endured so much; you truly deserve this."

Even with their assurances, it still didn't feel real.

"This is all happening too quickly," she murmured, almost to herself.

Her colleagues laughed warmly. "Evelyn, you're incredibly talented. If anyone is worthy of this position, it's you."

"Exactly, just embrace it. You have what it takes."

Evelyn allowed herself to relax slightly, a tentative smile touching her lips as she nodded. "I will try."

She made her way back to her desk and sank into her chair.

As department head, she would need to move her belongings into the manager's office.

The thought should have been exhilarating, but instead, a nagging question surfaced.

The President of Aethelgard Industries was Sebastian Hayes. And Sebastian was a close friend of Alexander Blackwood.

Was this promotion genuinely based on her merit? Or was it a favor granted by Sebastian, orchestrated for Alexander's sake?
###Chapter 274

Evelyn hesitated for a moment before typing a message to Sebastian. "Mr. Hayes?"

His call came through almost immediately.

She stepped out into the hallway for more privacy.

Sebastian's voice held a trace of genuine surprise. "Promotion? What promotion?"

Evelyn felt a flicker of uncertainty. He seemed completely unaware.

She quickly explained her new appointment as head of the design department.

Sebastian let out a soft chuckle. "Evelyn, do you really think I handle such minute details personally?" After a brief silence, she replied, "I suppose not."

He continued, his tone relaxed. "Why doubt yourself? Your talent is obvious. I may not manage the day-to-day, but I'm aware rival firms have been trying to poach you. If we hadn't acted to secure you with a promotion and a raise, we might have lost a valuable asset."

He paused. "HR undoubtedly recognized the same threat. Let's be honest, Evelyn, you've become quite the online celebrity lately."

Evelyn nodded slowly, the explanation making sense. "I see. Thank you for clarifying, Mr. Hayes. My apologies for disturbing you."

"Think nothing of it," Sebastian said warmly. "And please, call me Sebastian. You are Alexander's wife, after all. If you ever need anything, just ask."

"Alright," Evelyn agreed.

"Oh, by the way," Sebastian added casually, "did you know Alexander's birthday is coming up soon?"

Evelyn's interest was instantly captured. "It is?"

She realized she had no idea when his birthday was. Thank goodness Sebastian mentioned it.

Eagerly, she asked, "Do you have any idea what he might like? I want to get him something meaningful."

Sebastian's knowing laugh echoed down the line. "Alexander is notoriously difficult to buy for. How would I know his preferences? But I can say with certainty that he would cherish any gift from you."

After hanging up, Evelyn didn't waste time. She immediately began searching online for the perfect present.

She browsed for what felt like hours, but nothing seemed special enough.

Then, an idea sparked.

Why not bake him a cake herself?

A homemade gift would carry so much more heart.

Her decision was made. She would bake Alexander a rich chocolate cake for his birthday. She felt sure he would love it.

In the days that followed, she settled into her new office and adapted seamlessly to her leadership role.

During her free moments, she secretly watched baking tutorial videos.

The next morning, Evelyn arrived at work early. With no urgent tasks demanding her attention, she resumed the tutorial she hadn't finished the night before.

She had to be discreet. The cake was meant to be a surprise for Alexander. He couldn't find out.

Alexander seemed to be constantly by her side after work lately.

That was why she'd struggled to find time for the tutorials yesterday.

With his birthday approaching fast, she needed to master baking quickly.
###Chapter 275

Evelyn was completely engrossed in a video when her phone's sharp ringtone shattered the silence, making her jump.

She collected herself and answered the call.

A voice came through the receiver, smooth and calming. It was entirely different from Alexander's usual deep, commanding tone. This voice was softer, almost gentle.

"Is this Evelyn?" the man asked. The simple question inexplicably stirred a strange warmth within her.

Who was this? The voice was unfamiliar, yet it felt oddly comforting.

"Yes, speaking. Who is this?" Evelyn replied, her tone laced with subtle confusion, her brow furrowing.

On the other end, Theodore felt a sudden, powerful surge of familiarity. An inexplicable heat bloomed in his chest.

He gave a soft, warm chuckle. "It's been a long time. Have you truly forgotten me?"

He understood her confusion. During the brief, critical moments when Evelyn had saved him, he had been barely conscious, his awareness a blur.

It was logical that she wouldn't recognize his voice now.

Still, he couldn't resist a playful tease, eager to close the distance time had created.

Evelyn's frown deepened, her eyes narrowing with suspicion.

"Who are you, exactly?" she demanded, her voice now edged with skepticism.

She was now absolutely certain she did not know the caller.

Theodore maintained an even, patient tone. "You really want to know?" he lightly teased. "You'll find out when we meet. Tonight at seven, I'll be waiting at The Grand Crestwood. We can talk then..."

He was cut off by the abrupt, harsh beep of the call disconnecting. His words hung unfinished in the air.

Theodore stared at his phone, his expression a mix of shock and offense.

Did she just hang up on him? Unbelievable. That had never, ever happened to him in his entire life.

Meanwhile, Evelyn tossed her phone aside and scoffed, turning back to her video app with a dismissive snort.

"Where do these scammers get the nerve?" she muttered, her voice icy. "They're getting more audacious every day, even targeting someone like me."

The Grand Crestwood was renowned as the epitome of luxury in Crestwood, accessible only to the highest echelons of society.

Clearly, this scammer hadn't done his research. Evelyn paused as a realization dawned on her.

Was this that infamous scam? The one where con artists posed as wealthy admirers to lure victims into financial ruin?

They played the perfect part—rich, devoted, trustworthy—until they had a woman emotionally invested and financially drained.

The moment the victim believed the love was real and handed over her money, the scammer would vanish without a trace, leaving her devastated and broke.

That same afternoon, Evelyn's phone rang again.

It was the same number.

Her eyes narrowed, a wave of anger washing over her.

These lowlifes had probably swindled countless people with their lies. It was absolutely infuriating!

Without a second thought, Evelyn answered the call, her voice dripping with icy rage. "Stop calling me, you lying piece of trash! Go find some other fool to scam! You slimy bastard, you actually thought I'd fall for this? Not a chance! Crawl back into whatever hole you came from before I report you!"

After unleashing her fury, she hung up and immediately blocked the number.

On the other end, Theodore stared at his now-dark phone screen, enveloped in a heavy silence.

His subordinates, who had witnessed the entire exchange, were also stunned silent, not daring to utter a word.

After an extensive search, they had finally located the woman who had once saved Theodore's life.

They had anticipated a touching reunion. Instead, they saw their boss being viciously berated.

Watching Theodore's prolonged, tense silence, his team grew increasingly uneasy. They feared his frustration might be redirected toward them, given the disastrous outcome.
###Chapter 276

The silence stretched on for so long his subordinates were sure Theodore had decided not to speak.

Then, a sudden burst of laughter shattered the quiet.

His team exchanged startled glances. Had they heard correctly?

They turned to look at Theodore, who was lounging on the sofa. To their shock, he was wearing a broad, genuine smile.

What was going on? Had their boss finally lost it?

He had just been thoroughly chastised by the very woman who saved his life. Yet here he was, laughing.

Were they about to become the targets of his misplaced anger?

Contrary to their fears, Theodore was in a genuinely good mood.

After years of searching across continents, he had finally found her. The woman who had pulled him from the wreckage all those years ago.

Now that he knew her identity and her location, an unprecedented patience settled over him. He could wait.

Still, Theodore hadn't anticipated Evelyn would have such a fiery spirit.

But when it came to Evelyn, he found it remarkably easy to make excuses for her. She had a profound effect on his emotions.

The only thing that truly nagged at him was her immediate rejection. The way she had dismissed his invitation so outright.

Was it because he hadn't revealed who he was immediately?

He had thought it would be a pleasant surprise.

The more he dwelled on it, the more it irritated him. He turned to his team. "Why do you suppose Evelyn blocked my number?"

He felt he had been nothing but gentle and patient during their call.

His subordinates froze, caught off guard by the question.

The answer seemed obvious. An unknown number calls, makes a vague invitation... who wouldn't block it?

Though the thought was unanimous, voicing it was a different matter. Honesty could be dangerous.

One subordinate chose his words carefully. "Perhaps she's simply overwhelmed, sir. We uncovered that major plagiarism scandal linked to her recently. That must be causing immense pressure. She likely has no mental space for anything else. Don't overthink it, Boss. Evelyn isn't upset with you. She's just preoccupied with her own troubles."

Theodore listened, then gave a slow, thoughtful nod.

He found the explanation somewhat satisfactory.

"You have a valid point." His forehead creased in thought. "What is the status of those individuals causing trouble?"

The damaging articles about Evelyn had been weighing heavily on his mind.

How dare they slander her character so viciously?

If his attention hadn't been entirely consumed by winning her back, he would have already ensured those people faced severe repercussions for targeting Evelyn.

One subordinate promptly answered, "Evelyn's former manager and her coworker were both terminated by Aethelgard Industries. Regarding Cassandra Sterling, she is currently detained by the police, pending a full investigation."

At the mention of that name, Theodore's eyes flashed with pure disgust. "Cassandra Sterling..."

Another subordinate, assuming Theodore needed a reminder, began to explain, "She was the one who attempted to drug you at the gala..."

Theodore cut him off with a sharp glare. "That's enough."

After that incident, Theodore had promptly arranged for Cassandra's return to Crestwood.

The mere memory of her pathetic attempt to seduce him was enough to turn his stomach.

A sudden realization struck Theodore, shifting his expression.

"Incidentally, wasn't Cassandra rather close to the founder of Aethelgard Industries? Now that she's embroiled in this scandal, won't that person intervene to assist her?"

###Chapter 277

Theodore's subordinate shook his head, confused. "It seems unlikely. Cassandra probably has no real connection to the founder. The man is a ghost; almost no one knows his identity. How could someone of her caliber have any meaningful contact with him?"

Theodore gave a slight nod.

His initial pressure on Cassandra had been a test, a probe to see if the mysterious founder would reveal himself by acting on her behalf.

Now Cassandra had nearly destroyed Evelyn's reputation.

Theodore took a sharp breath and issued a cold command. "Everyone who has wronged Evelyn will pay the price."

The subordinate lowered his eyes and asked, "Sir, what are your orders?"

Theodore shot him an icy look, his voice frigid. "Must I explain everything? Ruin Cassandra's face. Ensure she can never return to her life as a celebrity. As for the others... break their legs. Make certain they never forget this lesson."

"Understood, sir," the subordinate confirmed.

With his commands given, Theodore now faced his primary challenge: how to approach Evelyn again.

"She has blocked my number. How am I supposed to get near her now?"

Theodore scowled in contemplation, then an idea sparked in his eyes. "What if I simply have someone bring her to me?"

His subordinates were startled by their boss's audacious suggestion.

That plan was clearly reckless and dangerous.

The subordinate who had spoken earlier responded quickly. "That may be unwise. If you force a confrontation, Evelyn will only resent you more deeply."

Theodore's frown intensified. "Then I..."

Seizing the moment of hesitation, the subordinate proposed an alternative. "Evelyn has just been promoted. Why not present her with a substantial business order? She would certainly appreciate the gesture. It could also serve as a pretext to exchange contact information under the guise of discussing business details."

Theodore listened and grinned in approval. "That is a solid strategy. Make it happen."

Without delay, Theodore set the new plan into motion.

Evelyn was at her desk, diligently working through the backlog Nathanial had left, when her phone rang.

"Hello, Ms. Rossi. I'm calling on behalf of Mr. Theodore Vance. He is a prominent figure in Crestwood's elite circles. We are interested in placing an order valued at five million dollars. Would that be of interest to you?"

Five million?

Evelyn was stunned. She composed herself and replied calmly, "What specific type of design are you seeking?"

The caller responded politely, "Mr. Vance wishes to commission an outfit for his fiancée. He prefers to discuss the particulars in person at The Grand Crestwood."

It was then that Evelyn registered the client's identity.

The Vance family? She realized she knew very little about them.

Sensing her hesitation, the caller continued, "The meeting must be conducted one-on-one. Mr. Vance highly values discretion. The presence of additional personnel would only complicate matters."

Evelyn's brow furrowed slightly. Something felt off.

Noting her reluctance, the caller quickly added assurance. "Evelyn, please trust our intentions are genuine. If you agree to the meeting, we will transfer the deposit immediately. How does a five hundred thousand dollar advance sound?"

Evelyn was taken aback by the offer.

The client was certainly not hesitant about investing in what he wanted.

After a moment's consideration, she agreed. "Alright, send over the details. I will be there."

After all, this was a wealthy family; surely there was no real cause for alarm.

Shortly after the call ended, Evelyn's phone buzzed with a message containing the meeting particulars.
###Chapter 278

The message read: "The Grand Crestwood, tonight at 7 o'clock."

"Why this hotel again?" Evelyn was visibly perplexed.

She recalled the previous scam call that had directed her to the very same location.

Could this truly be a coincidence?

Evelyn shook her head, momentarily dismissing her suspicions. The client had already transferred a significant deposit. A scammer wouldn't go to such lengths, would they?

Furthermore, The Grand Crestwood was one of the most luxurious hotels in the city. With numerous staff present, she felt her safety was reasonably assured.

Nevertheless, Evelyn wasn't taking any chances. She packed a few discreet self-defense items. It was a prudent precaution, given her recent streak of unfortunate events.

She couldn't afford another injury, especially with Alexander's birthday approaching.

After finishing her work, Evelyn informed her office of her evening plans and headed to The Grand Crestwood.

But upon arrival, the hotel manager stopped her.

His gaze swept over her, clear disapproval in his eyes, pinpointing her attire as unsuitable for the establishment's luxury standards.

"Who are you? Are you unaware of our formal dress code? Do you believe you belong here dressed like that?" the hotel manager addressed her, his tone dripping with disdain.

Caught off guard, Evelyn quickly apologized. "My apologies, I was unaware."

This was her first visit to The Grand Crestwood; the dress code was news to her.

It seemed she would have to return home to change.

At that moment, Theodore's assistant called again, his voice carrying a note of urgency. "Ms. Rossi, have you arrived?"

The question ignited a flicker of doubt in her mind.

Why did he sound so eager for her presence?

Shaking off her reservations, Evelyn responded, "I'm sorry, I've been stopped at the entrance. It may take me some additional time."

After a brief pause, the voice on the phone assured her, "Please wait right there."

Perhaps a wardrobe change wouldn't be necessary after all.

Evelyn paused, considering hailing a cab for a quick retreat, but decided to wait.

Soon, a man in a sharp suit emerged from the hotel.

He recognized Evelyn immediately and approached with formal courtesy. "Ms. Rossi, I am the one who contacted you. Mr. Vance is awaiting your arrival. Please, follow me."

Evelyn nodded. "Alright."

Without further deliberation, she followed him inside.

Suddenly, someone rushed over and blocked her path once more.

She looked up—it was the same hotel manager.

His haughty demeanor had completely vanished. Instead, he clutched Evelyn's hand, desperation in his voice.

"Please, forgive me! I had no idea you were an esteemed guest of Mr. Vance! I was completely ignorant of your importance. Please, overlook my mistake this once—I swear it will never happen again!"

Evelyn stood there, utterly dumbfounded, trying to process the sudden, dramatic shift.

Theodore's assistant frowned deeply and called out to the security personnel behind him. "What are you waiting for? Remove him. Immediately."

Almost instantly, two bodyguards approached and seized the hotel manager.

As they dragged him away, his face contorted and he began shouting, drawing stares from everyone nearby.

Evelyn's frown deepened, her confusion mounting. "What in the world is going on here?"
###Chapter 279

Daniel Pierce, Theodore Vance's assistant, spoke to Evelyn in a tone that brooked no argument. "Ms. Rossi, I am aware the hotel manager was disrespectful to you earlier. He will be dealt with appropriately. We should not let such a minor inconvenience delay us."

"Mr. Vance is waiting upstairs."

Evelyn stood frozen, her eyes fixed on the hotel manager trembling on the floor, his face a mask of pure terror.

"What does that mean?" Evelyn asked, her voice laced with worry.

Firing him seemed excessive. The incident had been barely a bother to her.

She was used to such slights and usually ignored them unless they were egregious.

Punishing the manager so severely for something so trivial felt like a massive overreaction.

Her shock intensified when Daniel answered with icy calm. "We'll simply break one of his leg."

"What?" Evelyn's voice broke, her disbelief clear.

All this because he was slightly rude? Now this man was going to have his leg shattered?

"That's insane! You can't do that!" Evelyn protested, her tone urgent. "He hardly did anything! I don't even care about it! Stop this before it goes too far!"

She was bewildered by the extreme reaction. Was this how the wealthy handled every minor offense?

Daniel looked at Evelyn, his expression one of genuine surprise. "Ms. Rossi, are you suggesting we let him go?"

Evelyn's patience snapped, irritation coloring her words. "What else would I mean?"

She shook her head, her voice sharp. "I certainly hope you weren't serious about breaking his leg. That's savage. He's a person, not a toy."

After a brief pause, Daniel's posture relaxed slightly in acquiescence. "Very well. If that is your wish."

He raised a hand, signaling his men to release the manager. He fixed the man with a stern glare. "Remember this. Ms. Rossi is Mr. Vance's honored guest. Any future disrespect will not be tolerated."

His voice dropped to a frigid whisper as he snapped at the manager. "Well? Don't just lie there! Thank Ms. Rossi!"

The manager shuddered, his voice quaking as he bowed repeatedly. "Thank you, Ms. Rossi! I swear it will never happen again!"

Despite the resolution, Evelyn felt uneasy. She managed a nod. "It's fine. You can go."

The manager didn't need to be told twice. He nodded vigorously and scrambled away, relief evident in his haste.

Daniel turned his cold gaze from the retreating figure back to Evelyn. "Is the outcome to your satisfaction, Ms. Rossi? Do you require anything further?"

"No, that's all," Evelyn replied, her voice steadier as she took a deep breath, trying to quell her inner turmoil.

Daniel's smile was a fleeting, barely perceptible twitch of his lips. "Then if you would please follow me. Mr. Vance has been anticipating your arrival."

There was a disquieting formality in his tone that sent a chill down Evelyn's spine.

She felt like she had accidentally stepped into a den of snakes.

The calculated precision of his assistant's actions spoke volumes about Theodore Vance's own nature.

What had seemed like a simple business meeting was rapidly transforming into something much darker.

If they were willing to break a man's leg over a petty insult, what would they do if she truly angered Theodore?

A wave of apprehension washed over her. Subconsciously, her grip tightened on the pepper spray hidden in her pocket.

Could she escape quickly if things turned dangerous?

Her mind raced through potential exit strategies, but she maintained a composed exterior.

With practiced casualness, Evelyn retrieved her phone from her purse, scrolling to find Alexander Blackwood's contact.

Her finger hovered over the call button, ready to summon help at the first sign of trouble.

Finally, Daniel stopped before the entrance to a private room. He gestured for her to enter alone, stepping aside. "Ms. Rossi, please, after you."

His retreat from the doorway emphasized the isolation of the meeting that awaited her.
###Chapter 280

Evelyn drew a sharp breath.

She pushed the heavy door open and stepped inside.

The room was immense.

A profound silence filled the air.

It was completely empty.

She hesitated for a moment, confused.

Then she moved further into the space.

The door clicked shut behind her.

The sound echoed softly.

Her eyes scanned the room.

They landed on a painting hanging on the far wall.

It felt familiar instantly.

A jolt of recognition hit her.

It was her painting.

She had created it years ago.

She was visiting a small, remote fishing village overseas.

She remembered donating it to the local school.

She wanted the children to learn from it.

How did it get here?

Memories flooded her mind.

They were powerful and vivid.

A deep voice spoke from behind.

It broke her concentration.

"Do you remember now, Evelyn?"

She spun around quickly.

A man stood there.

Theodore Vance.

He was impeccably dressed in a tailored suit.

His hair was perfectly styled.

Evelyn stared, trying to place him.

"Who are you?" she asked.

He seemed familiar, but she couldn't grasp the memory.

A soft smile touched his lips.

"I'm Theodore Vance. Years ago, in that fishing village, you saved me. I was badly hurt. You took care of me for weeks."

Her eyes widened in shock.

"It was you!" she exclaimed.

Joy and surprise mixed in her voice.

"You vanished so suddenly. I looked for you for a long time. I'm just so glad you're alive. I thought you might not make it."

Warmth shone in Theodore's eyes.

He took a step toward her.

"I've been searching for you too. I wanted to thank you properly. That's why I came back to Crestwood. Finding you again... it means the world to me."

His voice was heavy with sincerity.

Evelyn smiled warmly.

"I'm happy to see you, too."

She paused, her expression turning serious.

"But you don't owe me anything. Knowing you survived was all the thanks I needed."

The pieces of the evening fell into place for Evelyn.

She let out a soft, understanding chuckle.

"So, the five-million-dollar design contract... that was your thank you, wasn't it?"

It was clear to her now. Theodore was the mysterious client.

She didn't wait for his confirmation.

"I truly don't need any repayment. If you aren't genuinely interested in my work, you should cancel the order."

Theodore's gaze was intense and serious.

His voice was firm. "No. I owe you a debt. You saved my life. Without you, I wouldn't be here. Besides..."

He paused, leaving a deeper sentiment unspoken.

Back then, he had been certain he would die.

His injuries were severe.

But she, a stranger, had nursed him day and night.

She never gave up hope for his recovery.

Evelyn understood the weight of his silence.

She shook her head gently. "Mr. Vance, I didn't help you for a reward. My only wish was for you to live. That's all."

Theodore moved closer.

His resolve was unmistakable.

"Evelyn, will you marry me? I want to make you the happiest woman in all of Crestwood."

The proposal was utterly unexpected.

It stole the breath from her lungs.
###Chapter 281

Theodore.

Evelyn quickly raised her hands, stepping back to create distance. "Mr. Vance, please stop. I am a married woman."

Theodore let out a cold, dismissive laugh. "I'm aware. But that invalid, Alexander Blackwood, cannot possibly give you the happiness you deserve."

After confirming Evelyn was the one who had saved his life years ago, Theodore had ordered a deep investigation into her past. He discovered she had been betrayed by her ex-boyfriend just before marrying Alexander.

In Theodore's eyes, Alexander was nothing more than a wheelchair-bound reject from the Blackwood family, hardly a worthy opponent. He was confident he could make Evelyn his.

While initially relieved to see Theodore alive, his derogatory words about her husband instantly soured her mood. Her expression turned cold. "I must insist you show some respect when speaking about my husband."

Seeing her defend Alexander so fiercely only fueled the jealousy burning inside Theodore.

Clenching his jaw, he made his offer. "Evelyn, I can give you everything. Wealth, influence, anything you could ever want. Marry me, and it's all yours. Why remain loyal to a man like Alexander?"

Evelyn stared at him, taken aback by his audacity.

Their previous interactions had been limited. His sudden obsession was both puzzling and unwelcome. She realized any further conversation would be futile.

She took a steadying breath, her voice calm but firm. "Mr. Vance, I came here tonight to discuss business. If you are not interested in a professional partnership, then this conversation is over."

She moved to walk past him, but he blocked her path, his gaze intense. "And what if I'm not ready to let you leave?"

A spike of fear shot through her.

The man whose life she had saved was now threatening her.

Her first instinct was to fight back, but she remembered the bodyguards stationed outside the door. She was outnumbered. A direct confrontation would be unwise.

So, she changed her approach, forcing her posture to remain composed. "I cannot stop you from wanting to repay a debt."

A flicker of anticipation lit Theodore's eyes, like a child seeing a desired toy within reach.

Evelyn continued, her tone unwavering. "However, if your intention is truly to repay me, shouldn't it be on my terms? Forcing me into something I do not want would only turn gratitude into resentment."

Theodore's brow furrowed. "That is not my intention, Evelyn. I genuinely wish to treat you well."

"Then let me leave. All I want right now is to walk out of here safely."

Theodore fell silent.

Evelyn maintained her cool exterior, but internally, every nerve was on edge. Her hand slipped discreetly into her purse, her fingers closing around a small can of pepper spray.

Seconds stretched into an eternity before Theodore finally relented. "Very well. You may go."

Evelyn exhaled a silent breath of relief and turned towards the door. But his voice stopped her once more.

With her back to him, he posed his final, haunting question. "Evelyn, are you truly telling me you will never consider divorcing Alexander?"

Her answer was immediate and absolute. "My husband and I share a bond that is unbreakable. I will never abandon him. Not for any price."

A heavy silence filled the room. Then, suddenly, Theodore began to laugh.

The sound was harsh and unexpected. Puzzled, Evelyn turned to face him again, her brows drawn together in confusion as she tried to decipher the meaning behind his laughter.
###Chapter 282

Theodore studied Evelyn with an appraising look.
"Impressive," he stated calmly.
"You actually passed my test."

Evelyn blinked, completely bewildered.
She stared at him as if he'd lost his mind.

He raised an eyebrow, his expression unreadable.
"If you had agreed to marry me and abandoned your husband, I would have been deeply disappointed."
A slow smile touched his lips.
"But you are still the same woman who saved me all those years ago. You haven't changed at all."

His smile widened confidently.
"You may not want to marry me now, and that's acceptable."
His voice dropped to a low, certain tone.
"Sooner or later, you will fall for me. It's inevitable."

Evelyn was rendered speechless.
She let out a short, disbelieving laugh and shot him a look of pure scorn.
"You're completely insane."

She had no intention of remaining another second.
She turned swiftly, yanked the door open, and strode out without a backward glance.

Outside, Daniel was startled by her abrupt exit.
He hurried into the room.
"Sir, Ms. Rossi just left like that? Shouldn't you go after her? She seemed genuinely angry."

Theodore gave a lazy, knowing smirk.
His gaze drifted to an abstract painting on the wall.
"She can't run forever."

Daniel fell silent for a moment, choosing his words carefully.
"Tonight was a perfect chance. You should have tried to keep her here longer. After searching for her all these years..."

"I had no desire to let her leave," Theodore replied, his voice even.
"But it was obvious she didn't wish to stay. I could see she was ready to fight her way out if necessary."

Daniel was at a loss for a response.
Why was Evelyn suddenly so defensive around his boss?

Had he known about the sudden marriage proposal, he might have mustered the courage to question Theodore's methods.
What sane person proposes to a woman they've just reconnected with, especially when she's married?

But Daniel remained unaware of the specifics.

Theodore's next words were deliberate and measured.
"She has a fierce spirit. Trying to force her to stay would have backfired spectacularly."

Daniel paused, then nodded slowly.
"That's true, sir."

"There's no need to rush. Given time, Evelyn will come around," Theodore said, a deep, confident chuckle rumbling in his chest.

A faint frown briefly crossed Daniel's face.
Come around?
Hadn't his boss just described her as strong-willed?

He dismissed the thought as he observed Theodore's utterly self-assured smile.
It was better not to question his boss's convoluted plans.
Perhaps Theodore had finally decided on a more strategic approach.

Evelyn arrived home safely, but a deep unease settled in her chest.
Normally, seeing someone she had helped in the past doing so well should have been a happy occasion.
She never expected the encounter to take such a bizarre turn.
What would Theodore Vance do next?

During dinner, Alexander noticed Evelyn spacing out occasionally.
He felt compelled to ask, "Evelyn, is everything alright? You seem distant. Have you been overworking yourself?"
###Chapter 283

Evelyn’s thoughts snapped back to the present.

She shook her head firmly. "No."

Her tone left no room for argument. She wouldn't dwell on Theodore's pursuit. She certainly wouldn't mention it to Alexander.

She could easily imagine his reaction. His face would darken with displeasure. A hint of jealousy would flicker in his eyes.

His birthday was approaching. She wanted to keep the mood light.

She tilted her head, her voice casual. "Alexander, isn't your birthday soon?"

He blinked, genuine surprise on his face. "I'd almost forgotten."

Evelyn feigned shock. "How could you forget your own birthday?"

He shrugged, his gaze turning reflective. "I never celebrated much as a child. There was no one to celebrate with. A birthday alone isn't a celebration," he explained softly.

Her heart ached for him. She leaned closer, her voice warm. "But you have me now. I'm here with you. We have to celebrate."

A slow smile spread across Alexander's face. Anticipation lit his eyes.

"You're right," he agreed.

After a moment, he remembered something. He had once seen Evelyn's records. Her birthdate was blank. "When is your birthday?" he asked curiously.

"I don't know," she replied softly, her eyes downcast. She shook her head gently. A wistful smile touched her lips. "I was left at the orphanage doorstep. We all shared a birthday there. They used the day they found me. Sometimes, I envy people who know their real birthday."

A sharp pang of empathy struck Alexander. His chest tightened. He took a steadying breath. Then he reached out and gently took her hand.

"Then let's do this. My birthday is in a few days. We'll celebrate ours together this year."

Evelyn's face brightened. Excitement sparkled in her eyes. "Yes, let's do that. It will be much more fun."

With the plan set, Evelyn threw herself into preparations. She decorated Serenity Oaks with festive touches. She meticulously handcrafted invitation cards. Her dedication was clear. Her excitement was contagious.

Alexander watched her with an affectionate chuckle. "Isn't this a bit much for a birthday?" he teased, amused.

Her eyes shone with sincerity. "That's exactly why we should! It's a birthday. It deserves a proper celebration. I know you prefer things quiet. We'll keep the guest list small. Only a few friends you actually like."

He arched a brow and chuckled lightly. "Fine. I'll leave it to you."

As Evelyn finished writing the invitations, she handed him a stack. "These are for your friends. I'll handle the invites for Charlotte and the others from the orphanage," she said cheerfully.

"Absolutely, darling. Consider it done," Alexander promised with dramatic flair. He tucked the invitations away carefully.

The next morning, Alexander walked the quiet halls of Benjamin's private hospital. He was there for a check-up on his leg.

In truth, the visit was a cover. He needed to discuss Theodore's latest movements.

In the dim office, Benjamin pretended to examine Alexander's leg. He whispered, "I heard Theodore has been searching for years for the woman who saved him. He found her. They met at The Grand Crestwood last night. His security is impenetrable. We tried to get closer for information. His men are always a step ahead."

Alexander's expression hardened. "Stay on him. We need to identify that woman. Whatever the cost," he instructed sharply.

"Understood," Benjamin nodded discreetly. Then he changed the subject. "Alexander, did you have anything to do with Cassandra's injury?"
###Chapter 284

"What are you implying?" Alexander responded, his expression one of genuine bewilderment.

Benjamin studied him closely, searching for any hint of deception. The confusion seemed authentic. If Alexander was acting, he was a master. Benjamin raised a skeptical eyebrow. "Cassandra was attacked last night. Someone slashed her face. She was rushed to the hospital. The doctors stopped the bleeding, but the cut was deep. It will leave a scar."

Alexander showed almost no reaction. "That has nothing to do with me." His voice was calm, detached. He had only just learned of the incident himself.

His eyes narrowed slightly as he looked at Benjamin, a flicker of suspicion appearing. "Why would you immediately assume I was involved?"

He never needed to get his own hands dirty. That wasn't his style.

Benjamin leaned back against the desk, crossing his arms. "Because Cassandra targeted Evelyn." A faint, almost amused smirk played on his lips. "Sebastian and I both know how you are when it comes to your wife. It's not a stretch to think you might have taken matters into your own hands."

Alexander's expression remained unreadable. "I have no reason to go to such extremes. Cassandra brought this upon herself."

If she hadn't tried to frame Evelyn, she wouldn't have faced public humiliation for days.

Benjamin gave a slight nod, seemingly accepting the answer. There was no point in pushing further.

Then, Alexander seemed to recall something. "I have something for you."

He reached into his suit jacket's inner pocket and retrieved a small, elegant card.

Benjamin eyed him warily. "You're being unusually formal... This isn't bad news, is it?"

Alexander gave him a flat look before handing over the invitation. "My birthday is the day after tomorrow. Evelyn insists you attend the party."

Benjamin blinked, momentarily wondering if he had heard correctly.

He took the invitation, his eyes quickly scanning the details. It was genuine—a formal birthday party invitation.

His head snapped up in disbelief. "Are you seriously the same Alexander Blackwood I've known for years? Or have you been replaced by an imposter? You've never celebrated your birthday. What's changed this year? This is so unlike you!"

He flipped the invitation open, and his gaze immediately fell on the neat, graceful handwriting inside—definitely not Alexander's.

Benjamin smirked in understanding. "Ah... This was Evelyn's idea, wasn't it?"

"Precisely." At the mere mention of Evelyn's name, Alexander's usual stern expression softened into a rare, effortless smile. "She enjoys celebrations. So you will be there."

"Of course," Benjamin agreed without a moment's hesitation.

Alexander produced another invitation and held it out. "This one is for Sebastian. Deliver it to him."

"Understood," Benjamin said, taking the second card.

It felt surreal. Alexander, of all people, was hosting a birthday party.

Then again, since it was Evelyn's wish, it made perfect sense.

She had him completely wrapped around her finger.

Sebastian took the invitation from Benjamin and stared at it, utterly astonished.

"Good heavens, Alexander is becoming a complete stranger to me!" he exclaimed dramatically.

"This is entirely Evelyn's influence! If she weren't fond of birthdays, do you think he would even consider celebrating?"

Benjamin smirked. "Absolutely not."

"In that case, I need to pick out an exceptional gift." Sebastian nodded thoughtfully, stroking his chin.

He glanced at Benjamin. "So, what are you planning to get him?"

They had been friends for years but rarely exchanged gifts.

Even as close friends, they honestly had little idea what Alexander personally enjoyed.

But now, the answer was obvious. Alexander's world revolved entirely around Evelyn.

A knowing grin spread across Sebastian's face. "Naturally. I'll make sure to get them something truly memorable."
###Chapter 285

Sebastian leaned in, his expression eager.

"I need your support on this," he said, his voice low and serious.

Benjamin studied him, a flicker of interest in his eyes.

"What's the plan?" he asked.

Sebastian moved closer, whispering the idea into Benjamin's ear.

Benjamin's face immediately showed doubt.

He shook his head. "Are you sure about this? I doubt Alexander will take it well."

But Sebastian was unwavering.

"His opinion doesn't matter. Only Evelyn's approval counts. Don't you want to see Alexander's reaction when he sees the present?"

A slow smile spread across Benjamin's face. The idea was growing on him.

"That would be quite a scene," he admitted.

Sebastian's eyes gleamed with mischief. Benjamin found himself intrigued by the challenge of pushing Alexander's buttons.

They shared a look of understanding.

A conspiratorial bond formed instantly between them.

They began plotting their secret scheme in hushed tones.

Meanwhile, Evelyn was sharing news with Charlotte.

She told her about the upcoming birthday party.

Charlotte looked surprised. "A party? So suddenly?"

Evelyn laughed. "It's Alexander's birthday soon. Since I never knew my own, we decided to celebrate together this year."

Charlotte chuckled. "You two are attached at the hip. I'm starting to feel left out!"

Evelyn quickly hugged her friend.

"Listen," she said playfully. "No matter how close I am with Alexander, you will always be my number one. Propose to me right now, and I'll go home and file for divorce!"

Charlotte laughed and pushed her away gently.

"Remember when you used to follow me everywhere? It was exhausting! I'm finally free. Marry you? Never."

Evelyn put on a dramatically hurt expression.

She clung to Charlotte, pretending to be heartbroken.

Charlotte's face turned serious.

"I heard about the plagiarism accusations. Why didn't you tell me?"

She was clearly upset, having only recently learned about the online attacks.

Charlotte knew how much design meant to Evelyn. The allegations must have been devastating.

Seeing her concern, Evelyn smiled reassuringly.

"I'm fine, really. I stayed home and avoided the internet. I barely saw any of the hate. The issue was resolved quickly anyway. It didn't seem worth mentioning."

She gently patted Charlotte's hand.

"You're always so busy with work. I didn't want to bother you with something small. But I promise, if anything serious happens, you'll be the first to know."

Charlotte still sounded worried. "Just be careful, okay?"

She often worried about Evelyn being too trusting, fearing she could be easily tricked.

Fortunately, Alexander was now there to watch over her.

The two friends talked for hours.

They were still deep in conversation as night fell.

Two days later, at 7 PM sharp, Serenity Oaks was transformed.

The usually quiet estate was now a vibrant celebration.

The garden glowed with warm light.

Beautiful decorations covered every surface.

Birthday banners hung gracefully on the walls.

Sebastian stopped abruptly upon entering.

His eyes widened in shock.

"Whoa," he exclaimed. "Is this really Alexander's house? Or did I walk into the wrong place by mistake?"
###Chapter 286

The procession of gifts brought a continuous sparkle to Evelyn's eyes.
Each thoughtful present was a treasure.
She directed the staff to carefully store them all away.
She was eager to examine everything in detail once the celebration was over.

Then came the evening's main event.
With a dramatic flourish, Evelyn flipped a switch.
The room was instantly plunged into a soft, expectant darkness.
A hushed silence fell over the assembled guests.

Slowly, she wheeled in a cart.
On it sat an extravagantly decorated cake, glowing with candlelight.
Her voice, soft yet clear, cut through the quiet.
"Happy birthday to you, happy birthday to you..."
Her gentle, heartfelt singing was the perfect culmination of the magical night.

Seated in his wheelchair, Alexander watched her approach.
His eyes held a warm, intense light.
The flickering candles illuminated her face, casting a serene glow.
It was a sight that reached deep into his soul.

"Go on, Alexander, make a wish!" Evelyn urged softly.
She positioned the cake directly in front of him on the cart.

His gaze lingered on the homemade creation.
Evelyn felt a flush of pride mixed with sudden shyness.
"I made it myself," she explained. "It might not look as perfect as one from a professional bakery. But I promise the taste is good."

A profound emotion shimmered in Alexander's eyes.
He looked up at her, his voice slightly rough with feeling.
"When did you find the time to do this? I had no idea."

Evelyn let out a soft laugh, her eyes sparkling with playful secrecy.
"Well, it wouldn't be a surprise if you knew, would it?"

A powerful wave of warmth flooded Alexander's heart.
He reached out, his hand extending towards her.

With a shy smile, Evelyn stepped closer.
She slipped her hand into his.

Their friends, witnessing the intimate exchange, erupted into cheerful applause and laughter.

"Thank you, Evelyn," Alexander whispered.
His quiet tone was filled with unmistakable gratitude.
###Chapter 287

Sebastian chuckled, a mischievous glint in his eyes.
"Don't be so modest, Evelyn. Your cake is amazing—it could easily compete with any professional bakery."

As the candle flames danced, Alexander closed his eyes and made a silent wish.
With a gentle nudge, Evelyn followed suit.

The moment they blew out the candles, the lights in Serenity Oaks blazed back to life, filling the room with a brilliant glow.

Laughter echoed as they shared the cake, the simple celebration brimming with genuine warmth and happiness.

As evening fell, Eleanor Jenkins, citing her age, said her goodbyes and left early.

The party was winding down, and the group was about to go their separate ways when Sebastian, energized by the night, made a suggestion.
"It's still early. How about we hit a bar for a bit more fun?"

Alexander immediately refused. "No, that's not a good idea. Evelyn doesn't drink."

Sebastian, however, was not one to give up easily. "Who says you need to drink to have fun? Bars offer more than just alcohol."

He turned to Evelyn, his voice encouraging. "I bet you haven't been to many bars. They're exciting, full of energy! Don't worry, it's my place, perfectly safe, and we're all with you."

Evelyn's hesitation vanished, replaced by a playful smile as she looked at Alexander. "I want to go, Alexander."

Alexander let out a deep sigh, his head dipping slightly in resignation. "Fine. If you're set on it, then we'll go," he conceded, his tone reluctant.

Witnessing Alexander's sudden change of heart, Sebastian and Benjamin exchanged knowing glances, their eyebrows raised in silent commentary.
One moment he was firmly against it, and the next, a simple request from Evelyn made him fold.
His reversal wasn't just quick; it was absolute.

Soon, they arrived at Sebastian's popular bar, his own establishment.

Eager to make the night memorable, Sebastian had secured the most luxurious private room available, complete with plush seating and mood lighting.

As everyone settled into the opulent space, Sebastian produced a deck of cards, a sly grin spreading across his face.
"Let's make things interesting. Everyone draws a card. The person with the lowest card faces a round of Truth or Dare," he declared, shuffling the deck with practiced flair.

Somehow, luck was not on Evelyn's side, and she ended up drawing the lowest card.

Trying to keep things light, Alexander opted for a gentle question. "What did you have for dinner tonight?"

Sebastian scoffed, his face twisting into a mock expression of exasperation. "Alexander, do you even know how this game works?"

Clearly, he had been hoping for a question with more spice.

Evelyn burst into laughter and answered quickly, "Chocolate cake."

Sebastian narrowed his eyes, leaning forward with keen interest. "Evelyn, are you truly happy with Alexander?"

"Completely," Evelyn replied without a moment's hesitation.

Sebastian shook his head slightly. "That's not what I meant. I'm talking about Alexander's... performance. Is he good in bed?"

A deep blush instantly spread across Evelyn's cheeks.
"W-what?" she stammered.

Sebastian's smirk widened. "Why so flustered? Does that mean he's not good? If that's the case, marrying him must be a real disappointment. You're not a widow, but you might as well be living like one..."

Shocked, Evelyn waved her hands frantically. "No, no! Alexander is very good in bed!"

Sebastian's eyebrows shot up in feigned astonishment. "Oh? So you two have already... you know..."

He leaned in closer, his face a mask of curiosity, clearly eager to dig for more details.

But before he could continue, Alexander cut in, his tone sharp and final. "Sebastian, that's enough. You've asked your question."

Marrying A Secret Billionaire: A New Beginning
###Chapter 288

Hearing that, Sebastian could only sigh with regret.
"Alright then."
His voice was thick with disappointment.

Evelyn, who had just breathed a quiet sigh of relief, felt her frustration spike as she lost again in the very next round.
A wave of helplessness washed over her.
She was completely bewildered by her continuous streak of bad luck tonight.

In sharp contrast, Alexander's mood improved dramatically.
His eyes sparkled with mischief, eager to hear more scandalous details.

But before he could press for more juicy gossip, Alexander cut him off.
"According to the rules, if you refuse to answer, you can just take a drink, right?" Alexander challenged, one eyebrow raised.

"Absolutely," Sebastian replied, his tone a mix of intrigue and surprise.
He then turned his curious gaze to Evelyn, his question laced with concern.
"But I thought Evelyn didn't drink?"

With a dismissive snort, Alexander responded, "She doesn't. So I'll drink for her."
He swiftly grabbed the glass in front of him and downed its contents in one decisive motion.

Evelyn was caught completely off guard.
She had no chance to protest.
She could have handled the question herself, but Alexander's protective instinct was clear.
He was determined to shield her from the alcohol, and she was equally determined not to let him drink on her account.

"Oh, come on, Alexander, that's not fair!" Sebastian burst out, annoyed that his chance for gossip had been cleverly avoided.

Alexander merely smirked, his expression one of smug satisfaction.
"I'm Evelyn's husband. It's my job to protect her," he declared proudly, his stance firm and unyielding.

Reluctantly, Sebastian backed down.
He decided not to ask any more inappropriate questions.
Provoking Alexander would only lead to his own downfall later.

The game continued, and Sebastian found himself on a terrible losing streak.
Alexander was ruthless, digging deep into Sebastian's most painful past heartaches.

"Sebastian, I heard your first girlfriend left you because you lacked experience. Is that true?"

"Sebastian, rumor has it you drowned your sorrows in the nightlife after that heartbreak. Can you confirm that?"

"Do you still think about your first love?"

With each probing question about his early romantic life, Sebastian's face turned a deeper shade of red.
His embarrassment was palpable.

Evelyn and Charlotte listened with evident delight, soaking up every scandalous detail.

"Wow, Sebastian, who knew you were such a naive young man back then?" Charlotte remarked, her laughter ringing through the room.

All eyes were on Sebastian, waiting for his answer.
Instead, he grabbed his glass and took three large gulps as a form of punishment.

With a mix of resentment and resolve, Sebastian muttered, "Just you wait, Alexander. When it's your turn to lose, I'll make you squirm!"

Alexander just laughed, his confidence unshaken.
"Then show me what you've got! Make me lose!"

The game escalated into a fierce battle between Alexander and Sebastian.
The tension was thick, drawing loud laughter and gasps from the onlookers who were thoroughly entertained.

Ultimately, it was no surprise when Sebastian suffered a resounding defeat.
After downing several glasses in quick succession, he became so drunk he crumpled to the floor, unable to stand.

Alexander, unable to hide a grin, leaned over him and taunted, "Get up, Sebastian. Keep going. Don't just lie there defeated."

Amused yet concerned, Benjamin stepped in.
Seeing Sebastian was genuinely drunk, he intervened with a chuckle.
"Alright, Alexander, that's enough. He's far past his limit; he's not faking it. We should call it a night. He's been the evening's entertainment already."

Benjamin offered a hand to help Sebastian to his feet.
"Let's go. I'll take you home."

Despite his unsteady condition, Sebastian resisted, his words slurred but determined.
"I'm not leaving! I can still outdrink Alexander!"

With a dismissive snort, Alexander retorted, "Fine. Stand up on your own, and I'll give you another round."

Evelyn watched the scene, her expression one of disbelief.
This was a side of Alexander she had never seen.
His usual calm composure was replaced by a childish competitiveness.
It was revealing.
Beneath his cool exterior was a playful, almost boyish spirit, ignited by a simple challenge.
###Chapter 289

Evelyn could see the genuine contentment on Alexander's face.

He was truly at peace here.

Surrounded by his closest friends, he seemed to shed all his usual burdens.

Watching Sebastian sway slightly from the alcohol, Evelyn decided to intervene. Her voice was soft as she spoke to Alexander. "Please, go easy on him. You know how Sebastian gets when he's celebrating."

Alexander paused, considering her words. His stern expression softened at her gentle plea.

"Alright," he finally conceded. "Benjamin, make sure Sebastian gets home safely. Watch his step."

As Benjamin guided a unsteady Sebastian out, Charlotte also made moves to leave.

She sensed that Evelyn and Alexander might appreciate a moment alone.

"My inbox is overflowing," she explained, using work as a polite excuse. "I should really get back to it."

Evelyn, always concerned for her friend, immediately offered. "Let us give you a ride. It's really no problem."

Charlotte shook her head firmly. "I'm perfectly fine, and it's a short distance. I'll manage."

After a brief back-and-forth, Alexander smoothly resolved it. He signaled to his assistant. "Please ensure Ms. Sinclair arrives home safely."

"Right away, Mr. Blackwood," the assistant replied promptly.

With their departure, the room grew quiet. Only Evelyn and Alexander remained, bathed in the soft, intimate lighting.

Evelyn noticed the faint flush on Alexander's cheeks. Concern tinged her voice. "Alexander, you drank quite a bit tonight. Are you feeling alright?"

He handled his liquor far better than most.

But seeing her worry, he gave her a calm, reassuring smile. "Just a little. Some fresh air will clear my head."

Evelyn nodded in agreement. "Okay. I'll just be a moment in the restroom, then we can walk."

"Sounds perfect," Alexander said.

As Evelyn walked back from the restroom, a waiter stepped into her path.

"Pardon me, are you Evelyn Blackwood?" he asked.

Startled, Evelyn looked at him cautiously. "Yes? Who's asking?"

The waiter maintained a respectful tone. "I was asked to deliver a gift to you. Please accept it."

He then presented a lavish bouquet of deep red roses. The petals were velvety and rich, exuding a powerful, romantic fragrance.

Evelyn was taken aback. She hesitantly accepted the large bouquet, her mind racing.

"Who sent these?" she asked, her curiosity instantly ignited.

The waiter offered a polite, knowing smile. "A gentleman of considerable charm."

Immediately, Evelyn's thoughts flew to Alexander.

The roses were an unexpected, romantic gesture.

Today was, after all, a dual celebration of both their birthdays.

This must be his unique way of marking their special day.

A radiant smile bloomed on Evelyn's face, warmed by the thoughtful gift.

As she turned gracefully to return to the room, she was completely unaware of the enigmatic figure watching from the shadows.

Hidden from view, Theodore's eyes sparkled with triumph as he saw Evelyn accept the roses he had sent anonymously. A smug smile spread across his face.

This woman, who had so vehemently refused him, was now cherishing his roses.

Her eyes, reflecting the dark red petals, seemed to dance with a secret delight.

Theodore had been discreetly monitoring Evelyn's movements.

He knew tonight was a joint birthday celebration for her and Alexander, attended by their inner circle.

With this gesture, he intended to subtly push Alexander, her unworthy husband, aside.

His confidence swelled. He was certain the cracks in Evelyn's marriage would soon widen.

Once she was free, he would approach her anew, presenting his love with authenticity and passion.

After all, wasn't a grand romantic gesture the key to winning a resistant heart?

Blissfully ignorant of Theodore's dark plotting, Evelyn reentered the room, her spirits lifted by the beautiful bouquet.

However, the sight of the flowers immediately caught Alexander's attention. His eyebrow arched inquisitively.

"Evelyn," he asked, his tone measured. "Where did these come from?"
###Chapter 290

Evelyn stared, completely taken aback. "What? You didn't send these?" She let out a soft, incredulous laugh. "I was certain it was your idea of a romantic surprise."

Alexander's face clouded with genuine confusion. "No. I haven't ordered any flowers."

Intrigued, Evelyn moved closer to him, the bouquet of red roses still held securely. She peered down at the blooms, her brow furrowed in thought.

"Then who sent them?" she whispered, more to herself than to him.

Her fingers gently parted the velvety petals, searching for a note. There was nothing. No card, no clue.

She shrugged, a light laugh escaping her. "Must be from another friend, then."

Alexander rubbed his chin thoughtfully. "Perhaps it was Sebastian. He's known for his spontaneous, and often unannounced, gestures."

Evelyn's eyes sparkled with amusement as she looked at him. "Does that bother you? You sound almost competitive, not like best friends."

He gave a casual, good-natured shrug. "Just joking. Sebastian and I are close."

"Alright," Evelyn said, her smile softening as she accepted the mystery. "If Sebastian sent them, that's very sweet. We should appreciate the thought."

"Of course," Alexander replied, his tone warm and agreeable. "As long as you're happy."

Laughing together, they made their way out of the bar's dim private room, their cheerful conversation blending into the lively atmosphere.

From a shadowy corner, Theodore Vance watched them leave. His brows knitted together in confusion.

This was strange. Evelyn had just accepted roses from another man, and Alexander, her husband, showed not a flicker of jealousy.

Was their marriage lacking the passion it should have?

A slow, mocking sneer spread across Theodore's face.

Earlier, Evelyn's tender care for Alexander had almost convinced him of a deep bond between them. Now, it seemed like nothing but an act.

Perhaps Alexander knew he was inadequate for a woman like Evelyn.

Maybe, faced with a rival like Theodore, Alexander was simply too cowardly to put up a fight.

If that was true, then victory was practically in Theodore's grasp.

His original plan had been to drive a wedge between them and exploit the resulting conflict.

While things hadn't gone exactly as planned, Theodore wasn't concerned. Any development that worked to his advantage was a good one.

Evelyn, meanwhile, hadn't given Theodore a second thought.

After leaving the bar, she strolled along the shoreline, pushing Alexander's wheelchair.

The cool night sea breeze washed over them, refreshing and calming.

A peaceful silence settled between them, comfortable and deep.

Suddenly, Alexander's voice cut through the quiet. "What did you wish for when you blew out your candles?"

Evelyn arched an eyebrow, a secretive smile playing on her lips. "That's a secret. If I tell you, it won't come true."

"Is that so?" Alexander responded, his tone suddenly serious. "My wish was about you. I'm sure you're curious now."

Her interest was immediately piqued. "What was it?"

He looked at her mischievously. "Are you sure you want to know? Lean closer, and I'll tell you."

Drawn in by his promise, Evelyn bent down toward him.

Instead of a secret, she heard his soft chuckle near her ear as he repeated her own words back to her.

"It's a secret. After all, you said if you tell, it won't come true."

Surprise flashed across Evelyn's face. She saw the playful smirk on Alexander's lips and realized he had been teasing her all along.
###Chapter 291

Evelyn felt a wave of irritation.
"Alexander! Must you always be so juvenile?" she exclaimed.

He remained completely unfazed by her scolding.

In his mind, being a little childish with his own wife was perfectly harmless.

"If you're that desperate to know, I might be persuaded to share my secrets."
He leaned in slightly, a playful glint in his eyes.
"How about a kiss first? Then, I'll reveal anything you want to know."

Evelyn snorted dismissively and turned her face away.
Her lips formed a defiant pout.
"I've lost all interest in your silly games," she declared, convinced she had anticipated his usual tactics.

"Is that so?" Alexander's voice was light and teasing, a sound that tested her resolve.

Despite her burning curiosity, Evelyn held her posture rigid, arms crossed tightly.
"Absolutely. I don't want to know. You can keep your secrets."

Even with his back to her, Alexander could vividly imagine the fiery spirit in her eyes.
It made him adore her even more.

He was initially surprised.

He hadn't realized his wish had found its way into her subconscious.
It was a clear sign his words had struck a chord deep within her.

Alexander carefully lowered himself onto the edge of the bed beside her.
He leaned close until his breath was a whisper against her ear.
"Evelyn," he murmured with heartfelt sincerity. "My only wish is to spend eternity by your side."

At that exact moment, the antique clock in the living room began to chime midnight.
Its deep, resonant tones echoed through the silent house like a solemn vow.

Holding his breath, Alexander brushed a soft, affectionate kiss against Evelyn's forehead.
It was a silent seal on his whispered confession.

The next morning, the sun rose on another workday for Evelyn.
Her desk was already buried under a mountain of pending assignments and unread emails.

Her phone's shrill ringtone broke the morning's routine.
An unknown number flashed across the screen.

A flicker of anxiety shot through her.
Her mind instantly went back to the disturbing calls from Theodore Vance.

She took a steadying breath, gathering her courage before answering.
"Hello?" Her voice was calm but guarded.

"Evelyn, how have you been holding up?"

Evelyn's voice rippled with genuine surprise.
"Lucas? This is a shock!"

Lucas Bennett's warm laughter resonated through the phone.
"I know, it's been far too long," he said. "I was hoping you'd be free this weekend. We're organizing a small college reunion."

Evelyn paused, a flood of memories from her university days washing over her.
She had been stretched incredibly thin back then.
Juggling multiple jobs to make ends meet had left her with little time to socialize or bond with classmates.

Furthermore, a few of her peers had always been inexplicably hostile towards her.
Why would she willingly spend her time at a reunion that promised no joy?

"I'm afraid I might have prior commitments this weekend," Evelyn responded, her tone polite but distinctly distant.

Lucas sighed, the sound tinged with disappointment.
"Evelyn, you've been keeping yourself very busy lately. I just got off the phone with Amelia Clarke—she accepted the invitation immediately. She's really looking forward to seeing you."

At the mention of Amelia Clarke, Evelyn's eyebrows shot up in astonishment.
"Amelia? Wait, when did she come back? And she's actually going to the reunion?"
###Chapter 292

Evelyn thought about her university years.
Among all her classmates, only one had truly been a close friend.
Her roommate, Amelia.
Amelia was quiet and somewhat introverted.
But she possessed a natural, raw talent for design.
After graduation, she married and moved overseas.

From the bits of news Evelyn had heard, Amelia was doing well.
Evelyn had never felt particularly connected to her other former classmates.

She let out an exaggerated sigh.

It was a vibrant red.

"Isn't this a little... much?"
It was only a college reunion. Did she need to make such a statement?

Evelyn released a small sigh but took the dress and went to change.

Her eyes met Alexander's gaze.
Her voice was quieter than before. "Well? How does it look?"

In the depth of his gaze, she saw something unmistakable.
Admiration.
"It looks incredible," Alexander said, his voice steady and sure. "This is the one. You look absolutely stunning."
###Chapter 293

A warm blush spread across Evelyn's cheeks at Alexander's compliment. She quickly averted her gaze, suddenly feeling shy.

"You barely looked at me before declaring I look good. It sounds like an empty platitude." Evelyn pouted slightly, letting out a soft huff before turning to face the mirror.

Her breath caught in her throat.

The vibrant red dress made her skin appear radiant. Her features, usually understated, looked strikingly defined. For a fleeting moment, she saw a version of herself that commanded attention.

Was that really her?

Evelyn stared at her reflection, barely recognizing the woman staring back.

She had never dared to wear something so bold, so inherently eye-catching.

It took Alexander's casual suggestion to reveal how perfectly red suited her.

The dress's craftsmanship was impeccable. Every stitch, every seam, spoke of a master's hand.

Evelyn's professional instincts kicked in automatically. Her trained eye meticulously assessed the exquisite details.

"Alexander," she murmured, almost to herself. "You might understand my style better than I do."

She turned to him, her voice a mix of curiosity and excitement.

Her eyes sparkled as she smiled. "Alexander, have you ever considered a career in design? Or perhaps as a styling consultant? With your impeccable taste, you'd be exceptional."

If Sebastian were present, he would likely be laughing uncontrollably.

Alexander wasn't just 'good' at fashion. He was the visionary founder of Aethelgard Industries, a global trendsetter. His influence extended far beyond clothing, earning him respect across numerous sectors.

As the founder of Aethelgard, his superior sense of style was a given.

Yet, with Evelyn, Alexander remained humble. "I don't care about anyone else's opinion. I only want to be your personal designer."
