The assistant shut his eyes tightly, bracing himself. He sighed in resignation. "Mrs. Blackwood, go ahead, scold me all you want! But the doctor's orders are for your own good. Even if you beat me, I cannot stand by and let you ignore medical advice!"

Eleanor kept her cane raised, but did not bring it down.

Her gaze was sharp enough to cut stone. Her breathing became slightly uneven, and her face flushed with frustration.

"You foolish boy! Did the doctor also tell you to infuriate me?" she huffed, scolding him relentlessly.

Yet, her cane never once made contact.

The standoff at the entrance continued, the tension palpable. But Eleanor's vigor seemed entirely undiminished.

Evelyn, who had just walked out with Alexander, watched the scene unfold.

She stood frozen in place, completely stunned.

This was nothing like what she had been led to expect.

Hadn't Alexander said his grandmother was in poor health? That she had been convalescing at a sanatorium abroad for years?

But the woman bantering forcefully with the assistant seemed far more robust than Evelyn herself.

Eleanor appeared far too lively and strong to be someone who needed constant rest.

Beside her, Alexander let out a soft, resigned sigh. He seemed accustomed to his grandmother's dramatic entrances.

After a moment, he finally spoke. "Grandmother, please stop harassing my assistant. He is only acting on my instructions. Give the man a break."

Eleanor immediately scowled at him. "Alexander, what nonsense are you talking? How am I harassing anyone? I am simply eager to meet my new granddaughter-in-law, and your assistant is the one creating obstacles!"

She paused, then lifted her head. Her sharp eyes instantly landed on Evelyn, who was standing beside Alexander.
###Chapter 406

Evelyn's heart hammered against her ribs.

Her eyes met Eleanor's across the room.

An unexpected warmth bloomed in her chest.

Her fingers instinctively tightened around Alexander's arm.

Before she could form a greeting, Eleanor's face broke into a radiant smile.

Joy illuminated the older woman's features.

Eleanor moved with surprising speed, brushing past Alexander's assistant.

She closed the distance between them, her steps light and eager.

Evelyn braced herself, assuming the affection was meant for Alexander.

She gently released Alexander's arm.

"She seems very eager to see you," Evelyn murmured softly.

Alexander, misreading the situation, opened his arms for an embrace.

It had been too long since he'd seen his grandmother.

But Eleanor sidestepped him completely.

She gave his shoulder a light, dismissive push.

"Move over, dear," she said, her tone brisk.

Her attention shifted from Alexander to Evelyn in an instant.

Eleanor's eyes softened as she took Evelyn's hands.

Her touch was surprisingly warm and reassuring.

"Oh, my dear girl!" Eleanor exclaimed, her voice full of wonder. "You are even more beautiful than I imagined. Such luminous eyes! Such flawless skin!"

Evelyn stood frozen, completely caught off guard.

The initial shock began to melt away under Eleanor's genuine warmth.

Eleanor studied her with pure, unadulterated admiration.

"What an utterly charming young woman," she declared. "So rare to find such grace these days."

She let out a deep, heartfelt sigh.

"I was abroad when I heard the most outrageous rumor that Alexander had married. I dismissed it as nonsense! But here you are, every bit the splendid bride they described."

Evelyn finally found her voice. "Eleanor...?"

"Look at you," Eleanor cooed, her smile widening. "So gracious. So polite."

The intensity of the affection was overwhelming.

Evelyn had not been prepared for such a fervent welcome.

Eleanor's approval was evident in her every expression.

She clicked her tongue affectionately.

"What a treasure you are. Putting up with my temperamental grandson cannot be easy."

Evelyn was momentarily speechless.

She quickly rallied to Alexander's defense.

"He's very kind to me, Eleanor. Always considerate."

Eleanor snorted, her skepticism clear.

"Hard to believe. You've been married for a while now, and you're still so slender. Is he not feeding you properly?"

This portrayal made Alexander bristle with irritation.

"Grandmother," he interjected, his voice tight. "Is that really what you think of me?"

Eleanor turned a stern gaze upon him.

"Given your history of scaring away every suitable girl, how can you expect me to think otherwise?"
###Chapter 407

Alexander glanced at Evelyn, his expression sincere. "Grandmother, Evelyn is truly one of a kind."

Eleanor's eyes sparkled with amusement. "Is that so? Tell me, what makes her so special?"

He didn't pause, his voice dropping to a tender tone. "Evelyn means the world to me. My love for her makes her utterly irreplaceable."

A warm blush spread across Evelyn's cheeks. Her heart skipped a beat at his declaration.

"Stop that," she whispered, playfully swatting his arm. She felt flustered by his directness in front of his grandmother.

Alexander merely raised an eyebrow, completely unbothered. "Was anything I said incorrect? Or do your feelings for me differ?"

Evelyn's embarrassment deepened. She lowered her gaze, unable to look at him.

Eleanor watched their exchange with a contented smile. Her grandson's devotion was clear.

A broad grin stretched across the older woman's face as she observed the young couple.

Her eyes then drifted meaningfully towards Evelyn's abdomen, her smile widening. "You two are so perfectly matched. I expect it won't be long before I'm holding my first great-grandchild, hmm?"

Evelyn was startled by the forward comment. "Pardon?"

Eleanor's smile was warm and encouraging. "Given how close you and Alexander are, a baby seems like the natural next step, doesn't it?"

Evelyn met Eleanor's expectant gaze and was suddenly reminded of her previous false pregnancy claim. A shadow crossed her features. Her hand instinctively moved to her stomach.

Having grown up without a family, Evelyn yearned deeply for the warmth and connection she'd never known. This fueled her desire to build a family with Alexander.

She had stopped using birth control. They were both healthy, in their prime. By all accounts, she should have been expecting by now.

Yet, there was still no pregnancy to announce.

"Eleanor, I..." Evelyn's voice faltered. A wave of sadness washed over her, leaving her speechless.

Noticing her shift in mood, Alexander quickly interjected. "Grandmother, we have all the time in the world. There's no need to rush into anything."

Hearing this, Eleanor's expression shifted to concern, her brows furrowing. She surmised that the lack of pregnancy news might be because Alexander was reluctant to start a family.

He was probably just enjoying his time alone with his wife!

"You silly boy, are you being cautious or are you just slow?" Eleanor chided him disapprovingly, giving his arm a gentle tap. "It's better to start a family sooner rather than later. Waiting can complicate things for Evelyn physically."

Alexander remained calm, standing protectively beside Evelyn. "Grandmother, it's not like that. Evelyn and I are both focused on our careers right now. We're simply not in any hurry."

Eleanor's expression showed mild frustration, her eyes widening. "Who said anything about Evelyn quitting her job? You can balance a career and a family. If she becomes pregnant, we'll arrange for a personal nutritionist at her office. It won't disrupt her work. And after the baby arrives, don't worry—I will be here to handle everything. You two won't need to lift a finger!"
###Chapter 408

Isabella was completely taken aback.
She hadn't anticipated Eleanor would have everything planned out so meticulously.

Eleanor's words pulled Isabella from her sinking despair.

"Eleanor, the truth is..." Isabella started, her voice trailing off uncertainly.

Alexander cut in sharply. "Grandmother, you require care yourself. How can we possibly leave a child in your charge?"

Eleanor's face flushed with immediate irritation. "Are you implying I'm infirm?"

Her expression darkened dramatically.
She raised her hand, threatening to swat him. "I'll show you exactly how much strength I still possess!"

Isabella froze, her heart hammering.
Seeing Eleanor's genuine anger, she acted on instinct.
She stepped forward quickly, gently guiding Eleanor's arm down.

"Eleanor, the evening chill is setting in. Let's go inside," she suggested softly.
She refused to be the reason for further conflict between them.

With Isabella's calming presence, Eleanor reluctantly settled.

She still shot Alexander a withering glare. "You troublesome boy, always provoking me," she grumbled.

Isabella couldn't suppress a small smile.
She soothingly reassured Eleanor and guided her toward the mansion's entrance.

"Isabella, you have such a good heart. Alexander is fortunate. He's truly blessed to have you," Eleanor said with genuine warmth.

They entered the dining room just as dinner was being served.

At the table, Eleanor's doting on Isabella continued unabated.

"Isabella, dear, you must try this. It's exquisite. You're far too slender. You need to build your strength," Eleanor insisted, her tone full of concern.

Isabella replied obediently, "Of course, Eleanor."

The more Eleanor looked at her, the more pleased she appeared. "Don't you worry, my dear. As long as I'm here, you'll always have whatever your heart desires."

Isabella felt a wave of helpless affection.
It was clear Eleanor believed Alexander was neglecting her care.

Without pause, Eleanor continued heaping food onto Isabella's plate.

Isabella ate as quickly as she could, but she was no match for Eleanor's relentless serving.

Soon, Isabella felt completely overwhelmed.
Not wanting to offend, she cast a desperate, silent plea toward Alexander.

Her eyes screamed, "Alexander, help me!"

He understood immediately.
He cleared his throat. "Grandmother, please, let Isabella serve herself. You've just returned from your journey. You should eat something yourself."

Isabella gave a subtle, grateful nod.

In mere minutes, the food on Isabella's plate had formed a small mountain.

Eleanor glanced at Alexander, then a knowing grin spread across her face. Without a word, she began serving him instead.

"Oh, are you feeling neglected? Here, let me serve you as well," she teased playfully.

Alexander paused, momentarily stunned by her accusation.

To avoid further drama, he decided to play along.

Eleanor added some greens to his plate. "You haven't lost any weight—in fact, you might have gained a little. Perhaps you should ease up on the rich foods."

Isabella couldn't hold back a light laugh. "Eleanor, really? He's gained weight?"

She had been with him constantly and hadn't noticed any change.

"Absolutely! The moment I saw him, I thought he looked heavier," Eleanor stated with absolute conviction.

Alexander sighed in defeat. "Grandmother, look closely. I've added muscle, not fat. While you were abroad, I've maintained a strict gym regimen."
###Chapter 409

Evelyn blinked.

The image of Alexander's bare torso flashed in her mind.

His body was sculpted and powerful, every muscle sharply defined.

A sudden heat warmed her cheeks.

She quickly averted her gaze, a wave of embarrassment washing over her.

Thankfully, Alexander and Eleanor were still engrossed in their playful exchange.

They hadn't noticed her reaction.

Otherwise, Evelyn would have died of shame!

Eleanor paused, a thoughtful look on her face before she sighed. "It's difficult to believe three years have passed since I was last here. Everything feels so different now. The city has changed so much during my absence."

Evelyn chuckled, trying to dispel her awkwardness. "Has it? I suppose I haven't really paid attention."

Eleanor took her time before answering. "Oh, absolutely. Not just Crestwood itself. It seems my grandson has undergone quite a transformation as well."

"Hmm?" Alexander raised an eyebrow at the mention of himself. "Grandmother, are you suggesting I've aged or grown taller?"

Eleanor shot him a teasing glance. "I'm saying you've become far more vibrant. After you turned eighteen, you grew so cold and distant. But now? You're much more agreeable company."

Evelyn couldn't suppress a smile. "Eleanor is right. When I first met Alexander, I found him rather intimidating."

At her words, Alexander turned to look at her, his gaze softening with memory.

Their first meeting had been at their wedding.

Eleanor sighed dramatically, becoming more animated as she recalled Alexander's youth. "Some children are simply adorable," she said fondly. "He used to be so attached to me, always on the verge of tears if I left him alone for a moment. He never wanted to be separated from me, not even for a second!"

Evelyn's eyes widened in disbelief. She couldn't possibly imagine that scene.

Alexander was caught off guard by his grandmother's revelation, his expression immediately darkening with clear embarrassment.

"Grandmother, that's enough," he muttered, clearly uncomfortable.

But Eleanor was enjoying herself too much, laughing as she squeezed Evelyn's hand. "I'm not joking! I have a whole collection of his childhood photographs. I'll show them to you later, and you can see for yourself!"

"Really?" Evelyn's interest was instantly piqued, her excitement growing.

She had never seen what Alexander looked like as a child!

After dinner, Eleanor eagerly guided Evelyn to the living room sofa.

"Please, fetch my suitcase and bring me the albums from the bottom," Eleanor instructed a nearby servant with a casual smile.

"Right away, Mrs. Blackwood," the servant replied promptly.

A moment later, the servant returned with two photo albums and handed them to Eleanor.

As Alexander approached, his eyes landed on the familiar album in his grandmother's hands.

He swiftly stepped forward and took it from her.

The instant he saw the cover, Alexander recognized it as his childhood photo album.

So his grandmother hadn't been bluffing after all!

She had actually been carrying this album with her.

"Alexander, what are you doing? Return that album to me," Eleanor said, her voice tinged with displeasure.

Alexander shook his head stubbornly. "No, Grandmother. I'm keeping this album now. You won't be looking at it anymore."

He was serious. Why should anyone be browsing through his childhood photos while he was present?

Wasn't he far more handsome now?

Eleanor's irritation flared instantly. "I brought this album back with me. What gives you the right to confiscate it?"
###Chapter 410

Alexander stubbornly clutched the photo album, refusing to let go.
"This album is mine," he insisted, his voice firm.

Eleanor shot him a look of pure exasperation.
"You're unbelievable. I've seen these pictures a thousand times. I just want to show Evelyn. She's your wife! What's the harm in letting her see your childhood photos?"

A flicker of doubt crossed Alexander's face.

Evelyn, her curiosity thoroughly aroused, looked up at him.
Her expression was soft, pleading.
"Alexander, I really want to see them. Please? Aren't you going to share them with me?"

Perhaps these photos could help her understand the man beside her a little better.

Seeing the hopeful look in Evelyn's eyes, Alexander finally relented.
He let out a long, resigned sigh.

Under her gentle but persistent gaze, he slowly handed the album back to Eleanor.

A brilliant smile instantly lit up Evelyn's face.
"Thank you, darling!"

From the side, Eleanor couldn't resist a teasing comment.
"Well, well. Who would have thought? The great Alexander Blackwood, completely wrapped around his wife's little finger."

A delicate blush colored Evelyn's cheeks at the playful jab.

"Eleanor, let's just look at the album," Evelyn suggested quickly, eager to change the subject.

With growing curiosity, Evelyn opened the album and studied the first photograph.

The boy in the picture wore a snug red jacket.
His young face was full of innocent wonder as he observed the world around him.

Eleanor pointed to the photo.
"This was taken when Alexander was just one. Look how adorable he was. You could already tell he was going to be a handsome man."

Evelyn studied the child's features, trying to connect them to the man sitting beside her.
It was difficult to imagine they were the same person.

Alexander sat quietly next to Evelyn, watching her absorb the image.
A faint, involuntary smile touched his lips.

As they turned the page, however, his smile vanished.

The next photo showed a young Alexander sitting in a mud puddle.
His rosy cheeks were streaked with tears.

Evelyn burst out laughing.
"How on earth did you end up in a mud puddle?"

Eleanor chuckled softly.
"He was a terribly mischievous little boy. Things like this happened constantly. I got used to it after a while."

They continued flipping through the album.

In the following picture, a young Alexander looked utterly terrified, his eyes wide with fear.

Eleanor's voice softened as she explained to Evelyn.
"This was taken after a caterpillar scared him to tears. I remember it clearly. He was determined to play in the backyard. I warned him that spring brought out all sorts of insects, but he wouldn't listen. Of course, he ended up crying when a caterpillar startled him."

Evelyn pointed at the child.
"He must have been about five or six here."

Eleanor nodded, a smile playing on her lips.
"Yes, around that age."

Evelyn couldn't help but smile too.
"Afraid of bugs. What a kid."

Evelyn's gaze remained fixed on the album.
With each page she turned, she grew fonder of it.

Alexander had been such an adorable child, naturally charming.

Lost in her thoughts, she voiced them aloud.
"He was so cute as a little boy. How did he grow up to be so... reserved?"
###Chapter 411

I will protect Alexander.

Evelyn released a heavy sigh, her eyes fixed on Alexander. "The guilt still haunts me. I couldn't protect him back then. He was framed, trapped in that car accident, and I was utterly helpless to intervene. Later, to shield himself—and to keep me from being dragged deeper into the mess—he spent years pretending to be disabled. All of it happened because I was powerless."

Alexander, already uncomfortable with the conversation veering towards his past humiliations, tensed the moment Evelyn mentioned herself. A deep frown etched itself onto his forehead. "Grandma, you mustn't think like that. None of it was your fault. Please, don't say such things."

Eleanor's expression turned grave, her fingers unconsciously tracing the border of the photo album.

But Evelyn's face remained clouded with remorse. "I've always felt I failed him," she confessed. "That's precisely why I've been so desperate for him to settle down, to build his own family. He has endured far too much. As his grandmother, my greatest fear is him facing this world alone."

A soft, grateful smile touched her lips as she turned to Eleanor, warmly taking her hand. "Thankfully, fate seems to have finally heard my prayers and brought you to him. I truly couldn't have asked for a more perfect granddaughter-in-law."

The profound sincerity in Evelyn's voice was unmistakable. Eleanor met her gaze with steady resolve. "Evelyn, I give you my word—I will protect Alexander."

Together, Evelyn and Eleanor finished turning the pages of the substantial photo album.

Hours slipped by as they continued to talk, their bond strengthening with each shared story and exchanged confidence.

Alexander, observing from the sidelines, realized with a touch of wry amusement that he had been completely forgotten.

As night settled in, Eleanor suddenly remembered something and addressed the servant. "Please prepare the guest room upstairs. It has a wonderful view and a private balcony. I'm sure Evelyn will appreciate it."

Before the servant could respond, Evelyn gently waved a hand. "That won't be necessary, dear. I won't be staying here tonight. I'll be going to Crestwood Manor."

Her announcement took both Eleanor and Alexander by surprise.

Eleanor's brows furrowed in concern. "Evelyn, why? You're home now. Why would you choose a nursing home over staying with family?"

Alexander, equally opposed to the idea, immediately added his voice. "Grandma, no nursing home, no matter how luxurious, can compare to being here with your family. Let the servants prepare the room. It will be ready in moments."

Evelyn's heart warmed at their earnest pleas, but her decision was firm. "I appreciate your kindness, truly. But everything has already been arranged. Before I returned, I made plans to reside there. My accommodations are all set."

She paused briefly, then offered them both a playful, knowing smile. "Besides, I wouldn't want to intrude on your newlywed life. I think I'd much prefer going to the manor, meeting new companions, and simply enjoying my peace there."

Despite their persistent attempts to change her mind, Evelyn remained unwavering in her choice to stay at Crestwood Manor.

Left with no alternative, Alexander and Eleanor eventually acquiesced, though both silently resolved to find a way to bring her home properly soon.

Not long after, Evelyn departed.

That night, even after retiring to bed, Eleanor couldn't shake off a persistent feeling of reluctance.

Turning to Alexander, she murmured softly, "Alexander, I genuinely adore Evelyn. We must visit her frequently at the manor. I want to ensure she's truly content and well-cared for."

Alexander watched her, a flicker of amusement in his eyes. He couldn't help but smile at the earnestness with which she was already planning their visits.

Wordlessly, he pulled her closer into his embrace, his arm wrapping securely around her waist.

His warm breath brushed against her ear, sending an unexpected shiver coursing through her.

Suspicion dawned on her features as she instinctively tried to lean back. A faint blush colored her cheeks. "What are you doing?"
###Chapter 412

Alexander pulled Evelyn closer, a low chuckle rumbling in his chest. "Making Grandmother happy seems like a simple enough task, doesn't it?" He nuzzled her hair. "Now that you've reminded me, I suppose I should apply myself."

Evelyn blinked up at him, confusion clouding her features. "What are you talking about?"

His voice dropped to a husky, playful whisper. "You heard Grandmother perfectly well. She's waiting for a great-grandchild."

Understanding dawned, painting Evelyn's cheeks a bright crimson. She squirmed, attempting to push away from his solid embrace.

"You're impossible!" she protested, her voice strained. "Let me go!"

But Alexander only tightened his hold, his arms becoming unyielding bands around her. He dipped his head, his lips finding the sensitive skin of her shoulder in a series of soft, warm kisses.

A shiver ran through Evelyn. Her resolve melted under his teasing mouth. She bit her lip, a soft, involuntary sound escaping her.

"Alexander, this isn't fair..."

His hand slid slowly, deliberately, up her thigh. Her thoughts began to fog. She lifted a hand to stop him, then froze. The memory of his injured arm, still not fully healed, flashed in her mind.

This infuriating, reckless man.

Alexander's eyes darkened with unmistakable hunger, his gaze pinning her, waiting for her surrender.

His deep voice, smooth as velvet and barely audible, brushed against her ear. "You've been the one calling the shots. Now that I don't have to pretend, it's my turn."

He tilted her chin up, capturing her lips in a deep, claiming kiss.

The world narrowed to the meeting of their mouths, to breathless sighs and the burning heat in their ears. Evelyn melted into him, her arms encircling his neck as she gave herself over completely.

In one fluid motion, Alexander swept her up into his arms, cradling her against his chest.

A startled gasp was instantly swallowed by his relentless kiss. His expression was relaxed, almost lazily confident.

Evelyn pulled back slightly, her brow furrowed as her eyes darted worriedly to his arm. "Your arm..." Alexander smiled, flexing the injured limb just enough to demonstrate. "It's fine. You're light as a feather. One arm is more than enough."

A brief silence hung between them before his eyes smoldered again. He murmured, "Forget about my arm, Evelyn. You should be far more concerned about yourself." The way her name rolled off his tongue, deep and heated, made her heart stutter. Her pulse hammered wildly, a frantic rhythm against her ribs.

She met his gaze, her head spinning from the raw, unchecked desire she saw there.

A sudden realization struck her. With all the recent chaos and injuries, they hadn't been intimate for what felt like an age. The thought sent another wave of heat flooding through her. "Alexander, please... be reasonable," she muttered weakly.

He raised a brow, a teasing smile playing on his lips. "That depends entirely on you, my love."

Their bodies tangled together, sinking into the soft expanse of the bed. They were lost in each other, consumed by a passion that burned until the night itself faded away.

Evelyn awoke slowly. Morning light pressed insistently against her eyelids. Every muscle in her body ached with a deep, satisfying soreness, as if she'd been put through a wringer.

The memories of the previous night flooded back in a vivid, dizzying rush.

Her cheeks flushed a deep, mortified red. She wished the bed would simply open up and swallow her whole.

Alexander was utterly, completely insane.

Lying beside her, Alexander seemed to sense her stirring. He pulled her closer, his arms wrapping her in a secure, possessive embrace. "Awake?" he murmured, his voice thick with sleep.

Elena, once a pampered heiress, suddenly lost everything when the real daughter
###Chapter 413

Alexander's and Evelyn's bodies remained entwined beneath the silk sheets, their skin fused together in the aftermath of passion.

"Alexander, you're impossible..." Evelyn's voice was a raw, husky whisper.

He moved instantly, fetching the glass of water from the nightstand. He held it to her lips, helping her drink with a gentle hand.

Once the cool liquid soothed her throat, Evelyn found her strength. "Your arm is still injured. How could you be so careless? I told you to stop countless times last night, and you completely disregarded me. I mean it, Alexander. If this happens again, I will sleep in another room."

Alexander propped himself up on an elbow, a roguish smirk playing on his lips. "So, if I'm good now, does that mean once I'm healed, I have your permission to be as reckless as I want?"

Evelyn stared at him, momentarily stunned into silence. She shook her head in disbelief. "Alexander, can you ever be serious? Is a single rational thought too much to ask? Stop putting foolish ideas in your head!"

She pushed the water glass back toward his mouth.

He took a few quick gulps, pretending to choke dramatically. He coughed, his eyes watering slightly. "Darling, you almost drowned me."

Evelyn let out a soft, dismissive snort.

His credibility with her was completely shattered.

"You deserved that! I did it on purpose, after everything you put me through last night!" she retorted, the memories flooding back.

They lingered in the warmth for a few more minutes. Evelyn glanced at her phone and sighed quietly, beginning to extricate herself from the bed to dress.

"I have to go to work. You need to stay home and rest that arm. Don't aggravate the injury," she instructed firmly.

Despite the intense night, her professional responsibilities called. She had already taken several days off to care for Alexander. She couldn't afford to fall further behind.

As she moved, Alexander's gaze dropped to the graceful curve of her waist.

His eyes darkened with renewed desire. He reached out, his fingers lightly tracing the soft skin of her hip. "What are you doing?" Evelyn flinched away instinctively, a shiver tracing its way down her spine at his touch.

She threw an exasperated look over her shoulder as she finished buttoning her blouse. "If you make me late, you'll be sorry."

Alexander raised a challenging eyebrow, clearly entertained. "Oh? And what's your plan for punishing me?"

His tone was playful, yet laced with an intimacy that caused an involuntary smile to touch Evelyn's lips.

A warm, fluttering sensation bloomed in her chest.

How did he manage to make the simplest words sound so provocatively enticing?

Evelyn responded with a light, teasing huff, choosing to ignore him for the moment.

But Alexander didn't miss the faint blush coloring her cheeks. The sight made his heart stutter.

He instantly forgot her warning. He sat up further, the sheets pooling around his waist, revealing the sculpted strength of his torso.

His larger frame leaned into her slender back.

Alexander wrapped his arms around her from behind, pulling her firmly against him. The embrace was both tender and possessive, leaving her no room to escape.

"Hmm? What's wrong? Why so quiet all of a sudden?"

His voice, husky and warm, whispered directly into her ear, sending another delicious shiver through her.
###Chapter 414

Evelyn shuddered slightly.

An undeniable irritation colored her voice as she elbowed Alexander. "Alright, let me go. I absolutely must leave now."

She paused for a moment, then decided to add a serious threat. "If you don't stop right now, I will seriously consider moving to the guest room."

This man was becoming more and more inseparable.

Just as expected, the moment she finished speaking, Alexander froze completely.

Even without looking at his face, Evelyn could vividly sense the wave of grievance emanating from him.

"Honey, that's too cruel. We've only been married a few months, and you're already talking about separate rooms? That's really harsh."

Evelyn couldn't suppress the smile that broke on her lips.

She turned around and gently cupped Alexander's face in her hands.

Seeing his utterly pitiful expression, she chuckled softly.

She leaned down and placed a soft, brief kiss on his lips.

"Enough of that. Alexander, you've truly mastered the art of playing the victim. I honestly don't know where you learned it."

Alexander watched her rosy lips, his thoughts drifting. "Nonsense. I'm being completely genuine. There's no performance here."

It finally clicked for Alexander that Evelyn truly had to go.

He knew how dedicated she was to her work. He would never obstruct her career.

"I'll drive you," Alexander offered immediately.

Evelyn shook her head quickly. "No, your injury isn't completely healed. You need to stay home and rest properly." Alexander pressed his lips together, acquiescing. "Fine. Then I'll at least walk you to the door."

A radiant smile instantly lit up Evelyn's face. "Deal."

Alexander dressed swiftly.

With a thoughtful demeanor, he escorted Evelyn to the entrance of Serenity Oaks.

He lingered just long enough to watch her get into the car.

Just as he turned to go back inside, a loud, familiar voice called out from behind him. "Alexander! Hey, Alexander!"

Hearing that boisterous, unmistakable voice, Alexander didn't need to turn to know it was Sebastian.

Among all his friends, Sebastian was the only one incapable of staying quiet for more than five minutes.

Alexander continued walking inside, his tone calm as he responded, "What brings you here so early in the morning?"

Sebastian caught up to him, a wide grin on his face. "I heard Eleanor is back! I figured I'd come by early to see her!"

Alexander raised an eyebrow. "Why are you so concerned about my grandmother? She's my family, not yours."

A flicker of surprise crossed Sebastian's face before he shrugged, his tone turning playful. "You're in a feisty mood today, huh? Teasing me like this."

He walked further into the house as he spoke. "I heard Eleanor returned last night. I didn't want to disturb you guys so late, so I thought I'd wait until this morning to visit."

Sebastian nudged Alexander, his excitement palpable. "Come on, let's go see her. I've been dying to. It's been years! She was always so good to me. I miss her terribly!"

Alexander could see Sebastian's genuine affection for Eleanor. Before she left for overseas treatment, Sebastian visited often, and they had formed a close bond.

"She had dinner with us last night and then left," Alexander explained. "She said she preferred to stay at Crestwood Manor. We didn't stop her."
###Chapter 415

Sebastian's face fell instantly. "What? You mean I made this trip for nothing? Alexander, I have to ask—how could you possibly let Eleanor live by herself in a retirement home at her age? That's simply unacceptable!"

His voice grew louder, tinged with accusation. "Serenity Oaks is enormous. You could easily have given her a suite. If you're going to be like this, I'll just take her to live with me!"

Alexander barely suppressed the urge to roll his eyes at his friend. "Grandmother chose to move into Crestwood Manor herself." He paused, remembering her words clearly. "She said she didn't want to intrude on my new life with Evelyn. Honestly, she's far from pitiful. I inspected the facility thoroughly. It's quite luxurious, and I intend to make a significant investment to ensure her comfort is unparalleled."

Sebastian was silent for a long moment, his eyes wide with shock.

"Eleanor actually said she didn't want to disrupt your marriage to Evelyn?" Sebastian let out a soft, incredulous laugh. "So, she met Evelyn last night. How did it go? She must adore your wife, right? I mean, Evelyn is the first woman you've allowed close to you in years."

Ignoring the teasing lilt in Sebastian's voice, Alexander answered with complete sincerity. "Grandmother is genuinely fond of Evelyn. And Evelyn feels the same way about her. Since their meeting, the connection between Evelyn and me has deepened considerably. Grandmother is essentially the only family I acknowledge now."

The rest of the Blackwood family held no significance for him.

Sebastian gave a slow, understanding nod. A real smile spread across his face, happy for his friend.

Then, a new thought seemed to strike Sebastian. He shot Alexander a mischievous look. "Speaking of which, Eleanor used to constantly nag you about settling down. Now that you're married, don't you think she'll start pressuring you for a great-grandchild?"

Alexander didn't even blink. "She already has."

"See? I knew it." Sebastian chuckled. "Eleanor would absolutely be on that."

He tapped his chin thoughtfully. "But it is a bit odd, don't you think? You and Evelyn have been married for months now. Both of you are in peak physical condition. Yet, there's no pregnancy news. What's the hold-up?"

Sebastian scrutinized Alexander's face, a wicked glimmer in his eyes. "Hold on a second... Could it be that you're... impotent?"

Alexander's expression turned thunderous in a heartbeat. His voice dropped to a dangerous, icy tone. "Sebastian. Are you actively trying to get yourself hurt? Did you come here today with the sole purpose of provoking me?"

No man appreciated having his virility questioned, and Alexander Blackwood was certainly no exception. Sebastian had crossed a line.

But when he saw the cold, murderous intent flashing in Alexander's eyes—a look that promised dismemberment—Sebastian quickly backtracked, realizing his blunder.

"I was only joking!" Sebastian said, forcing a wide grin. "Seriously, Benjamin gave you both a clean bill of health. With your robust constitutions, conceiving should be effortless. You'll probably have a whole football team of kids."

Sebastian wasn't entirely wrong. Doctor Benjamin Carter had confirmed both Alexander and Evelyn were perfectly healthy. There were no physiological barriers.

The couple was deeply in love, inseparable.

By all logic, there should have been an announcement by now...

As Sebastian spoke, Alexander couldn't help but recall the faint, wistful shadow that had passed over Evelyn's face the previous night. His own mood soured, growing heavy.

"We will let nature take its course," Alexander stated quietly, his tone firm. "I refuse to let Evelyn feel any additional pressure about this."
###Chapter 416

Sebastian didn't miss the shadow that crossed Alexander's face. The lack of a pregnancy was clearly a sore subject.

Wanting to change the topic, Sebastian spoke up. "Now that Evelyn is settled at the manor, you should take me to see her. I brought some health supplements. Maybe she'll have a little surprise for me, too."

Alexander scoffed. "She didn't give Evelyn or me anything. Why would she have something for you?"

But the moment they arrived, Alexander's expression darkened. Eleanor, looking utterly delighted, handed Sebastian a beautifully wrapped gift.

Sebastian grinned, a triumphant laugh escaping him as he watched Alexander's mood sour.

"Eleanor, you're the best! I knew you wouldn't forget my present!" Sebastian beamed, his chest swelling with pride.

Alexander had always been possessive of those he cherished.

As a child, he would throw tantrums whenever Eleanor hugged Sebastian.

Now, even as a grown man and a husband, that old, familiar jealousy pricked him when Eleanor seemed to favor his friend.

If Evelyn could see him now, she would surely be shocked.

Noticing the storm in her grandson's eyes, Eleanor chuckled softly. She reached into her suitcase and produced two large boxes, handing them to Alexander.

"You and your moods," she teased, shaking her head affectionately. "Did you honestly think I'd forget you and Evelyn? The luggage only arrived last night."

Alexander accepted the gifts, the tension in his shoulders easing slightly.

"Grandmother, you misunderstand," he said, his voice sincere. "I wasn't pouting. I was merely concerned that if Evelyn didn't receive a gift, she might feel slighted."

Eleanor gave him a knowing look, amusement sparkling in her wise eyes. "Oh, please. Using Evelyn as a shield? After all these years, you haven't changed a bit. That stubborn pride of yours is still intact."

Alexander didn't argue.

Laughter and light conversation filled the room, the easy warmth between them making the space feel brighter.

"That reminds me," Eleanor said, her expression turning serious as she focused on Alexander. "You've told Evelyn the truth about your legs, but have you explained the situation with Aethelgard Industries yet?"

The ease vanished from Alexander's face, replaced by a flicker of rare unease. "Not yet," he admitted. "I plan to tell her at the Sterling family banquet in a few days."

Sebastian let out a low whistle, shaking his head. "Eleanor, I think Alexander is truly gone for Evelyn. Have you ever seen him like this before?"

Confusion and hesitation, emotions that were foreign to Alexander, were now plain on his face.

For the first time, he looked genuinely uncertain.

The thought of seeing disappointment in Evelyn's eyes unsettled him deeply. But what terrified him more was the possibility that, once she learned the full truth, she might never trust him again.

Eleanor, perceptive as always, sensed his inner conflict. Her voice was calm yet firm. "Alexander, I've only met Evelyn once, but her sincerity is evident. A woman with such a genuine heart will not tolerate deception from the man she loves. If you wait too long to tell her the truth, you may live to regret it."

Alexander absorbed her words, his expression growing grave. "I understand, Grandmother."

Her advice solidified his resolve.

No matter how Evelyn reacted, he could not keep this secret any longer.

And most importantly, once the world knew that Evelyn's husband was the founder of Aethelgard Industries, no one would dare lay a finger on her again.
###Chapter 417

Alexander left Crestwood Manor with a heavy heart.

His mind was a tangled mess, consumed by a single, terrifying question.

How would Evelyn react when he finally told her the truth?

He was so lost in thought he didn't notice the sunset.

He didn't even register that Sebastian had followed him all the way back to Serenity Oaks.

It was only when the car stopped and the door opened that Sebastian's voice broke through.

"Perfect timing! I can smell dinner from here."

Sebastian grinned, already heading for the grand entrance.

"It's been too long since I had a meal prepared by your chef. I've missed it."

Alexander finally looked at him, his lips pressed into a thin line.

A flicker of irritation crossed his face.

"Why are you still here?"

Sebastian waved a dismissive hand, strolling inside as if he lived there.

"I'm here for dinner, obviously. And to see Evelyn. It's been a while."

Alexander's expression darkened further.

His voice dropped to a low, dangerous murmur.

"Evelyn and I were supposed to have a private evening. This is an intrusion."

Sebastian placed a hand over his heart, feigning deep offense.

"Such gratitude. After everything I've done for Aethelgard Industries."

He shook his head in mock disappointment.

"Maybe I should have negotiated for a larger share of the profits."

Before Alexander could retort, a warm, familiar voice interrupted them.

"What are you guys talking about?"

Sebastian froze.

His eyes widened in sheer panic as he watched Evelyn approach.

His heart hammered against his ribs.

When did she get back?

More importantly, what had he just said?

Did he just mention Aethelgard Industries?

Did she hear?

Oh, no.

Alexander hadn't told her yet.

He had just ruined everything.

Sebastian stood rigid, his blood running cold.

He was a dead man.

He had carelessly revealed the secret Alexander had guarded so fiercely.

Alexander was definitely going to kill him.

Beside him, Alexander was equally frozen.

His sharp, calculated mind had completely short-circuited.

She knew.

Evelyn knew he was the founder of Aethelgard Industries.

What would she do?

Would she ever forgive him for the deception?

He remembered her words clearly.

She hated lies.

She despised being kept in the dark.

His breath hitched, his usual composure shattered.

He opened his mouth, ready to confess everything.

To beg for her understanding.

But the words died in his throat.

For a terrifying moment, he had thought it was over.

She hadn't heard a thing.

The earbuds had blocked their entire conversation.

Why was he even stressing over someone else's marriage?

Alexander let out a slow, careful breath.

The crushing weight on his chest eased slightly.

He stepped forward, his fingers gently intertwining with Evelyn's.

His voice was soft, carefully controlled.

"You're home early. Is everything alright?"

Evelyn blinked up at him, her tone light and easy.

"Yes, I finished my work on time, so I came straight back."

Her gaze shifted to Sebastian, who still looked like he'd seen a ghost.

"Did you two go somewhere together today?"

Seeing she wasn't pressing about their discussion, Sebastian seized the opportunity.

"I heard Eleanor was back in town."

He shrugged, trying to sound nonchalant.

"So I dragged Alexander to Crestwood Manor to see her."
###Chapter 418

Sebastian's expression brightened as he nudged Alexander. "Oh, that's right! Eleanor got something for Evelyn. What are you waiting for? Go get it."

Evelyn's eyes widened with surprise and delight. "Really? Eleanor bought me a gift?"

Sebastian smirked, pleased with how effortlessly he had redirected the conversation to safer ground.

Evelyn, utterly captivated by the present, spent a considerable amount of time admiring it before finally, and somewhat reluctantly, placing it aside.

Alexander watched her intently, realizing something he had previously overlooked. Having grown up in an orphanage, she likely wasn't accustomed to receiving gifts.

Besides E, the kind-hearted director of the orphanage, who else had ever done such a thing for her?

The realization hit him with profound force.

In that quiet moment, Alexander made a silent vow—to treasure her, shield her, and ensure she never felt that particular kind of loneliness again.

Oblivious to the emotions swirling within him, Evelyn carefully stored the gift away and turned to Sebastian with a radiant smile. "Will you be staying for dinner? The kitchen should be nearly finished."

Sebastian let out a soft laugh. "Thank you, Evelyn."

"No need for thanks. I truly appreciate how much you watch out for Alexander." Evelyn shook her head, her smile genuine and warm.

Hearing this, Sebastian shot Alexander a meaningful look before nudging him with his elbow. "Hear that? Your wife is very concerned about you."

His words weren't merely teasing—they carried a clear, unspoken reminder. Alexander needed to heed Eleanor's advice and finally tell Evelyn the truth.

After all, someone as genuinely sincere as Evelyn was a rarity.

Alexander met Sebastian's gaze, fully understanding the implication. He gave a small nod. "Yes... my wife takes very good care of me."

Evelyn stood up, carefully holding the gift. "Alright, you two, go and get ready. Dinner will be served soon. I'll go put this away."

"Let me take this upstairs first."

Evelyn's voice was flat as she turned toward the staircase.

Alexander watched her ascend, a heavy sigh tearing from his chest. The pressure in his heart intensified with every step she took.

Sebastian observed his friend, a brow quirking upward. "Why are you still so tense? The Sterling banquet isn't for days. You need to relax."

Alexander's gaze dropped to the floor, his voice dropping to a near whisper. "That's the problem. The closer it gets, the more it terrifies me. Her anger I can manage. I can face that. But what if she decides to leave? Not just for now. What if she leaves me for good?"

Sebastian blinked, stunned by the raw dread in Alexander's tone.

He understood Evelyn had every reason to be furious. But vanishing completely? That seemed extreme.

Alexander was spiraling.

Yet, seeing the depth of his friend's fear, Sebastian couldn't bring himself to dismiss it. The thought of what Alexander might become if Evelyn truly walked away sent a cold chill down Sebastian's spine.

He didn't even want to picture it.
###Chapter 419

Sebastian left after dinner.

The night was vast and dark outside, dotted with countless twinkling stars.

Alexander emerged from the bathroom, a towel in his hand. He expected to find Evelyn in the bedroom, but she was nowhere to be seen.

Where had she gone?

"Evelyn..." he called out, running the towel through his damp hair as his eyes scanned the room.

Just as he was about to leave and search for her, her cheerful voice rang out from the walk-in closet behind him. "I'm in here!"

Alexander turned and saw her standing inside the closet. A measuring tape was draped over her hands.

"Darling, come here, quick!" she said, her eyes shining with a brilliant light.

Her radiant smile was infectious. Alexander felt a smile tug at his own lips.

He walked towards her, completely captivated by the excitement sparkling in her gaze.

"You seem exceptionally happy tonight," he noted, pausing for a moment. "Is it because of Grandmother's thoughtful gift?"

Evelyn blinked, then nodded enthusiastically. "That's certainly one reason! Speaking of which, I still don't have Eleanor's contact information. Perhaps you could take me to Crestwood Manor sometime soon. I'd like to thank her in person."

"Of course," Alexander agreed without a second thought.

Such a simple request was never a question for him.

He studied her for a moment, a curious glint in his eyes. "You said Grandmother's gift was one reason. What's the other?"

Evelyn had been waiting for him to ask. Her eyes sparkled even brighter. A wide, eager grin spread across her face as she held up the measuring tape. "Alexander, I want to design a suit for you!"

The flicker of genuine surprise that crossed his features was exactly the reaction she had hoped for.

"Well? Bet you didn't see that coming!" She let out a light chuckle, playfully swinging the measuring tape.

"Design a suit for me?" Alexander arched an eyebrow, then nodded slowly. "You're correct. I didn't expect that. What inspired this sudden decision?"

He was well aware of Evelyn's exceptional talent in design. But at this moment, the quality of the suit was secondary. The most important thing was that she was the one who wanted to create it for him.

A profound warmth spread through his chest at the thought.

"I've been thinking about it for a while," Evelyn explained, her voice filled with conviction. "Especially with the Sterling family banquet approaching. When you walk into that room, everyone will see a man of remarkable presence. I want to be part of that."

As she spoke, a proud smile graced her lips, and her voice brimmed with confidence. "Combining my design skills with your impeccable physique and handsome features, plus a suit crafted specifically for you by me... you will undoubtedly be the most dazzling star at the entire banquet."

Alexander watched her, utterly charmed by her palpable excitement. A low, fond chuckle escaped him, completely disarmed by her sweet yet fiercely determined enthusiasm.
###Chapter 420

"You believe I should deploy my charm to seize attention?" Alexander inquired, one eyebrow arched in amusement.

Evelyn shook her head, her expression utterly sincere. "Absolutely not! I merely suggest a bit of refinement could be advantageous. Ultimately, however, your competence is paramount. A polished presentation makes influential guests more receptive to interaction. That translates to greater opportunities."

She paused, tapping a finger thoughtfully against her chin. "Despite the Blackwoods maintaining a subdued presence in recent years, the Sterling guest list will undoubtedly be formidable."

Something fractured within Alexander then. The dam containing his turbulent emotions finally burst. Acting on pure impulse, he drew Evelyn into his arms, holding her firmly against his chest.

Her warmth seeped into him, soft and intimately familiar. He buried his face in the curve of her neck, inhaling her scent as if it were his only anchor.

A profound stillness descended upon him as he released a slow breath. The habitual icy sharpness in his gaze melted away, replaced by a rare, unguarded softness.

Evelyn tensed momentarily in surprise, her mind reeling before her body instinctively relaxed into his. "What's this for?"

The measuring tape hung loosely from her fingers. She hesitated, uncertain whether to return the embrace or remain passive.

After a moment, she gave his back a few gentle pats. "Alexander? Are you alright? You seem... unlike yourself."

If so, this entire process would be redundant.

Alexander raised a brow, lying with effortless ease. "No. No one has ever designed clothing for me personally. I typically wear standard sizes. Custom measurements have never been necessary."

Evelyn narrowed her eyes skeptically. "Is that so? Then how is it that every suit you own fits as though it were tailored specifically for you?"

Seeing his deception unravel, Alexander brazenly persisted. "I couldn't say. A fortunate coincidence, perhaps. Regardless, I do not possess my measurements. You will have to take them yourself."

This man was simply inventing excuses to engage with her.

Alexander was already gone.

A faint sense of puzzlement flickered through her sleep-addled mind.

Alexander had been recuperating at home these past few days.
Where could he have gone at this early hour?

Puzzled, Evelyn rose from the bed.
She quickly washed up and left the master bedroom.

His tone was low and intense. "You're certain Theodore has purchased a flight leaving the country in one week?"

The assistant's reply was immediate. "Yes, Mr. Blackwood. The information just came in. Theodore has been making preparations for his departure. It appears he has no intention of returning."

Evelyn stopped just outside the door, stunned.

Why was Theodore leaving so abruptly? His shoulder injury had seemed severe.
Could it have healed that fast?

It seemed impossible. Theodore was only human.

She lifted her hand to knock, but the door suddenly swung open.
The assistant stood there, his expression sharp. "Who's there?"

Evelyn flinched, caught off guard.

Recognition dawned on the assistant's face, followed by immediate contrition. "My apologies, Mrs. Blackwood. I didn't realize it was you."

Seeing her, Alexander's stern features softened into a smile. "You're awake early."

"It's alright," Evelyn said to the assistant before stepping into the study.
She walked over to Alexander's desk. "I have to go into the office today. I set an early alarm."

Alexander's eyebrow quirked upward. He picked up a sheet of paper and handed it to her.

"What is this?" Evelyn asked, confused.

VIP

Mr. CEO You Have Lost

MY HEART FOREVER!

Mr. CEO, You Have Lost My Hea...
###Chapter 422

Alexander didn't speak.

His eyes gleamed with mischief as he motioned for Evelyn to open the paper herself.

A sequence of numbers was scrawled across the page.

She understood their meaning instantly.

How utterly shameless!

He knew his own measurements perfectly well.

That led to them spending the entire night entangled in the closet.

His fingers found the sore spot on her back. "Is it here?"

His assistant, sensing the intimate moment, quietly excused himself.

Their affection was palpable. It made a single man envious.

The recent days had been intense. Her sleep had suffered.

Seeing the sincerity in his eyes, Evelyn felt somewhat appeased.

"Earlier," she began, changing the subject. "I overheard you and your assistant. I wasn't eavesdropping. The door was ajar."

Alexander smiled, his hand still on her waist. "It's fine. You can hear any report I receive."

Evelyn mirrored his smile and leaned in, brushing a quick kiss on his lips. "Likewise. No secrets between us."

For a fleeting moment, his expression tightened.

It returned to normal just as quickly.

It's fine, he told himself. Soon, he could tell her everything.

Evelyn missed the brief shift. "Is Theodore really leaving the country?"

Alexander feigned irritation. "Are you that concerned about Theodore Vance?"

"I'm merely asking," she sighed, recognizing his jealousy. "I want no secrets from you. Because of Margaret's scheme, I hurt him. I still feel guilty. Even though we found the real poisoner, I can't absolve myself completely."

Seeing her guilt, Alexander's heart softened.

He cupped her cheek, his thumb stroking her soft skin. A silent, tender comfort eased her troubled heart.

"Don't blame yourself. None of this is your fault," he said softly.

Evelyn met his gaze, earnest. "Please understand my relationship with Theodore. There is nothing between us. Our connection exists only because I saved him overseas."

Alexander brushed a strand of hair from her face. His playful tone vanished. "I believe you," he said steadily. "But I find it hard to accept that Theodore would simply leave. He seems... obsessed with you."
###Chapter 423

Isabella Rossi frowned slightly. "I heard your assistant mention that Theodore Vance has already purchased a new ticket and is packing his bags. It appears his decision to leave is final, correct?"

She paused, a realization dawning. "That day at the hospital, he believed I was the one who poisoned him. Now that the truth has emerged, perhaps he finally understands. So, you needn't worry. He will no longer be a disruption in our lives."

Alexander Blackwood's possessiveness remained unchanged.

Unless she offered him proper reassurance, his mind would not be settled.

Yet, something felt off. His brow creased with doubt.

"If he knows you never intended him harm, why would he simply relinquish his claim on you?" he questioned, the words escaping before he could stop them.

Evelyn's reply was measured and calm. "We had a conversation previously. Perhaps he finally listened. He is the heir to the Vance family. His entire life, women have surrounded him. Why would he squander his time clinging to someone like me?"

Alexander was reluctant to admit it, but after numerous encounters with Theodore, he could perceive the man's genuine feelings for Evelyn.

And to Alexander, no other woman in the world could ever compare to her.

The mere thought of Theodore returning, attempting to compete for her affection, ate away at him.

A new strategy began to form in his mind. Revealing his identity as the founder of Aethelgard Industries had never seemed pertinent before. But now? The more he considered it, the more logical it seemed.

If Theodore were aware of the truth, perhaps he would retreat permanently.

Their discussion did not prolong further. Evelyn needed to arrive at work punctually.

As was his custom, Alexander accompanied her to the entrance of Serenity Oaks, watching until she entered the vehicle and drove away. Only then did he turn back inside, a sense of calm finally descending upon him.

At her office, a subordinate presented Evelyn with a portfolio of design drafts.

"These require your final approval," the designer stated. "If everything meets the standard, we can proceed with production." Evelyn gave a slight nod. "Very well. I will review them and provide feedback."

Once alone, she set the portfolio aside, intending to examine the contents later.

But as she straightened the stack, a single sheet slipped into her line of sight.

Pulling it free, she instantly identified it—the wedding gown design Theodore Vance had commissioned from her.

She had almost forgotten its existence entirely.

Evelyn hesitated, the memory of Theodore Vance's substantial payment flashing in her mind. Professional duty warred with personal disinterest. After a brief internal debate, she picked up her phone and dialed his number. The line connected after a few rings, but was met with absolute silence. Theodore offered no greeting.

Pressing her lips together, Evelyn decided to speak first. "Theodore, regarding the wedding gown design you commissioned. Do you wish for me to continue?"

Only silence answered her.

"Theodore? Are you there?"

Just as she was convinced he wouldn't respond, his voice finally came through. It was low, gravelly, thick with unmistakable fatigue. "What's the current status?"

Evelyn shuffled the draft designs in her hand, her eyes scanning the details quickly. "Only the final details remain. If you still want it, I can complete the final version within two days."

For a moment, all she heard was his ragged, uneven breathing. Then, a sharp, controlled exhale. "Destroy it."

Evelyn went completely still. "What?"
###Chapter 424

Theodore's voice was frigid, each word sharp and deliberate. "Dispose of it. It holds no value for me now."

Evelyn froze, her breath catching in her throat. She struggled to maintain her composure. "But... you've already transferred the payment."

A harsh, mocking laugh escaped Theodore. "We have a signed contract. The money is irrelevant. Consider it yours."

Evelyn paused, collecting her thoughts. Her voice was remarkably steady when she spoke. "You approached me directly for this commission. I hadn't yet involved Aethelgard Industries' resources. According to our agreement, the company's full involvement was contingent on the design moving to production. Since you no longer require it, I will arrange for the full refund."

It felt wrong to keep the money for a design he no longer wanted.

Theodore fell into a heavy silence.

Evelyn massaged her temples, a dull ache forming. The situation was uncomfortable.

A pang of guilt twisted inside her, yet a stronger instinct screamed for distance, for a clean break.

Suddenly, Theodore let out a low, bitter chuckle. It was a sound devoid of any warmth. "So this is it, Evelyn? You won't even accept my money now?"

Evelyn was speechless, his words striking a nerve. Before she could formulate a response, his voice returned, colder than ice. "If you don't want it, throw it away. Let's not contact each other again."

The line went dead. He had hung up without another word.

The monotonous dial tone buzzed in her ear. A profound sadness washed over her, cold and heavy.

His resentment was palpable, a living thing between them.

But Evelyn refused to dwell on it. Theodore was leaving. That chapter was closing.

She simply wished him peace, and a swift recovery.

As for the payment, she decided she would ask Alexander to facilitate its return once she was back home.

In the following days, Evelyn buried herself in her work. Designing Alexander's suit became her focus, a welcome distraction between her professional commitments.

When Alexander's eyes fell upon the garment, a clear spark of admiration ignited within them.

The deep blue fabric of the evening suit seemed to capture the light, shimmering with a subtle intensity. Its cut was impeccable, far surpassing any ordinary design.

A brilliant bow tie complemented the suit perfectly, accentuating its inherent luxury.

But the most striking detail was the silk handkerchief pinned to the chest, its contrasting color radiating an undeniable sophistication.

"Incredible!" Samuel exclaimed, his eyes wide. "I've never seen a men's suit with such presence! Mrs. Blackwood, your talent is exceptional!"

Evelyn smiled, a faint blush coloring her cheeks. "You're too kind, Samuel. Men's formalwear is inherently simpler than women's gowns. I merely focused on refining the details."

Alexander's gaze remained fixed on the suit, then shifted to Evelyn. His eyes held a deep, appreciative warmth.

"It is stunning," he affirmed, his voice sincere. "Samuel isn't exaggerating. This is, without question, the finest evening suit I have ever seen."

Evelyn's heart swelled with a deep, satisfying pride at his genuine praise.

Her smile widened, the fatigue of the past days melting away under his approval.

"Thank you," she said, her grin infectious. "Please, try it on. If any adjustments are needed, I can make them immediately."

Alexander nodded, taking the suit to change.

Alexander emerged.

All conversation ceased. Every eye in the room was drawn to him, and a collective, awed silence fell.

He was breathtaking.
###Chapter 425

Alexander Blackwood stood before her, his frame lean and commanding. The impeccably tailored evening suit accentuated his powerful shoulders and back. It lent him an even more formidable and authoritative presence.

The soft living room light washed over him, making the fine fabric of his suit shimmer subtly.

For the first time, Evelyn saw him like this. He possessed an innate elegance, a noble bearing that seemed part of his very essence.

He hadn't made any special effort tonight, yet his sharply defined features held a natural sophistication that was impossible to ignore.

Evelyn's pulse hammered in her ears. The sight of him captivated her utterly. It was only when Alexander stopped directly in front of her, a faint smile playing on his lips, that she realized she had been staring. Even then, she found she couldn't look away.

Seeing the pure admiration in her eyes, Alexander's smile widened.

Evelyn snapped back to reality, though her eyes remained glued to him.

###Chapter 426

###Chapter 427

###Chapter 428

Evelyn nodded, offering a faint smile. "Alright. I'll go to the hospital. Thank you."

Evelyn glanced at her schedule. Tonight was the Sterling family banquet.

If she still felt unwell this afternoon, she would ask Alexander to take her to the hospital after the event.
###Chapter 429

Work ended.

It was elegant, subtle, and perfect for the occasion.

He stepped out of the luxury car.
His presence alone immediately drew the gaze of everyone nearby.

Guests began whispering to each other, curious about his identity.
At that moment, another tall, imposing figure strode confidently toward him.

The appearance of this newcomer intensified the crowd's curiosity.

The man stopped before Alexander and offered a slight, knowing smile.
"It's been a while, Mr. Blackwood."
###Chapter 430

Alexander Blackwood lifted his gaze. A faint, composed smile touched his lips. "Mr. Sterling."

Jonathan Sterling was impeccably dressed in a tailored suit. He carried himself with the innate grace of a gentleman.

Every gesture was polished. Yet beneath the refined exterior, a sharp, icy glint resided in his eyes. It was a silent warning to anyone who dared to cross a line.

He stepped forward, meeting Alexander's look directly. "It's an honor to have you join our gathering, Mr. Blackwood."

He then gestured gracefully toward the hotel's grand entrance. "Shall we proceed?"

Alexander gave a slight, acknowledging nod. "You're too kind, Mr. Sterling."

Side by side, the two men walked into the venue. Their powerful auras commanded immediate attention.

The other guests, however, were too stunned to simply admire them.

Those who had witnessed the brief exchange stood frozen in disbelief.

"Did I see that correctly? Jonathan Sterling showing such deference?"

"Everyone knows the Sterling family's standing in Crestwood. For Jonathan to personally greet a guest... this man must be immensely important!"

"His presence is overwhelming. But I've never seen him before. Which powerful family does he belong to?"

"I was wondering the same thing..."

"Wait a moment... Doesn't he bear a resemblance to Alexander from the Blackwood family?"

An awkward silence instantly fell over the group.

"Why is everyone so quiet? He really does look like Alexander, doesn't he?"

"Are you dreaming? Alexander Blackwood? Impossible! That good-for-nothing would never be allowed at a Sterling event!"

"Precisely! And remember the most obvious difference—Alexander is confined to a wheelchair. The man who just walked in with Jonathan has full use of his legs. Don't be absurd."

That crucial point settled the matter. The idea was immediately dismissed.

Meanwhile, Jonathan led Alexander into the opulent banquet hall. Dazzling chandeliers illuminated the space. Their light cast a golden glow over the lavish decor.

The event hadn't officially started. Yet the room was already filled with elegantly dressed guests.

As one of Crestwood's most influential families, the Sterlings commanded respect. They had maintained a low profile recently, but their power was undeniable. The sheer number of attendees proved it.

Just then, a vibrant young woman in an exquisite gown approached them.

"Jonathan!" Amelia Sterling, Jonathan's younger sister, beamed as she reached them. She grabbed his arm with playful affection. "Well? Don't I look absolutely breathtaking tonight?"

With a cheeky grin, she lifted the hem of her gown. She spun in a graceful, energetic twirl.

Jonathan's gaze softened as he looked at his sister. His smile held a touch of indulgence. "You look beautiful, Amelia."

Satisfied with the compliment, Amelia turned her attention to the man beside her brother.

Her eyes locked onto him. She found she couldn't look away.

Having lived her whole life in Crestwood, she had met many distinguished men. None had captivated her like this.

It wasn't just his strikingly handsome features. His entire being radiated a natural authority. An air of dominance that demanded admiration.

Amelia studied him openly. A mischievous smile played on her lips. She leaned closer to her brother, whispering with curiosity. "Jonathan, who is this remarkable gentleman?"

Anyone personally escorted by Jonathan had to be extraordinary.

With a knowing smile, Jonathan made the introduction. "Mr. Blackwood, allow me to present my younger sister, Amelia."
###Chapter 431

Alexander’s gaze shifted to the young woman beside Jonathan. “Amelia, this is the man I told you about—the founder of Aethelgard Industries, Mr. Blackwood.”

Amelia’s eyes sparkled with immediate fascination, her expression one of pure admiration. “So you’re the founder of Aethelgard Industries!”

She had heard endless tales about the secretive, immensely powerful billionaire behind the global tech giant—one of the most influential figures in all of Crestwood.

In her imagination, she had always pictured Alexander as an older man, perhaps with silvering hair and a portly build.

But reality had completely overturned her expectations. He wasn’t just young; he was devastatingly handsome.

Her interest intensified. She moved a step closer, her voice softening into a playful tone. “Mr. Blackwood, it’s such an honor to finally meet you. I’m Amelia Sterling, but please, call me Amelia, just like Jonathan does.”

She offered a charming smile and extended her hand, hoping for a warm handshake.

Alexander’s face remained an unreadable mask as he accepted the gesture with minimal contact. “Miss Sterling,” he acknowledged, his tone icy and detached.

He pointedly ignored her invitation to use her first name.

Moreover, the moment their hands touched, he withdrew almost instantly, as if the contact were a mere formality.

Amelia’s smile wavered for a split second. Had he really dismissed her so coldly?

It was just a handshake—was such obvious distance really necessary?

But she refused to be discouraged. She quickly masked her disappointment.

If anything, his aloofness only made him more intriguing. A man like this—distant, enigmatic, and seemingly immune to charm—was a rare challenge. That only made him more worth pursuing.

A mischievous glint flickered in her eyes. She tilted her head and smiled sweetly. “Mr. Blackwood, you’re far more handsome and refined than Jonathan.”

Alexander’s brow furrowed slightly before his expression smoothed over. His voice was steady. “Miss Sterling, this is our first meeting. Such a compliment is unnecessary.”

Then, glancing at Jonathan, he added lightly, “Besides, if you continue, Mr. Sterling might become jealous.”

With Aethelgard Industries and the Sterling Group on the verge of a major collaboration, Alexander had no intention of openly offending Amelia. Regardless of her forwardness, she was still the cherished daughter of the Sterling family.

Jonathan had been watching the exchange closely. Though inwardly annoyed by his sister’s behavior, he played along with Alexander’s remark, feigning offense. “Amelia, I’m standing right here. How could you say that in front of me?”

Amelia pouted playfully, though a trace of dissatisfaction lingered in her eyes.

The more Alexander kept his distance, the more determined she became to break through his defenses.

Men always fell for her charms—why should he be any different?

Her confidence only solidified her resolve.

An idea suddenly struck her, and she acted on impulse.

Reaching out, she placed a delicate hand on Alexander’s shoulder. “Since it’s our first meeting, shouldn’t we seal it with a friendly kiss on the cheek?”

Before Alexander or Jonathan could react, she rose onto her toes and leaned in toward his face.

But just as her lips were about to brush his cheek, a firm force yanked her backward.

She was pushed back to her original spot. Alexander’s grip on her arm was unyielding.

Shock flashed in her eyes, quickly turning to anger. She looked up to protest, but froze.

Alexander’s gaze was cold and piercing, sharp enough to cut through steel.

Her heart lurched. A strange fear crawled up her spine, making her instinctively step back. The dull ache from his grip was secondary to the intensity of his stare.

There was something terrifying in his eyes.

Jonathan, startled by the sudden shift, instinctively moved between them, shielding his sister.
###Chapter 432

It was the raw intensity in Alexander's expression that was so alarming.

For a split second, it seemed he might hurl Amelia aside without a shred of mercy.

Jonathan's eyes widened in shock. He forced himself to stay calm, offering a strained smile. "Mr. Blackwood, I must apologize for my sister's behavior."

Amelia bit her lip, her voice trembling with indignation. "Even if you are the founder of Aethelgard Industries, this is too much! A real gentleman doesn't treat a woman like this! I was just trying to have a normal conversation. Let me go!"

She tried to wrench her arm free, but his grip was like iron.

Alexander's face was an unreadable mask, but his voice held a quiet, dangerous edge. "I am a married man, Miss Sterling. I suggest you adjust your conduct accordingly."

Humiliation washed over Amelia in a hot wave. Her cheeks flushed crimson, and tears of anger pricked her eyes. She turned to her brother, clutching his arm like a lifeline. "Jonathan..."

Seeing his sister's distress, Jonathan's heart softened. He sighed, his tone becoming more placating. "Mr. Blackwood, my sincere apologies. Amelia had no idea you were married. I will speak to her about this."

As he spoke, he shot Alexander a pleading look.

A flicker of recognition passed through Alexander's sharp gaze. He remembered the rumors about Amelia's fragile heart condition.

The potential alliance between Aethelgard Industries and the Sterling Group was far too valuable to jeopardize over this incident.

Reluctantly, he released his grip and gave a curt nod. "Very well."

The moment she was free, Amelia let out a frustrated huff, stamped her foot, and fled down the corridor without a backward glance.

"Amelia!" Jonathan called after her, but she was already gone.

Watching her disappear, he sighed heavily before turning back to Alexander.

The icy chill in Alexander's demeanor had thawed slightly.

"Amelia has always been spirited. My parents and I have never been strict with her. She's been unwell since she was a child," Jonathan explained, a shadow of sadness in his eyes.

Her health had been a constant worry for the Sterling family.

Alexander's gaze settled on him, his tone now devoid of its earlier sharpness. "She will recover."

Jonathan nodded slightly, then hesitated. He looked at Alexander with newfound curiosity. "I'd heard the rumors of your marriage, Mr. Blackwood, but I never knew they were true. Now I find myself wondering... what kind of woman managed to capture your heart?"

At the mention of Evelyn, Alexander's entire presence shifted. The hardness in his eyes melted away, replaced by a soft warmth that touched his usually stern features.

"She is extraordinary. And I love her deeply," he stated simply.

Jonathan blinked, taken aback by such directness.

Raising an eyebrow, Jonathan smirked. "You must care for her a great deal to reveal your identity so openly tonight."

Then, a thought occurred to him, and he frowned slightly. "But why didn't she come with you?"

Alexander's expression remained serene. "She was delayed in traffic. She will be here shortly."

Meanwhile, outside the venue, Evelyn finally arrived after hurrying across the city. Her driver stepped out and gestured toward the imposing hotel entrance. "Mrs. Blackwood, shall I call Mr. Blackwood to come out and meet you?"

Evelyn shook her head with a smile. "It's alright. I'll go in and find him myself." She turned back, adding, "Thank you for the ride."

"Of course, Mrs. Blackwood. Do call if you need anything," the driver replied with a nod.

Gathering the hem of her elegant gown, Evelyn made her way toward the entrance.

She was just about to present her invitation to the staff when a figure suddenly burst out from inside the venue and collided with her forcefully.
###Chapter 433

A sudden impact sent Evelyn stumbling backward.

Her heels skidded on the polished floor.

She barely managed to keep her balance.

The person who had crashed into her was not so fortunate.

A woman, rushing out of the venue in a frantic hurry, had been wearing impossibly high heels.

She lost her footing completely.

With a sharp cry, she landed hard on the marble floor in a tangled heap of expensive fabric.

The delicate silk of her gown ripped at the seam.

For a long moment, Amelia Sterling just sat there, stunned.

Then, realizing no one had immediately rushed to her aid, her shock turned to fury.

"What is wrong with you people?" she shrieked, her voice echoing in the entrance hall. "Are you all blind? Help me up!"

"Miss Sterling!" The frozen onlookers sprang into action.

Several guests and hotel staff hurried forward, carefully helping her to her feet.

They tactfully avoided looking at the bright red flush of humiliation on her cheeks.

"Miss Sterling, are you hurt?" a young hotel employee asked, his voice full of concern.

Amelia took a deep, shuddering breath.

Then, without any warning, her hand flew out.

The sharp crack of her palm connecting with the employee's face echoed through the space.

The young man staggered back, his hand flying to his stinging cheek. He said nothing.

Evelyn watched, utterly speechless.

She had not expected such a violent outburst, especially toward someone who was only trying to help.

The sheer entitlement took her breath away.

But Amelia was not finished. Her rage needed a new target.

She turned, and her venomous gaze landed squarely on Evelyn.

A flicker of pure, unadulterated jealousy crossed Amelia's features.

Evelyn's dress for the evening was elegant but understated, not overtly luxurious.

Yet her natural beauty and poise made her impossible to ignore.

"Who do you think you are?" Amelia snarled, her eyes narrowing to slits. "You clumsy fool! Look what you've done to my dress!"

Evelyn opened her mouth to explain, but Amelia cut her off.

She waved a dismissive hand toward the security guards hovering nearby.

"Get this woman out of here!" she commanded. "She is banned from all Sterling family events from now on!"

It never occurred to Amelia that Evelyn might be someone she couldn't afford to offend.

Having grown up in Crestwood's elite circles, Amelia knew all the important socialites and female entrepreneurs.

Evelyn's face was unfamiliar.

Therefore, in Amelia's mind, she was a nobody.

As a daughter of the prestigious Sterling family, Amelia believed she had every right to blacklist an insignificant guest.

At her command, two security guards stepped forward.

"Ma'am, you heard Miss Sterling. Please come with us," one of them said, his tone firm.

It was then that Evelyn fully processed the situation. This was Amelia Sterling, the daughter of the hosting family.

She reminded herself quickly—she was here as Alexander Blackwood's guest. Causing a scene would be unwise.

She remained calm, her voice level and polite but firm.

"Miss Sterling, you ran into me. I was standing still. And for the record, I am an invited guest. I have my invitation right here. You cannot simply throw me out."

Amelia let out a contemptuous sneer.

"We invited many people tonight. An invitation does not give you the right to be reckless."

She stopped midsentence.

Her eyes had locked onto the invitation in Evelyn's hand.

It was black, edged in shimmering gold.

Amelia's breath caught in her throat.

For this event, the Sterling family had issued invitations based strictly on the guest's status.

Ordinary guests received blue-and-white invitations.

Those of higher standing received red ones.

But the exclusive black-and-gold invitation…

That was reserved only for the most distinguished, the highest-ranking guests of all.
###Chapter 434

Evelyn held an invitation of black and gold.
Its appearance immediately caught Amelia Sterling's attention.

"What is this?" Amelia's voice was tight.
A flicker of fear showed in her eyes.
She wondered if Evelyn was someone important after all.

Then, a thought occurred to her.
She smirked, her expression turning scornful.
"Now I understand. You must have stolen this. Or it's definitely a forgery!"

Evelyn was bewildered by the accusations.
Was she serious? Since when were invitations categorized as real or fake?

"This invitation is legitimate," Evelyn stated firmly.
Her brows furrowed in frustration.
Alexander had given it to her. It had to be valid.

Amelia remained unconvinced.
She stepped forward quickly and snatched the invitation from Evelyn's hand.

"You—" Evelyn was shocked by the rudeness.
