It was hard to believe a member of the prestigious Sterling family could be so ill-mannered.

Despite her anger, Evelyn maintained her composure.
She watched silently as Amelia inspected the card.

Amelia's scornful look soon changed to one of surprise.
"You are actually—" she stammered.
Her eyes were fixed on the words "Aethelgard Industries".
She realized Evelyn was indeed the wife of the founder of the renowned global company.

It made perfect sense that Evelyn possessed the exclusive black-and-gold invitation.

Evelyn, perplexed, raised an eyebrow. "Is there a problem, Miss Sterling?"

She noticed Amelia's finger pressing down on the company's name.
But she didn't think much of it.

Memories of Alexander ignoring her earlier flooded Amelia's mind.
A wave of jealousy towards Evelyn washed over her.
How was this woman any better? Amelia wondered silently.
The founder of Aethelgard Industries wouldn't even glance at her because of this woman.

Just as Evelyn braced for more unreasonable behavior, Amelia's expression softened.
She smiled. "I apologize. It was all a misunderstanding. Please, don't be upset."

This sudden shift put Evelyn even more on edge.

What was Miss Sterling really planning?

Evelyn kept her expression neutral, not revealing her suspicion. "May I go in now?" she asked calmly.

"Of course," Amelia Sterling replied, returning the invitation. She gestured to a nearby waiter. "To apologize for the inconvenience, let this gentleman escort you inside." Amelia gave the waiter a slight, almost imperceptible nod.

The waiter understood the signal immediately. A knowing look passed between them.

He stepped forward eagerly. "Right this way, Ma'am."

Evelyn, remembering past traps, was instantly wary. "That won't be necessary. I can find my own way," she declined quickly.

She took her invitation back and walked past them, heading straight into the banquet hall.

As Evelyn disappeared inside, her plan thwarted, Amelia's face darkened with frustration.

She stood rigidly, her eyes fixed on the spot where Evelyn had vanished. A bitter hatred churned within her.

Just you wait, she thought. Your time will come.
###Chapter 435

Evelyn stepped into The Grand Crestwood.

The opulent lobby immediately assaulted her senses.

It was almost too much.

She walked further inside, her heels clicking softly on the marble.

She was sure the invitation said the first floor.

But the Sterling family's banquet hall was nowhere to be found.

A flicker of doubt crossed her mind.

Perhaps she should have taken Amelia up on her offer to send an escort.

Too late for regrets now.

Just as a wave of frustration began to rise, her phone vibrated.

Alexander's name flashed on the screen.

She answered quickly.

"Evelyn, have you arrived?" His voice was a low, familiar rumble.

She tried to sound confident. "Just parked the car. I'll be right there."

Alexander chuckled.

The sound warmed her ear. "Are you sure you're not lost?"

"I am not lost!" she insisted, a little too sharply. "I'm almost there. Don't rush me."

"Alright," he conceded, amusement still in his tone. "But if you're not here in five minutes, I'll have the hotel make an announcement."

An announcement? Panic seized her.

She pictured it clearly. "Would Mrs. Blackwood please report to the Sterling banquet on the first floor? Your husband is waiting."

The mere thought made her cringe.

"Alexander Blackwood, don't you dare! If you embarrass me, I swear I'll never forgive you!" Her voice was a mix of genuine threat and flustered irritation.

On the other end, Alexander smiled. He could perfectly imagine her indignant expression. "Fine, I'll be good. But hurry up."

He ended the call, a faint smile still playing on his lips.

Jonathan Sterling, standing nearby, observed him with interest.

"Remarkable," Jonathan remarked with a teasing grin. "I almost didn't recognize you for a moment."

Alexander met his gaze, unbothered. "No mystery. You'll meet my wife soon enough."

Jonathan raised an eyebrow, intrigued. "I look forward to it."

Meanwhile, Evelyn was still circling the first floor.

The sprawling layout was confusing.

Finally, she spotted a housekeeping attendant near a service corridor.

Relief washed over her.

She gathered the folds of her gown and hurried over.

"Excuse me, could you direct me to the Sterling family banquet?"

The woman straightened up and turned.

But upon seeing Evelyn's face, her own expression froze in sheer shock.

Her eyes widened in disbelief.

"It's you again!" she exclaimed, staring at Evelyn as if she'd seen a ghost.

Evelyn was taken aback. "I'm sorry?"

The woman, Miriam, was utterly overwhelmed.

She never expected to see this face here, of all places.

The resemblance to M Sterling was uncanny.

And strikingly, this young woman appeared to be about the same age as her own daughter, Amelia.
###Chapter 436

In a flash of terrifying clarity, Miriam Croft understood.

The young woman standing before her was Evelyn.

She was the true daughter of the Sterling family. The child she had secretly swapped all those years ago.

Miriam’s heart hammered against her ribs. Her breath hitched. "Who are you?" she demanded, her voice unsteady.

Evelyn caught the rapid shift in the woman's expression. Shock. Raw fear. Then, a dark, menacing glint. It was deeply unsettling.

Frowning, Evelyn instinctively took a step backward. "I'm a guest at the Sterling banquet. Is there an issue?" Her eyes remained fixed on the agitated middle-aged woman. "A moment ago... you said, 'It's you again.' What did you mean by that? Have we met?"

Evelyn studied the woman's face intently. She searched her memory, but came up empty. She was certain she had never seen this person before.

So why did this stranger look at her as if she were a ghost returned from the grave?

Miriam, however, remembered Evelyn perfectly. The memory was seared into her mind.

It was not long ago, the day Miriam had received her devastating cancer diagnosis. As she left the clinic, numb and clutching the test results, she had literally bumped into Evelyn on the street.

And now, here she was again. At the Sterling family banquet. A gathering where every single Sterling relative was present.

Miriam’s face hardened into a mask of cold resolve. This could not happen. Evelyn could not meet them.

If they saw her face, if they looked into her eyes, the entire carefully constructed lie of her life would shatter.

Time was a luxury she no longer possessed. Miriam was determined to carry her secret to the grave. No one, especially not this girl, would be allowed to destroy the privileged life her own daughter enjoyed as a Sterling heiress.

"Oh, my mistake. I thought you were my friend's daughter," Miriam fabricated quickly. She forced a tight, unconvincing smile onto her lips. "My apologies. I meant no offense."

Evelyn watched her closely. The smile didn't reach the woman's eyes. A cold dread trickled down Evelyn's spine.

This woman was hiding something. Something significant.

"Is that so?" Evelyn replied, carefully masking her growing suspicion. "If you don't know, I'll find someone else to ask."

There was a predatory quality in the woman's gaze that made Evelyn's skin crawl.

As Evelyn turned to walk away, a dry, rough hand shot out and clamped like a vise around her wrist.

The grip was surprisingly strong.

Evelyn tried to pull her arm free, but the woman held fast.

"Let go of me," Evelyn said, her voice low and firm.

Miriam’s eyes darted around the corridor. "Not here. We need to talk. Somewhere private."

"Talk about what? I have nothing to say to you."

"Please," Miriam insisted, her voice dropping to a desperate whisper. "It's important. About your past."

Evelyn froze. Her past was a mystery, a void she had never been able to fill. This woman knew something.

Miriam saw the hesitation and pressed her advantage. "This way. Quickly."

She began pulling Evelyn, not toward the grand staircase leading back to the ballroom, but toward a plain, unmarked door at the end of the hall.

Evelyn dug her heels in. "Where are you taking me? The exit is the other way."

"We can't use the main stairs," Miriam hissed, her eyes wild with panic. "Someone might see us."

She shoved the heavy door open, revealing a dark, narrow concrete staircase. The air was cold and smelled of dust.

It was a service stairwell.

"Why are we taking the stairs?" Evelyn demanded, her suspicion turning to alarm.

"Just come!" Miriam tugged harder, pulling Evelyn into the stark, echoing space.

The door swung shut behind them with a heavy, final thud.

The sounds of the opulent banquet vanished, replaced by the hollow echo of their footsteps.

They were alone.

"Wait!"

Evelyn's voice was a sharp, gritted command.

Miriam's heart lurched. Instinct took over. She tried to pull her wrist free. "Let me go, please."

But Miriam's fingers only clamped down harder. Panic seemed to give her strength. She couldn't allow Evelyn to leave.

Miriam's eyes narrowed to slits. She spoke slowly, deliberately. "You said you were going to the Sterling banquet. I will take you."

Evelyn ignored the ache in her wrist. She forced her breathing to remain even. "Just point me in the right direction. That's all I need."

The woman's gaunt face made her strained smile appear grotesque.

"Please. Allow me this courtesy." After a brief pause, Miriam finally released her grip. She didn't want to seem overly suspicious.

She set down her cleaning supplies. Turning, she began to walk ahead. "Follow me. The Sterling event is starting soon. You wouldn't want to be late."

Evelyn stood still for a moment, watching the woman's retreating back. Hesitation gripped her for a few seconds. Then, she followed.

Something was undeniably wrong with this woman.

Since Miriam was so determined to lead, Evelyn decided to play along. She would discover the real game.

Soon, Miriam guided her into the stairwell.

Evelyn raised a single brow. Her expression remained carefully blank. "Why are we taking the stairs?"

Miriam didn't bother to look back. "The hotel elevators are out of order. We have no other choice."

A faint, cold smile touched Evelyn's lips. "But the Sterling banquet is on the first floor. Why are we going up? And in a hotel of this caliber, is it possible every single elevator is broken?"
###Chapter 437

Their conversation continued as Evelyn followed Miriam up the stairs.

Miriam walked ahead in silence. Her head was tilted at a slight angle. A flicker of malice passed through her eyes.

Suddenly, she spun around and shoved Evelyn with force.

Evelyn reacted instantly. She caught Miriam's arm, her own footing solid.

Frowning deeply, Evelyn stared at the woman. "Did you just try to push me?"

She paused for a beat before continuing. "I don't know you. We have no history. What possible reason could you have to hurt me?"

Evelyn had been cautious since arriving. But she never expected an attack from a stranger, a cleaner she'd just met. This woman had tried to send her tumbling down the staircase.

A thought struck her.

Perhaps this wasn't their first encounter. Maybe this woman knew exactly who she was.

"What are you talking about? I don't understand." Miriam, her plan foiled, panicked. She tried to wrench her arm free. "Let me go! I'm warning you, I'll call the police!"

Evelyn let out a cold laugh. "Go ahead. We can explain everything. Why you brought me up here. Your attempt to push me down the stairs. Tell me, what is your motive?"

Panic flashed in Miriam's eyes.

"You're mistaken! I was just checking you were still behind me. Don't twist my actions." Frustrated, Miriam countered vehemently. "This is an injustice! I was only helping you find your way, and now you accuse me? I'm just a cleaner!"

Evelyn remained unmoved. She tightened her grip. "Whether it's a false accusation or the truth isn't for you to decide. We're going to security. The hallway cameras will show what really happened. Then we'll see how you explain yourself."

Determined to uncover the truth, Evelyn knew this was no simple misunderstanding. The cleaner's action was deliberate and malicious. There had to be a deeper reason. Without another word, Evelyn forcefully grabbed Miriam and started pulling her back down the stairs. Miriam's eyes widened in sheer terror. She struggled violently to break free.

It was useless. Her prolonged illness had left her weak. She was no match for Evelyn's firm hold.

Evelyn summoned unexpected strength. She dragged Miriam all the way to the first floor.

"Release me!" Miriam screamed in desperation.

As they neared the hotel's security office, Miriam's fear intensified.

In a sudden, desperate move, she stomped hard on the hem of Evelyn's gown. The fabric jerked taut, pulling Evelyn backward. She lost her balance.

"Ah!" Evelyn cried out in surprise.

Seizing the opportunity, Miriam shoved Evelyn sideways and broke into a run.

"Stop right there!" Evelyn yelled, giving chase.

In her rush, Evelyn's heel twisted awkwardly. She stumbled forward dramatically.

A faint snapping sound echoed. A sharp, immediate pain shot through her ankle.
###Chapter 438

Evelyn barely caught her balance.
Her hand shot out, slamming against the wall to stop her fall.
She inhaled sharply, ignoring the sudden pain in her ankle.
Her head snapped up.

The cleaning woman was gone.

Evelyn's brow furrowed deeply.
That woman had spoken in cryptic phrases.
Her behavior was bizarre.
Now, she had vanished into the empty hallway.
What secret was she hiding?

A cold certainty settled in Evelyn's gut.
This woman's secret was somehow connected to her.
The connection was a tangled mess she couldn't yet unravel.
Otherwise, why would the cleaner have tried to harm her upon their first meeting?

Meanwhile, the Sterling family's banquet was reaching its peak.
The event was officially underway.
Jonathan Sterling ascended the stage.
A hush fell over the glittering crowd.

"Ladies and gentlemen," he began, his voice smooth and commanding.
"We welcome you to our home tonight."
He offered a perfectly calibrated smile.
It was a smile designed to charm and captivate.
Many eyes followed him, particularly those of the young heiresses present.

"As you are aware," Jonathan continued, "it has been many years since we've hosted a gathering of this scale."
He paused, letting the anticipation build.
"Tonight, under the banner of the Sterling Group, we have a significant announcement to share."

A wave of murmured speculation swept through the guests.
Jonathan allowed the whispers to continue, a knowing smirk playing on his lips.

"What could be so important?" someone hissed nearby.
"Do you think it's related to that man Jonathan personally greeted earlier?"
"Perhaps. His identity remains a mystery."

As the suspense thickened, Jonathan's smile broadened.
He raised his voice, cutting through the chatter.
"Effective immediately," he declared, his tone firm and clear, "the Sterling Group is entering a formal partnership with Aethelgard Industries."

A collective gasp echoed through the grand ballroom.
Excitement erupted.
"Aethelgard Industries? Is that even possible?"
"The Sterling Group has just aligned with the most powerful corporation in the country!"

"So the entire purpose of this banquet was to announce the collaboration?"

"If that's the case, they must consider it extremely significant."

"Significant? It's Aethelgard Industries. Any corporation would go to great lengths for such a partnership."

"Do you think the founder of Aethelgard Industries is present tonight?"

"I'd give anything to finally see his face! No journalist has ever managed to capture his photograph all these years."

Jonathan Sterling allowed the excited murmurs to subside before speaking again. "In thirty minutes, we will hold an official signing ceremony. We invite you all to join us in witnessing this historic occasion."

"An official signing? So the founder truly is here?"

"Quiet! I heard a whisper—he is. And more than that, he intends to publicly introduce his wife for the very first time."

"If that's accurate... that's monumental."

"Precisely! We're speaking about the founder of Aethelgard Industries—a billionaire, one of the most influential men in the city. His wife must be a remarkable woman."

"The anticipation is unbearable."

Having delivered the announcement, Jonathan descended from the stage with smooth professionalism. He moved towards a private room located at the rear of the grand hall.

Inside, Alexander Blackwood was pacing. His phone was clenched in his hand. Anxiety was etched across his features.

Sebastian Hayes, also a guest at the Sterling family's event, observed him from nearby. He offered a calm, reasoned assurance. "Alexander, relax. Evelyn is a grown woman. She wouldn't simply disappear. She will show up soon enough."
###Chapter 439

Alexander's expression remained grim. It tightened further.

"I can't get through to Evelyn's phone." He faced Sebastian, his jaw clenched. "She promised to be here in ten minutes. She's still not here."

Sebastian released a tired breath. He was about to speak when Jonathan Sterling entered. "Ah, Mr. Blackwood, you've arrived."

Jonathan walked over and looked at Alexander. "Is there a problem?"

Alexander, still relentlessly dialing, didn't glance up.

Seeing this, Sebastian intervened. "His wife hasn't arrived. He's extremely concerned."

Jonathan's frown intensified. "That is strange. She should be here by now. Shall I dispatch someone to locate her?"

Before he finished speaking, Alexander spun around. "No. I will go myself."

Sebastian's eyes widened. "What? The ceremony is about to start. You're supposed to reveal your identity tonight!"

Jonathan also looked displeased. "Mr. Blackwood, it's fine. I can send my men. You shouldn't miss this event."

Alexander shook his head decisively. He looked at Sebastian. "I have to go. I can't leave her out there alone."

Sebastian hesitated. He recalled all the hardships Evelyn had endured. For anyone else, this might be trivial. But for her, he couldn't guarantee her safety either.

"Alright," Sebastian relented with a sigh.

Jonathan, however, was far from satisfied. "Mr. Blackwood, tonight's schedule is set. You're really leaving now?"

Before Alexander could answer, Sebastian quickly interjected, "Mr. Sterling, you may not know, but his wife is a special case. Her reputation has made her enemies. She has faced genuine danger before. His concern is justified."

Alexander met Jonathan's gaze sincerely. "Mr. Sterling, I apologize. But I must go. If I return in time, we proceed as planned. If not, Sebastian will handle the ceremony for me."

Jonathan was still unhappy, but he saw Alexander's resolve was unshakable. The priority tonight was securing the partnership with Aethelgard Industries. Everything else was secondary.

"Very well, Mr. Blackwood. Go. I hope your wife is safe," Jonathan conceded.

A moment later, embarrassment flooded her. A blush heated Evelyn's cheeks as she watched Alexander kneel before her.

"I twisted my ankle running earlier," she confessed softly.

Alexander gently lifted her foot and removed her high heel. His expression hardened the instant he saw her ankle. The sprain was severe.

His fingers lightly traced the swelling as he inspected it. "When I called, I wasn't trying to hurry you. Walking in heels is difficult. Running in them is asking for trouble."

Realizing the misunderstanding, Evelyn let out a small laugh. "No, Alexander, you misunderstood. I wasn't running to get to the banquet."

Alexander paused, clearly confused. "Then why were you running?"
###Chapter 440

Evelyn pressed her lips together, hesitating for a moment before deciding to tell Alexander about what had just happened.

When she described how the cleaning woman had tried to shove her down the staircase, Alexander's expression turned stormy.

"She laid hands on you?" he demanded, his voice sharp with alarm. "She's clearly unhinged."

"That's what I thought. But this dress and these heels made it impossible for me to move quickly enough to stop her," Evelyn explained, frustration lacing her words.

The woman hadn't seemed particularly powerful.

If she'd been wearing her usual clothes, Evelyn was sure she could have handled it and discovered what the woman was hiding.

Alexander looked tense.

He was shocked that someone within the hotel would dare to target Evelyn.

After the evening's scare, he felt even more reluctant to let her go anywhere alone.

"Don't worry about it. I'll have this looked into immediately," Alexander assured her.

He was determined that anyone who threatened or deceived Evelyn would face the consequences.

He stood, his arm encircling her waist firmly, and lifted her effortlessly into his arms. Evelyn instinctively wrapped her arms around his neck, feeling secure in his grasp.

As he began to walk, Evelyn looked at him, confused. "Where are we going?"

"To a clinic nearby to have your ankle examined. If it's serious, we'll go straight to the hospital for a full check-up," he explained, glancing down at her.

Seeing the deep concern etched on his face, Evelyn's expression softened.

She reached up and gently smoothed the worry lines from his forehead with her fingertips.

"Don't look so worried, Alexander." Her voice was soft as she leaned closer to him. "It's likely just a sprain. I'm certain it's not broken."

Her attempt to reassure him only made Alexander's expression grow more severe.

"Do you actually want it to be broken?" he asked, a note of exasperation in his tone.

Evelyn was taken aback. "Why on earth would I want that? You have quite an imagination."

She ruffled his hair affectionately, offering a small, comforting smile. "I was only trying to make you feel better."

He let out a soft sigh, his voice gentle. "Your safety is the only thing that matters to me."

Knowing how deeply he cared, Evelyn decided not to mention her ankle again. Suddenly, she remembered something important.

"Wait, can we just leave the hotel like this? The Sterling family banquet must have started by now. Perhaps you should go back. Someone else can accompany me to the clinic," she suggested quickly.

Alexander seemed to ignore her, continuing his brisk pace out of the hotel lobby.

"Alexander, did you hear me?" Evelyn asked again, a thread of concern in her voice.

He finally stopped and turned his gaze to her, his expression calm and resolute. "Evelyn, do you truly believe any banquet could ever be more important to me than you are?"

Her heart fluttered at his words.
###Chapter 441

Evelyn was startled by Alexander's uncharacteristically tender words.
A deep blush instantly spread across her cheeks.

"Why are you being so sweet all of a sudden?" she managed to whisper after a long pause.

Alexander chuckled softly, clearly enjoying her flustered reaction as he carried her towards the clinic.

He spoke in a calm, deliberate tone. "Darling, I know you believe tonight's Sterling family banquet is a crucial opportunity for me to network with influential figures."
He paused, adjusting his grip on her. "But your safety and well-being are my primary concern. I need to be certain you're alright before I can even think about socializing."
His gaze was intense. "If I were to leave you now, my mind would be entirely preoccupied with worry. I wouldn't be able to focus on anything else."

Evelyn's heart fluttered at his sincere declaration.

Alexander always knew exactly what to say to ease her anxieties.

Yet she could feel the genuine concern behind his words.

A bittersweet smile touched her lips.

She gently brushed the lapel of his impeccably tailored suit. "I had this suit specially made for you to wear to the banquet tonight. It looks so distinguished. It's a shame it won't be seen."

Alexander gave her a charming smile. "The only opinion about this suit that matters to me is yours. Seeing the appreciation in your eyes makes the effort you put into it completely worthwhile."

Evelyn playfully tapped his lips. "You're a serious businessman. How can you afford to be so focused on romance?"

Alexander raised an eyebrow, considering her question with mock seriousness. "This suit is too finely crafted for just one occasion. Since I missed wearing it tonight, I'll simply save it for another important event."

Evelyn's eyes sparkled with amusement. "What if someone notices you've worn it before?"

Their conversation had taken a strangely serious turn over such a trivial matter.

With utter sincerity, Alexander explained, "In Crestwood, I'm hardly the center of the social scene. I could wear this same suit a dozen times and no one would likely notice."

Evelyn shook her head in disagreement. "That's not true at all! You command attention wherever you go. The moment you enter a room, all eyes are drawn to you."

Now that Alexander was no longer using a wheelchair, his striking presence was undeniable.

Evelyn's observation was entirely accurate.

His brief appearance at the Sterling estate earlier had indeed left a lasting impression on the guests.

Unbeknownst to them, many attendees were still speculating about the identity of the enigmatic man who had arrived with Jonathan Sterling.

After a moment's thought, Alexander grinned. "In that case, it seems I'll need my talented designer, Evelyn, to create many more exceptional suits for me in the future."

Without hesitation, Evelyn replied, "Of course! Designing for you is one of my greatest pleasures."

As they finished speaking, Alexander carried Evelyn into the clinic.

"Doctor," he said immediately upon entering, his voice firm with concern. "Could you please examine my wife's ankle? She took a bad fall earlier."
###Chapter 442

The doctor gestured for Alexander to carefully place Evelyn onto the examination table.

Alexander held Evelyn's hand tightly, his gaze fixed on the doctor as the examination began.

Evelyn, however, seemed miles away, her thoughts completely separate from the pain in her ankle.

She broke the silence, giving Alexander's hand a gentle squeeze. "I feel terrible that you missed the Sterling's banquet because of my accident. It's my fault."

Alexander noticed the guilt shadowing Evelyn's eyes. He leaned in closer, his lips pressing into a thin line before he spoke.

"Darling, please don't think that way," he said, his voice soft but firm. "Yes, it was an opportunity, but it wasn't the only one. There will be others. Let's forget about tonight."

He gently cupped her cheek with his warm hand. "Your well-being is the only thing that matters right now. Everything else can wait."

Evelyn found it difficult to argue, disarmed by the raw sincerity in his eyes, though a sliver of frustration remained.

"Alright, I understand," she conceded with a soft sigh, letting her concerns fade.

Seeing her accept his words, a gentle smile touched Alexander's lips. He affectionately ruffled her hair.

His only real regret was not revealing himself as the founder of Aethelgard Industries at the gala, especially in front of Evelyn.

But this was merely a minor deviation in his much larger plan.

Since he had skipped the Sterling family's event, Alexander began considering hosting an extravagant affair of his own. There, he could unveil his corporate identity and publicly announce his relationship with Evelyn simultaneously.

He was certain this would create a far greater impact than his original intention at the Sterling gala.

Alexander was confident that when the time came, Evelyn would be both stunned and overjoyed.

In the quiet room, Alexander meticulously formulated this new strategy in his mind.

Shortly after, the doctor finished examining Evelyn's ankle.

"First, I'll apply a medicated spray to reduce the swelling," the doctor stated. "You must use this consistently at home. It's crucial you limit any weight-bearing activity on this foot."

"Limit your movement. Rest the ankle for the next several days. You should heal properly," the doctor advised.

Before Evelyn could respond, Alexander spoke. "We understand. Thank you, Doctor."

Fortunately, it was the weekend. Evelyn didn't need to worry about missing work.

The doctor left the room to get a medicated spray for her injury.

A familiar voice suddenly came from the live broadcast on the television.

"As the CEO of Aethelgard Industries, I am honored to represent our company at the Sterling family gala tonight. We eagerly anticipate finalizing our partnership with Mr. Sterling and look forward to a prosperous collaboration."

Evelyn's eyes moved to the screen. Sebastian Hayes stood on the stage at the Sterling banquet, looking impeccably dressed.

"So Mr. Hayes is attending on the company's behalf," she concluded.

It made sense. Sebastian was the public representative for Aethelgard Industries.

A frown creased her brow. "Wait. I thought the founder of Aethelgard was supposed to be there tonight. Why haven't I seen him?"

Alexander, the very founder in question, remained perfectly composed. "He was likely delayed by an unexpected matter."

Evelyn nodded slowly, though her voice held a hint of letdown. "That's a shame. I was really looking forward to finally meeting the founder in person."
###Chapter 443

Alexander’s chest tightened at Evelyn’s words.

“You attended the Sterling banquet just to catch a glimpse of Aethelgard Industries’ founder?” he asked, his voice low.

Evelyn blinked, feigning innocence. “I’m curious! Aren’t you? The whole city is talking about him.”

A sliver of suspicion entered Alexander’s gaze. “They say he’s young and handsome. The most eligible bachelor in Crestwood. Are you one of those admirers hoping to meet him?”

She had no idea it was him.

Even as the man himself, a sharp pang of jealousy shot through him.

Still, he wasn’t ready to tell her the truth.

Noticing his shift in mood, Evelyn laughed softly. “Alexander, you’re jealous. I only mentioned his appearance, and you’re already building scenarios.”

His expression darkened. “You didn’t deny it, Evelyn. So you do think that way!”

“I absolutely do not!” she retorted quickly. “Who said I believe the founder is young and handsome? With what he’s achieved, building Aethelgard so rapidly, he’s probably in his late thirties. It’s highly unlikely he’s a young man.”

Her unexpected reasoning left him momentarily speechless.

She was denying the attraction, technically.

But why did her denial feel so… wrong?

Evelyn seemed genuinely convinced the founder was a middle-aged man pushing forty.

“Besides,” Evelyn added, a thoughtful pause hanging in the air, “he’s always been so secretive. Maybe he’s just not very confident about his looks. That could be why he hides.”

Alexander was utterly lost for words.

If she had expressed admiration for a dashing, young founder, his jealousy would have been straightforward.

But her painting him as some aging, insecure businessman caused a completely different, and profoundly strange, discomfort.

Regardless, the feeling was deeply unpleasant.

He swiftly changed the subject, glancing toward the hallway. “What’s taking the doctor so long?”

Evelyn paused, realizing her comments about the Aethelgard founder had truly dampened his spirits.

Seeing no alternative, she decided to drop the matter and follow his lead.

What else could she do? She adored this possessive, complicated man.

Soon after, they collected the medication and left the doctor’s office.

They settled into the car, the route taking them toward Serenity Oaks.

As the city lights began to blur past, Alexander pulled Evelyn closer. “It’s getting late. If you’re tired, lean on me and rest.”

He remembered how exhausted she had looked that morning and worried she was still pushing herself.

His words brought back the memory of her earlier discomfort.

Yet, the nausea and fatigue had vanished after work.

She shook her head. “No, I’m fine. I’m not tired at all.”

Perhaps it had just been stress and lack of sleep.

Nestled comfortably in Alexander’s arms, a sudden, crucial thought flashed into Evelyn’s mind.
###Chapter 444

"Alexander," Evelyn said, glancing at him with a warm, easy smile. "Theodore came to me recently. He requested a modification to a wedding dress design and wanted me to oversee its creation. But after everything that happened, I never completed the task. I called him today, intending to cancel the order and return his deposit. He refused."

She paused, a slight frown creasing her brow. "I still believe I should return the money. Could you assist me with the transfer? I don't wish to be indebted to him. It's been bothering me."

Alexander's lips curved into a quiet smile. He was pleased by her decisive action to sever ties with Theodore.

It was clear she held no lingering affection for the man.

"I'll handle it," he said smoothly. "I'll ensure his payment is returned to him."

He turned to his assistant in the front seat. "Pull up Theodore Vance's bank details. Process a refund for the amount he paid Evelyn."

"Right away, Mr. Blackwood," the assistant replied, already taking out his phone.

A moment later, the assistant's face showed confusion. "Sir, the transfer failed. Theodore Vance has blocked our accounts."

Evelyn let out a short, frustrated sigh. Theodore was being unnecessarily difficult.

"Forget it," she said, shaking her head. "If he's determined to refuse the refund, he'd likely just find a way to send it back. I won't waste any more energy on this."

Alexander nodded. "What's your plan now?"

She pressed her lips together, considering. "The agreement still stands. I'll complete the dress design and have it made. We'll deliver it when it's finished."

After a moment, she added, "If he refuses to accept it, that's his problem. He's leaving the country soon anyway. We'll be done with him for good."

A sudden sparkle lit her eyes as she broke into a grin. "As for the money? I'll donate it discreetly to a local orphanage."

Alexander raised an eyebrow, a smirk tugging at his lips. "So you're making Theodore an anonymous donor?"

Her grin widened, bright and mischievous. "Exactly. He has more than enough money. This works out perfectly."

Alexander respected her decision. "If you're comfortable with this plan, I support it. Let me know if you need any help."

Evelyn's smile softened as she settled back into his arms. A weight felt lifted from her shoulders.

Over the next couple of days, she rested at home.

The strange fatigue and nausea from the banquet did not return. She soon put the episode out of her mind.

By Monday, her ankle felt stronger. She could take a few steps without pain.

Alexander was concerned, but Evelyn was eager to return to her normal routine.

"I'll drive you," he said, his tone calm but firm.

She dismissed the idea with a quick shake of her head. "There's no need to coddle me. Your shoulder is still healing. You should stay here and rest."
###Chapter 445

Alexander chuckled softly, shaking his head.

"I've been stuck inside for too long."

"It's just a short drive to your office. It's nothing."

"What's important is getting to spend a little more time with you."

His voice was warm, his eyes sincere.

Evelyn felt her resistance crumbling.

Her initial refusal faded before she could even grasp it.

After a brief hesitation, she sighed in defeat.

"Alright, fine. You win."

Seeing her finally agree, Alexander's mood brightened.

He flashed her a triumphant smile and got into the car after her.

Soon, they reached the entrance of Aethelgard Industries.

"You can drop me off here," Evelyn said to the driver.

She then turned to Alexander. "You should head back now."

Alexander nodded casually.

He reached out and gently tucked a stray strand of hair behind her ear.

"Okay," he said softly. "Call me when you finish work. I'll be here waiting for you."

Wait, was he planning to pick her up too?

Evelyn paused.

She didn't want to inconvenience him.

But she also didn't want to spoil his good mood.

Deciding not to dwell on it, she simply smiled.

"Alright."

With that, she got out of the car and walked towards the building.

She had only taken a few steps when she suddenly remembered something.

She immediately turned and jogged back to the car.

Alexander raised an eyebrow. "Forget something?"

Evelyn didn't answer.

Instead, she leaned through the open window and pressed a soft kiss to his lips.

"Almost forgot your morning kiss!" she said with a playful grin.

Then she dashed off without looking back.

The driver, who had been watching silently, couldn't help but marvel.

This couple's affection seemed to grow deeper every day.

Alexander sat still for a moment.

His fingertips gently brushed the spot where Evelyn had kissed him.

A small, contented smile touched his lips.

His eyes followed her retreating figure until she disappeared inside the building.

Only then did he reluctantly look away.

"Let's go," he instructed the driver.

Just as the driver was about to start the car, a sudden knock on the window interrupted them.

Alexander frowned slightly and rolled down the window.

He found himself looking at a familiar face.

Sebastian Hayes stood outside the car.

"What are you doing here?" Alexander asked, his tone even.

Sebastian blinked. "Isn't there a virtual meeting with the Sterling Group executives today? It's pretty obvious why I'm here."

Alexander paused briefly, then remembered the important morning meeting.

Maintaining his composure, he replied smoothly. "Right. That's why I'm here."

"Huh? That's odd," Sebastian said, glancing at his watch. "The meeting isn't until ten. Why are you here so early?"

Alexander certainly wasn't going to admit he'd just dropped Evelyn off.

If Sebastian found out, he'd never hear the end of it.

Besides, Alexander still remembered that Sebastian had once shown interest in Evelyn.

Even though it was in the past, he had no desire to discuss her with him.

His possessiveness towards Evelyn only seemed to intensify over time.

Keeping a straight face, he answered calmly. "I wanted to get a head start on some paperwork that's been piling up."

Without giving Sebastian a chance to question further, he gestured to the backseat.

He instructed the driver, "Unlock the door. He's riding with us."
###Chapter 446

Dustin slid into the passenger seat with a grin. "Back entrance again today?"

Collin gave a single, firm nod. "Evelyn still doesn't know about my connection to Aethelgard Industries. I prefer to keep it that way for now."

"Your secret's safe with me," Dustin replied, a hint of a laugh in his voice. "I've been keeping this quiet for you for ages. I'm not about to slip up now."

His expression then turned serious. "But Alexander, you need to tell her. Soon. You missed a major opportunity at the Sterling banquet. Wait too long, and the anxiety of telling her might become paralyzing."

"I have it under control," Alexander stated, his tone leaving no room for argument. "I know what I'm doing."

Minutes later, Alexander and Sebastian entered Aethelgard Industries through the private rear entrance and ascended to the secure executive levels.

Access to this floor was severely restricted, reserved for a handful of trusted individuals. It protected both the company's most sensitive projects and the founder's identity.

Despite the tight security, news that the founder had arrived with Sebastian spread through the offices like wildfire.

Evelyn had just settled at her desk when the excited whispers reached her.

"Big news! The real boss is here today!"

At first, the announcement was met with skepticism. "Sebastian is always here. What's the big deal?"

The messenger quickly clarified. "Not Sebastian! I mean the founder. The actual owner!"

A wave of intrigued murmurs rippled through the open-plan office.

"Is that for real? Or just another rumor?"

"It's true! My friend was in the underground garage and saw Sebastian escorting someone through the side entrance."

"That had to be him. Sebastian was being incredibly deferential. My friend didn't get a clear look at his face, but the man's presence was undeniable. He radiated power!"

Another employee leaned in, eyes wide. "That's amazing! Did your friend manage to get a photo?"

The excitement dimmed slightly. "No, they moved way too fast for that."

"What a shame. I've worked here for years and haven't laid eyes on him once."

Hearing the growing commotion, Evelyn decided it was time to refocus her team. She stood up to leave her office.

Just then, another coworker burst in, buzzing with fresh gossip. "Wait, wait! I have some juicy news! You won't believe this!"

The crowd's interest seemed to waver. "More rumors? Is it about the founder's identity? Do you have a picture?"

The coworker waved a dismissive hand. "It's not exactly that. Well, it's related, but there's more to it..."

This successfully hooked everyone's attention. "Well? What is it? Don't keep us waiting!"

The coworker lowered her voice conspiratorially. "Apparently, Miss Sterling is here today, too."
###Chapter 447

Alexander Blackwood sat behind his imposing desk in the top-floor office of Aethelgard Industries.

His assistant stood before him, delivering the latest findings.

"Sir, our investigation into The Grand Crestwood, the venue for the Sterling family banquet, has yielded a result. We identified the suspicious cleaning lady Mrs. Blackwood encountered. However, there is a significant anomaly. This woman does not appear in any of the hotel's official employment records."

Alexander's face hardened instantly. His eyes turned to ice.

"If she wasn't employed by the hotel," he said, his voice dangerously low, "then what was her purpose for being there, cleaning near the first-floor restrooms that night?"

The assistant shifted his weight slightly. "Considering the Sterling family managed the entire event that evening, their personnel were present throughout the building. It is highly probable she was among them."

A curt nod from Alexander confirmed he had considered the same possibility.

"This complicates matters, Mr. Blackwood," the assistant continued, his brow furrowed. "If she is indeed connected to the Sterlings, any further investigation on our part will be seen as a direct intrusion. It will undoubtedly alert them and could compromise our newly formed partnership."

Alexander shared the concern.

Aethelgard Industries had just finalized a major contract with the Sterling Group. Probing their internal affairs now was a considerable risk.

He weighed his options carefully.

Confrontation seemed inevitable if he wanted answers.

"Very well. After my eleven o'clock meeting, contact Jonathan Sterling's office. Arrange an appointment—"

A sharp knock on the office door cut him off.

His secretary's voice filtered through the heavy wood. "Mr. Blackwood? Apologies for the interruption. The front desk has informed me that Miss Amelia Sterling is here. She is requesting to see you."

Alexander's gaze snapped to his assistant. Their eyes met in silent understanding.

Without waiting for instruction, the assistant responded firmly. "Inform Miss Sterling that Mr. Blackwood is unavailable. Find a way to turn her away."

Downstairs, in the gleaming lobby of Aethelgard Industries, Amelia Sterling stood near the reception desk.

She was flanked by several large, stern-faced bodyguards.

Their collective presence created a palpable tension in the air.

The receptionist looked visibly uncomfortable under their silent scrutiny.

A moment later, the phone on the desk rang, its sharp trill breaking the silence.

Carol's expression brightened immediately.
"Answer it, now!" she demanded, her voice sharp with impatience.

The receptionist quietly lifted the receiver.
She listened for a moment before turning back to Carol.
"Miss Sterling," she said carefully, "I'm afraid our CEO is unavailable today. Perhaps Mr. Hayes would be able to meet with you instead?"

Carol's carefully maintained composure shattered.
She fixed the receptionist with a venomous glare.
"Are you stupid?" she hissed. "I specifically asked for the founder of Aethelgard Industries, not some underling! Do not attempt to pawn me off on someone else!"

Her eyes turned cold and hard.
She gritted her teeth, her voice dropping to a threatening whisper.
"My brother informed me personally that the founder is meeting with our executives today. Are you calling my brother a liar?"

In one swift motion, she grabbed a pen from the desk.
She brandished it like a weapon, pointing it directly at the receptionist's face.
"Stop playing games with me. The Sterling Group is a key partner. My visit today concerns vital corporate business. If you continue to lie to me, I will ensure your boss hears every detail of your incompetence. You will not enjoy the consequences."

The receptionist's face lost all color.
She visibly trembled.

She wasn't being deceitful.
She had received explicit orders from the highest level: Amelia Sterling was not to be accommodated under any circumstances.
But how could she possibly explain that now?
###Chapter 448

The receptionist felt her composure cracking.

Amelia Sterling’s formidable entourage and sharp temper were too much. How could she possibly manage this situation?

"Miss Sterling, please wait. I will verify once more." With no other option, the receptionist reluctantly dialed the executive floor again.

Near the commotion, Evelyn found herself pulled along by her excited colleagues from the Fashion Design Department. The air buzzed with anticipation.

Curious staff had gathered, their murmurs creating a steady hum.

"It really is Miss Sterling! Why is she here?"

"Rumor says she's seeking the founder. Who knew they were acquainted?"

"Wait, does that imply she has actually met the founder?"

A new speculation started to spread. "Consider this—could our company's alliance with the Sterling Group be due to a personal connection between the founder and Miss Sterling?"

The unspoken suggestion was clear.

"That seems plausible. The Sterling family holds immense power. A union with the founder would be a strategic match."

"But isn't the founder said to be married?"

"Those are just rumors. Nothing is confirmed. It could easily be a smokescreen."

The gossip intensified.

Amelia, overhearing these comments, felt a surge of gratification.

As anticipated, the public saw her as the ideal partner for the head of Aethelgard Industries.

That woman from the banquet? She was insignificant in comparison.

The thought filled Amelia with deep satisfaction.

Her eyes casually swept the crowd—and then locked onto a familiar face. Her smug expression instantly froze.

There, amidst the spectators, stood Evelyn. Amelia's eyes widened in disbelief. Wasn't she supposed to be the founder's wife? What was she doing here?

Evelyn hadn't intended to get involved, but her colleagues' enthusiasm was infectious.

From a distance, she recognized Amelia at the front desk. Memories of the awkward encounter at the Sterling family banquet flooded back—how Amelia had deliberately tried to humiliate her.

Though Amelia had ultimately allowed her to pass, Evelyn had sensed a deep-seated animosity.

Even now, Amelia's presence felt threatening.

After a moment of observation, Evelyn decided to leave. "I have work to complete. I'm going back upstairs."

Her colleagues, absorbed in the drama, barely acknowledged her.

Evelyn didn't mind. She turned and walked away, unaware that Amelia had already spotted her.

By the time Amelia pushed through the crowd, Evelyn had vanished.

But Amelia was certain of what she had seen.

Evelyn's striking beauty and elegant poise were unforgettable.

The realization fueled Amelia's irritation.

So, Evelyn had used her looks to enchant Alexander and secure her position as his wife?

Employees from the Fashion Design Department nearby tensed as Amelia approached them, fearing they had inadvertently caused offense.

One brave soul stepped forward. "Miss Sterling, can we assist you with something?"

Amelia's face softened into a pleasant smile. "I noticed a woman with your group earlier. She looked familiar. Could you tell me her name?"
###Chapter 449

Isabella hesitated for a moment before speaking. "She was wearing a company ID badge."

The woman at the reception desk had a sudden look of recognition. "Ah, you must mean Evelyn. She was here, but she left just a few minutes ago."

Keeping her composure, Isabella asked, "Does she work here as well?"

Given Evelyn's relationship with the founder of Aethelgard Industries, she probably held a senior position.

The woman's reply took Isabella by surprise. "Yes, Evelyn is the head of the Fashion Design Department."

This revelation startled Isabella. "Just a department head?"

The woman looked confused. "Is that... a problem?"

Isabella thought for a second before explaining. "I've known her for a while. She's incredibly talented. I just expected her to be in a more senior role."

The woman gave an awkward laugh. "Evelyn is still quite young, though. Becoming a department head at her age is actually a significant achievement."

Isabella, coming from a life of privilege, might not fully grasp how difficult career advancement was for ordinary people.

Curiosity getting the better of her, Isabella pressed further. "Is Evelyn from a prominent family?"

The woman's expression shifted subtly. "Not particularly. Most of our employees come from fairly average backgrounds."

The only notable exception was Genevieve from the wealthy Prescott family.

The woman was starting to wonder why Isabella was asking so many specific questions.

Ready to move on, Isabella politely thanked the woman and walked away.

It seemed no one at Aethelgard Industries knew that Evelyn was the founder's wife.

Even more shocking was that, despite this status, she only held a mid-level management position.

This led Isabella to one conclusion: perhaps the founder didn't truly love his wife.

The possibility solidified in her mind, becoming more convincing with each passing second.

As suspicion turned into certainty, a knowing smirk curled on Isabella's lips.

This could be her golden opportunity.

5:05 PM.

The thought of having the charismatic founder of Aethelgard Industries within her grasp sent a thrill of anticipation through her.

At that very moment, Alexander entered the conference room, preparing for an imminent video call with executives from the Sterling Group.

Before he could sit down, Sebastian hurried in, looking anxious. "We have a situation downstairs. The Sterling girl is causing a scene at reception, demanding to see you. The staff looks like they're about to break. Could you handle her?"

Annoyance was clear on Alexander's face. His response was blunt. "I have no interest in speaking to her."

Considering Amelia's behavior at the banquet, he was certain her visit today would only bring more trouble.

Sebastian continued cautiously. "She brought a team of bodyguards. Employees are starting to gather downstairs. If this continues, reporters might get wind of it. That would create unnecessary complications."

He then leaned in closer, his voice dropping to a near whisper. "Worse still, Amelia knows your true identity as the founder of Aethelgard. If she says something inappropriate publicly, and it reaches your wife's ears... things could spiral out of control."

Alexander's expression darkened considerably at the thought.

"I don't understand how the Sterlings—a family known for their manners and dignity—managed to raise such an entitled daughter. Since her brother can't control her, it seems I have to deal with it." With clear authority, Alexander instructed Sebastian, "Bring her up to my office immediately. Do it before my meeting starts."

Moments later, Alexander's assistant relayed the message to the reception desk.

"Miss Sterling, the founder has agreed to meet with you upstairs," the receptionist said, turning to Amelia with a polite smile.

Instantly, a wave of murmurs rippled through the crowd that had gathered.
###Chapter 450

"The founder is in the office today!"

"That's not the real shock. The real shock is he agreed to see Miss Sterling!"

"This confirms it. There's definitely something between them."

Carol's—Amelia's—lips curved into a smug smile as she heard the receptionist's words.

Of course the founder would see her.

She was a Sterling, after all.

The hushed whispers around her only fed her growing arrogance.

"Understood," she replied, her tone dripping with indifference.

She started to turn but paused, a flicker of irritation crossing her features. She shot a sharp, displeased look at the receptionist.

"If my memory serves, you were the one attempting to block my path earlier."

The receptionist froze, her face instantly paling. "Miss Sterling, I was merely following directives from management. I intended no offense."

Was Miss Sterling actually going to pursue this?

A fresh wave of terror washed over her, making her heart hammer against her ribs.

Amelia let out a derisive snort, her sharp eyes glinting with cruel amusement. "I warned you. Show me disrespect, and you won't have a job here much longer."

The thought of finally seeing that devastatingly handsome man from the banquet again filled her voice with smug certainty.

"You'll see. I doubt your presence will be required tomorrow."

With a final, contemptuous toss of her hair, Amelia turned and strode confidently towards the elevator.

The receptionist, overwhelmed by dread, felt her legs give way. She sank weakly to the cold floor.

A concerned colleague rushed to her side. "Don't worry. We'll explain the situation to management."

The receptionist trembled violently. "It's over for me."

Other employees exchanged uneasy glances, sighing quietly amongst themselves.

"It's obvious. The founder and Miss Sterling have a connection. It must be serious."

"At this rate, she could very well become the future Mrs. Blackwood."

Amelia, her spirits soaring after putting the receptionist in her place, rode the elevator to the top floor with unwavering confidence.

Inside the conference room, Alexander was finalizing points for a crucial upcoming meeting.

Led by an assistant, Amelia entered. Her eyes immediately found the man who had dominated her thoughts.

Seeing him again only intensified her fascination.

Her heart gave a little flutter.

"Mr. Blackwood," she greeted, stepping forward with a practiced, graceful smile.

Alexander didn't bother to look up. "What is the purpose of your visit?" His voice was flat, devoid of warmth.

Amelia wasn't deterred by his coldness.

A man of his stature and success was entitled to a certain aloofness. It was expected.

Besides, with her beauty and pedigree, she was certain that given time, Alexander would recognize her true value.

Winning his affection was merely a matter of persistence.

"Mr. Blackwood, I've come to deliver an invitation." She presented a sleek, embossed envelope. "My birthday is approaching. My family is hosting a celebration. I would be honored if you would attend."

She paused, allowing a hint of vulnerability to soften her tone. "I was also hoping... that you might do me the honor of being my escort for the evening. I'd like you to share the first dance with me."

The possibility of refusal never even entered her mind.

In her imagination, she could already see it: his strong arms around her, their bodies moving in perfect harmony across the ballroom floor, her cheeks flushed with triumph. The mere thought made her pulse quicken.
###Chapter 451

Alexander didn't look up.

His focus was entirely on the papers spread across his desk.

He dismissed her request without a second thought. "That's not going to happen."

Amelia's confident smile vanished.

A worried frown creased her forehead. "You didn't even check your calendar. How can you be so sure you're busy?"

He cut her off, his voice icy. "I am always busy for you."

Humiliation washed over her.

She balled her hands into fists at her sides. "Mr. Blackwood, this is excessively rude. Is this any way to treat a member of the Sterling family?"

Alexander offered no reply.

Amelia took a sharp breath, her tone turning grave. "If you persist with this attitude, it will jeopardize the alliance between Sterling Group and Aethelgard Industries. You do understand my brother only proposed this deal to secure treatment for my heart?"

He remained completely unfazed.

Finally, he lifted his gaze.

His eyes were like chips of ice, boring into her. His voice was deceptively soft, yet each word carried a deadly chill. "Miss Sterling, let this be your last warning. Know your place. Stop trying to insert yourself into my life. I've made it clear—I am a married man. My wife and I are inseparable."

Amelia stared at him, stunned.

She tried to speak, but he gave her no opportunity.

His voice dropped even lower, more dangerous. "If you ever attempt something like this again, I will not hesitate to have you physically removed from this building. In front of everyone."

His words hit her like a physical blow.

She stood frozen, her cheeks flaming with mortification.

Tears welled in her eyes, glittering with unshed anger and hurt. She gritted her teeth. "You... You're such a jerk!"

The tears finally spilled over.

She whirled around and fled the office, her hurried footsteps echoing down the corridor.

Once the sound faded, Sebastian dared to emerge from his hiding spot in the corner.

He peered cautiously out the door, ensuring the coast was clear.

Alexander raised an eyebrow. "What's wrong with you? Don't tell me you're frightened of her?"

Sebastian quickly closed the door.

He turned to Alexander with a solemn expression. "Who in this city isn't afraid of Amelia Sterling? She's the notoriously pampered daughter of the Sterling family. I'd rather face my parents' wrath than cross her."

Leaning against the desk, Sebastian continued. "Her family prefers to stay out of the spotlight, and she hasn't truly unleashed her potential for chaos... yet. But if she ever does, the fallout would be unimaginable."

Alexander let out a cold, dismissive snort. "You're being overly dramatic."

Sebastian dropped into the seat next to him. He tapped his fingers rhythmically on the polished table surface. "Am I? The way you shut her down was brutal. Absolutely frigid."

He shot Alexander an impressed look and gave a thumbs-up. "Seriously, man. I have to respect that level of ruthlessness."

Alexander's tone was flat and pragmatic. "Amelia Sterling has her own motives. Giving her even an inch of encouragement would only fuel her persistence. Maintaining clear boundaries is the only logical way to prevent complications."

Sebastian arched an eyebrow, a knowing smirk playing on his lips. "Ah, I get it. So all that softness is reserved for one person only. Your wife."

He paused for a beat, then added with a chuckle. "Oh, and your grandmother, of course. Can't forget about Eleanor."

Alexander's expression didn't flicker. "I am a married man. That entails keeping appropriate distance from other women. I have no desire to give Evelyn a reason to be upset."
###Chapter 452

Alexander Blackwood’s expression softened as he spoke, a genuine smile touching his lips.

Sebastian Hayes observed him, a flicker of astonishment in his gaze. The depth of the bond between Alexander and Evelyn was still astounding.

He felt a slight shiver.

How had they sustained such intensity for so long?

But the smile vanished as quickly as it appeared. Alexander’s brow furrowed. He set his pen down deliberately. His voice was low and heavy. "There was another incident at the Sterling family banquet. I haven't found the right moment to tell Evelyn. It's been delayed too long. I'm unsure how to broach the subject now."

Sebastian sighed deeply. "You cannot avoid this indefinitely. Procrastination only compounds the difficulty. You must act."

Having known Alexander for years, Sebastian rarely saw him this conflicted.

Evelyn had indeed transformed him.

Wanting to assist his friend, Sebastian sat up straighter, his demeanor turning earnest. After a thoughtful pause, he ventured cautiously, "Do you have a new plan?"

Alexander gave a slight nod. "I was considering... hosting a public event as the founder of Aethelgard Industries."

Sebastian’s eyes lit up immediately. "That's an excellent idea!"

Then, a new thought occurred to him, and a sly grin spread across his face. "Wait. Isn't our company's annual gala approaching? We always host prominent figures. Why not utilize that occasion? Since Evelyn is also an employee, we could orchestrate a surprise for her."

As Alexander contemplated the suggestion, a slow, determined smile curved his mouth. He nodded firmly. "That could indeed work."

Sebastian grew more enthusiastic. "Exactly! You and Evelyn had a rushed marriage. You never formally proposed, did you? A public proposal at the gala would move her profoundly."

Alexander hesitated, looking thoughtful. "You believe so?"

Before Evelyn, romance was foreign to him. He naturally deferred to Sebastian's judgment in such matters.

Sebastian spoke with conviction. "Trust me. Women adore grand, romantic gestures. Proceed with it."

Alexander considered this for a few seconds before his gaze solidified with resolve.

"I do owe Evelyn a proper proposal. We will proceed with this plan."

Meanwhile, a startling rumor ignited through the corridors of Aethelgard Industries. Amelia Sterling had fled the top floor in tears.

"This is unbelievable! I thought the founder and Miss Sterling were close. What caused her to run off crying?"

"Could there be hidden turmoil behind the scenes?"

"I'm not certain, but one thing is clear—the receptionist must be breathing easier."

"Absolutely! Miss Sterling had her trembling with fear."

"We all assumed Amelia held the power to dismiss anyone. Who imagined the founder would be the one to reduce her to tears?"

"Quiet! Lower your voices. If Amelia's allies overhear, you'll face consequences."

"Frankly, I expected the founder to be more gentle-mannered. He proved surprisingly formidable."

"I suspect the founder already has a wife. If he's protecting someone, it must be his wife. He has no reason to show kindness to Amelia, correct?"
###Chapter 453

"Absolutely! The founder is famously devoted. Remember, he just dropped a billion on a necklace at auction for his wife."

"Oh, that's right! I completely forgot about that!"

Evelyn, however, had little interest in such gossip.

Whether it was Amelia Sterling or the enigmatic founder of Aethelgard Industries, their lives had little overlap with her own.

Her only flicker of curiosity was about the founder's appearance. This morning, her thoughts were completely occupied with Theodore Vance's design.

She and Alexander had agreed—since Theodore refused to take the money back, the only way to settle the debt was to finish the design and deliver it.

That would clear her obligation.

After two solid days of intense work, Evelyn finally completed the wedding gown Theodore had commissioned.

Once it was finished, Alexander arranged for it to be delivered directly to Theodore's residence.

They didn't bother to check if he received it personally.

That day, Evelyn was deep in her work when a company-wide announcement sparked a wave of excitement.

"The annual company event is coming up soon!"

"The prizes are always unbelievable! I wonder what they'll be this year!"

"Never mind the prizes, I just want some extra paid leave!"

Stepping out of her office, Evelyn heard the chatter and smiled.

"Sounds like we all share the same dream. A few extra days off would be perfect," she commented.

A few colleagues turned to her, smiling. "Evelyn, what brings you out here?"

She grinned. "I saw the announcement too."

Then, someone proposed a wild idea. "What if the grand prize this year is a private dinner with the founder himself?"

The office erupted with excited murmurs.

"Oh my God! That would be a dream come true! I'd die for that!"

"Imagine the incredibly fancy restaurant he'd take you to!"

"All you ever think about is food."

One colleague turned to Evelyn with a playful grin. "What about you? You must want this prize. You've always looked up to the founder, haven't you?"

A few others turned to look at her, their expressions curious.

"Is that true?"

Evelyn gave a light laugh. "Well, my introduction to design was through the company he built. It inspired me to enter this field, so in a way, he played a part in shaping my career path."

"Ohhh, that makes sense."

"But I highly doubt I'd win anything," Evelyn added with a shrug.

"You never know! Everyone has a chance!" a colleague encouraged.

Evelyn smiled but shook her head. "Even if I did win, I couldn't go. My husband is ridiculously overprotective. He'd undoubtedly create a massive scene over it, and frankly, I don't have the energy to handle that."

The room fell silent for a moment as the realization sunk in. Oh, right. Evelyn wasn't just married—her husband was Alexander Blackwood, the eldest son of the powerful Blackwood family.

Alexander had a formidable reputation in Crestwood, but her colleagues genuinely wished her happiness.

"She says it like it's a burden, but you can hear the fondness in her voice."

"She's so kind and stunning—if I were her husband, I'd be watching everyone around her too."
###Chapter 454

The comment made everyone laugh.

Evelyn smiled and shook her head. "You all are too much."

A colleague suddenly spoke up. "Evelyn, have you and Alexander discussed having children? You two are so perfect together, but we haven't heard any plans."

She paused for a moment, then answered with a gentle smile. "We've talked about it. If there's any news, you'll all be the first to know."

"With your genes, Evelyn, your child will be absolutely beautiful!"

After the cheerful chat, she returned to her desk and focused on her work.

But the subject stayed with her.

The thought of having a baby with Alexander was surprisingly sweet.

A soft smile touched her lips as she imagined it.

Her phone vibrated.

She picked it up.

A new message appeared on the screen.

"I have a business trip to a nearby city for a few days. Take care of yourself. I'll be back as soon as I can. Call me anytime if you need anything."

Evelyn was surprised. She hadn't expected him to leave so suddenly.

She typed a quick reply. "Did you pack everything?" "Yes, all done."

A moment later, another message arrived. "I noticed you were low on sanitary pads, so I restocked them. Your period is due soon. You might have cramps. Let me know if you feel unwell, okay?"

Evelyn smiled at his thoughtfulness. She responded, "I understand. Don't worry about me. Focus on your trip. I'll be fine."

He never missed even the smallest detail. It was incredibly touching.

That evening, when Evelyn got home, Alexander had already left.

The mansion seemed too still without him.
An unsettling silence had settled in the rooms.
Even the carefully prepared dinner on her plate tasted bland.

A maid approached, her voice soft with concern.
"Mrs. Blackwood, Mr. Blackwood was very specific before he departed."
"He insisted we ensure you eat properly."
"Shall I have the kitchen prepare a different dish for you?"

Evelyn blinked, pulled from her wandering thoughts.
She shook her head quickly.
"The food is fine, truly."
"I was just... thinking."

It wasn't the meal.
It was Alexander's absence that stole her appetite.

After forcing down a few bites, she retreated to their suite to get ready for bed.
Opening the bathroom cabinet, her gaze fell on the stack of sanitary pads.
He had arranged them himself, neatly.
A sharp pang of longing hit her chest.

He was gone, yet reminders of him were everywhere.
A small, involuntary sob escaped her.
She was surprised by the intensity of her own emotion.

She considered calling him.
But she knew he was likely immersed in his work.
With a heavy sigh, she slipped under the cool sheets, closing her eyes and begging for sleep to come.

This dependence on him was growing.
It was both comforting and terrifying.

Just as drowsiness began to pull her under, a thought electrified her nerves.
Her period.
It was late.

Evelyn bolted upright in the large bed.
She grabbed her phone from the nightstand, her fingers trembling as she opened the calendar app.

She scrolled back through the weeks, counting.
One month.
And then another two weeks.
Her heartbeat thundered in her ears, loud in the quiet room.

A single, staggering possibility filled her mind.
Could it be?
Was she pregnant?
###Chapter 455

Evelyn was awake.

Sleep wouldn't come.

She sat up, her excitement palpable.

She reached for her phone on the bedside table.

Should she tell Alexander right now?

She bit her lip, hesitating.

Then she decided not to.

Alexander was traveling for business.

He was completely focused on his work.

Asking him to return on such short notice was impossible.

Besides, this was just a suspicion.

It wasn't confirmed.

She remembered the last false alarm.

It had ended in disappointment for everyone.

This time, she would be sure first.

She resolved to go to the hospital for confirmation.

Only then would she surprise Alexander when he returned.

The plan solidified in her mind.

A fresh wave of excitement bubbled up inside her.

She took a deep, calming breath.

Her hand drifted unconsciously to her stomach.

"Let this be true," she whispered hopefully.

A hopeful smile touched her lips.

She quickly opened the calendar on her phone.

Saturday was only two days away.

It was the perfect day for her appointment.

Satisfied, Evelyn put her phone down.

She settled back into bed.

She closed her eyes, her mind alive with anticipation.

Slowly, sleep finally claimed her.

The night passed without dreams.

The next morning, Evelyn woke up feeling refreshed.

She arrived at work on time.

Her thoughts were already leaping ahead to Saturday.

The possibility of confirmation filled her with quiet joy.

Evelyn was deeply engrossed in her designs.

A soft knock at her office door broke her concentration.

"Evelyn," a colleague said.

Her expression was full of gossipy intrigue.

Her eyes sparkled with curiosity.

"You have a visitor."

Evelyn started to ask who it was.

But the woman leaned in closer first.

"It's Amelia Sterling," she murmured conspiratorially.

"She says she's here to discuss a design."

The news took Evelyn by surprise.

Amelia Sterling wanted a design from her?

The Sterling family had access to the world's top designers.

Yet Amelia had come here personally.

Uncertainty flickered within Evelyn.

She composed herself quickly.

"Has Miss Sterling been shown to the meeting room?" Evelyn asked.

"She has," the colleague confirmed with a quick nod.

"Alright. I'll go see her now."

Evelyn swiftly organized the papers on her desk.

She picked up her notebook and pen.

She walked down the hall toward the meeting room.

Evelyn stepped through the doorway.

Her eyes immediately found Amelia.

She was perched elegantly on the central sofa.

Her tailored designer suit was impeccable.

Her flawless hairstyle and overall appearance screamed old money.

It was the refined demeanor of the powerful Sterling family.

Hearing the door click, Amelia gently set her cup down.

She turned her head gracefully.

She looked at Evelyn but did not rise.

"Evelyn," she said, her voice cool. "It's been quite some time."

Evelyn gave a small, polite nod as she approached.

"Miss Sterling."

Just as Evelyn moved to sit, Amelia's voice cut through the air.

It held a casual authority.

"Evelyn, don't sit just yet."

Evelyn stopped mid-motion.

She stood poised, her expression serene as she met Amelia's gaze.

"Is there something else you need first, Miss Sterling?" Evelyn asked.

Amelia gestured negligently toward the abandoned coffee cup.

Her lips curled in mild distaste.

"I sampled the coffee here," she said. "I found it disappointingly ordinary."

She looked directly at Evelyn.

"Perhaps you could make a quick trip to my favorite café instead?"
###Chapter 456

Amelia Sterling’s intense stare remained fixed on Evelyn.

Evelyn felt a flicker of discomfort under the scrutiny.

She briefly looked away, composing herself before speaking. “Miss Sterling, have you come to discuss the design project?”

Amelia answered with deliberate casualness. “Naturally. Though I find it difficult to focus without my usual coffee.”

She leaned back, a blend of playfulness and pointedness in her tone. “You lead the Design Department at Aethelgard Industries. Surely, accommodating a client’s simple preference is within your purview?”

Evelyn immediately recognized the provocation.

Such hostility was familiar territory. Years of navigating corporate politics had sharpened her instincts.

Her naive days were long gone, replaced by a keen understanding of people’s motives.

She had no illusions about Amelia’s feelings toward her.

The underlying tension had been palpable since their encounter at the Sterling estate.

This clarity brought Evelyn a strange sense of calm. A faint smile touched her lips as she maintained her posture. “As you noted, my expertise lies in design. Personal errands, however, are best handled by dedicated services.”

Before Amelia could object, Evelyn was already reaching for her phone. “Given your background, you might not be familiar with concierge apps. I can recommend an excellent one for you.”

Amelia’s expression tightened, her smile vanishing. “I expected a more personal touch for such a simple request.”

Evelyn responded without hesitation, her voice firm. “That would be a misallocation of my skills. You sought me out for my talent. Why would I diminish that by running errands?”

Amelia studied Evelyn, struck by the quiet confidence radiating from her.

She clenched her jaw, unsettled by Evelyn’s complete lack of intimidation. Most people would be falling over themselves to please a Sterling.

Yet Evelyn behaved as if the Sterling name held no power over her.

This woman possessed a remarkable boldness.

The thought sparked a cunning idea in Amelia’s mind.

It occurred to her that the staff at Aethelgard Industries were probably unaware Evelyn was married to the company’s founder.

Did Evelyn even know the connection herself?

Perhaps she was determined to succeed on her own merits, refusing to leverage her husband’s immense influence.

Amelia found the possibility amusing. A subtle smirk played on her lips. “Evelyn, I heard a rumor you’re married. Is it true?”

Evelyn’s brow furrowed slightly, puzzled by the sudden shift. “Is my marital status relevant to our discussion?” she asked, her tone politely detached.

Amelia gave a soft, dismissive laugh. “There’s no need to be defensive. I was merely making conversation.”

Her eyes drifted to Evelyn, who had remained standing throughout. “Forget the coffee. Let’s sit down and begin properly.”
###Chapter 457

Evelyn glanced silently at Amelia before choosing to sit on the nearby sofa.

As she settled into the plush cushions, a memory surfaced.
She recalled Amelia's recent visit to Aethelgard Industries to meet its elusive founder.

Whispers from her colleagues echoed in her mind once more.

Could Amelia have developed feelings for the founder of Aethelgard Industries?

But what concern was that of hers?

Despite working there, Evelyn had never once laid eyes on the mysterious founder.

Why would Amelia connect her to that man?

The puzzle left Evelyn utterly perplexed.

She could think of no other reason for Amelia's sudden animosity.

They had only just met; their paths had never crossed before.

After a moment's thought, Evelyn decided on honesty. "I am married," she stated clearly.

She hoped this simple fact would dispel any unfounded suspicions.

The last thing she wanted was to make an unnecessary enemy.

Amelia leaned forward, her curiosity piqued. "Evelyn, with your talent, your husband must be quite remarkable. Does he own a business in the city? Perhaps I know him."

Evelyn maintained her composure. "Miss Sterling, you flatter me. My life is quite ordinary, as is my husband's."

Her expression shifted slightly. "If you are here to discuss business, let's be direct. There's no need to waste time."

Evelyn's bluntness visibly annoyed Amelia.

"Evelyn, I was merely hoping for a relaxed conversation. Must you be so formal?" She took a sharp breath. "Remember, the Sterling Group is about to form a major alliance with your firm. I am a Sterling. Your tone is surprisingly dismissive."

Evelyn remained calm. "I meant no offense. My schedule is packed today. If this is just a social call, perhaps we should reschedule."

Amelia scowled, shocked by the dismissal. She was not used to being treated this way by someone of a lower status.

She lifted her chin proudly. "I came here for a design. I'm eager to see the skills that made you head of the Design Department so quickly."

Without hesitation, Evelyn opened her portfolio. "Very well. Describe the garment you need. What is the occasion?"

Amelia's eyebrow arched. "Your firm is hosting the annual gala soon. As a major partner, my family will attend. I need an evening gown worthy of my status. It must be extraordinary."

Evelyn began taking notes. "Do you have any specific preferences, Miss Sterling?"

Amelia thought for a moment. "The design must be grand. I want a gown like no other. We can discuss details after I see your first draft."
###Chapter 458

"Understood," Evelyn said, her voice calm and even.

Amelia stood up, swinging her designer handbag onto her shoulder. She walked out alone. "Ensure the design is completed promptly. I will return to evaluate it in a few days."

Stepping out of the conference room, a glint of pure confidence shone in Amelia's eyes.

A subtle, knowing smile curved her lips.

Just as anticipated, Evelyn remained completely unaware that her husband was the powerful figure behind Aethelgard Industries. His choice to reveal this truth to Evelyn spoke clearly of his utter indifference towards her.

A beautiful face meant little without the right pedigree and connections.

She, Amelia Sterling, was in an entirely different league compared to Evelyn.

This certainty only served to amplify her self-assurance.

Meanwhile, Evelyn had just shut her notebook when her colleagues quickly swarmed around her desk.

"Evelyn, how did the meeting go?"

"What brought Miss Sterling here to see you?"

"It's a shock to see her back after she left in tears last time, all because of something involving our founder."

"I saw her leaving—Miss Sterling actually looked pleased."

"Our founder upset her, yet she's smiling after a meeting with you."

Evelyn let out a soft laugh at their wild speculations. "Your imaginations are truly something else."

Holding up her notebook, she raised an eyebrow. "Miss Sterling was here for a business discussion. That's all."

A wave of disappointment washed over the group.

"Just business? We were hoping for some real drama."

Evelyn playfully tapped the head of the most notorious gossip. "Always digging for scandal, aren't you? Want to know all the secrets."

She clapped her hands sharply to get their attention. "Enough chit-chat. Everyone back to work, unless you want to be here until midnight."

"Understood, Evelyn," they chorused, dispersing back to their workstations.

Evelyn released a quiet sigh of relief as they scattered.

Thankfully, the conference room's soundproof walls had kept her entire conversation with Amelia private.

Back in her private office, Evelyn placed her notebook on the desk.

Despite Amelia's agreement to proceed with the design collaboration, the probing questions about her personal life lingered uncomfortably.
