"What is the meaning of this?"

The hall fell instantly silent.
All eyes turned to see Jonathan Sterling striding forward.
His expression was dark and imposing.

Miriam's confidence faltered at his arrival.
Jonathan had instructed her to remove Evelyn discreetly.
Not to create a scene.

But Evelyn had been difficult to manipulate.
She had forced the situation into the open.

Now, if Jonathan discovered the deliberate framing...
The careful lies she had built for Amelia would crumble.

Miriam's mind raced.
She moved quickly before anyone else could react.

Bowing her head slightly, she spoke.
"Mr. Sterling, my sincere apologies."
"A thief appeared at Miss Sterling's birthday party."
"She stole one of the birthday gifts."
"We are handling the matter urgently."

Jonathan's brows furrowed.
"A thief?"
His gaze swept over the assembled guests.
His voice was calm, yet carried an icy edge.
"A thief? At our event?"

Then his eyes locked onto Evelyn.
She stood restrained by two large bodyguards.
Her posture was rigid, yet strangely composed.

His breath caught.
He had told Miriam to have this guest removed.
Why was she still here?

Then, as Amelia subtly moved closer to him, Jonathan's pulse quickened.
No.
Amelia must not notice.
She must not see the striking resemblance between him and Evelyn.
###Chapter 493

Jonathan understood Amelia's fragile nature.
If she suspected the truth, she would immediately think Evelyn was their father's secret child.
Amelia's heart could not handle such a shocking revelation.

Luckily, Amelia was too distracted to see the worry flash across Jonathan's face.
She jabbed an accusing finger toward Evelyn, her voice shrill with anger. "Jonathan! She's the thief who took my necklace. We have the security footage, but she still denies it! She's sabotaging my entire celebration!" Amelia gripped Jonathan's arm, shaking it pleadingly. "Jonathan, please! The guests are arriving. I won't let a common criminal ruin this night for me. You have to do something!"

To Amelia, every detail was falling perfectly into place.
Miriam had assured her that Jonathan was managing the situation for her benefit.
That was why she felt no hesitation in demanding his action.

Jonathan's expression darkened as he looked at Evelyn.
A wave of contempt rose within him.
So this woman wasn't just a nuisance; she was an actual thief.

"The evidence seems conclusive," he stated coldly. "Remove her for further questioning. Involve the authorities if needed. I will not have this spectacle disrupt the evening."

At his command, the security team moved swiftly, seizing Evelyn roughly.

Her eyes widened in shock.
He hadn't even examined the so-called proof.
He simply accepted Amelia's word without question.

Incredible.
How corrupt was this Sterling family?

Furious, Evelyn tried to speak, but a guard clamped a hand over her mouth, silencing her.

"Let me go!" She fought against their hold, but their grip was like iron.

Evelyn took a sharp breath and forced herself to go limp.
Resisting was useless now.
Instead, she became still, her icy gaze sweeping over everyone in the room.
Fine. She would wait.
When the time was right, she would make them all pay.

The moment Evelyn was taken away, the banquet hall's mood lightened instantly.
Conversations and laughter resumed, the party atmosphere quickly restored.

Miriam allowed herself a small, relieved smile.
The misunderstanding between Amelia and Jonathan had worked out better than she'd hoped.
The outcome was even smoother than she had anticipated.
Now, all that was left was to deal with Evelyn discreetly.

Meanwhile, Jonathan gently guided Amelia to a quieter corner.
He studied her face carefully. "Amelia," he asked, his voice softer now, filled with concern. "Are you truly alright?"

Amelia's heart swelled with triumph.
She gave him a bright, playful smile, taking his hand and swinging it gently. "Jonathan, you worry too much. What could possibly be wrong?"

Still holding his hand, she tilted her head, her voice sweet with gratitude. "But thank you for handling that so efficiently. That woman was becoming unbearable."

She knew perfectly well that Evelyn had never stolen the necklace.
The entire scene had been staged, orchestrated by Jonathan himself.
As long as he helped her eliminate Evelyn, she would happily play along with his little game.
###Chapter 494

Jonathan smiled, his hand gently tousling Amelia's hair with clear affection.

"I merely handled a minor inconvenience," he said casually, though the idea of that woman stealing from Amelia ignited a cold fury within him. "Did you extend the invitation to her personally? In the future, individuals of her character should not be permitted at our events. It's a security risk."

Amelia let out a light laugh. "Jonathan, Evelyn only attempted to steal something. The way you speak, one would think she had committed some heinous, unpardonable act."

Despite her dismissive tone, a deep sense of gratification warmed her from within.

Evelyn's social standing needed to be completely dismantled.

After a brief moment, Amelia added, her voice softening, "You needn't worry, Jonathan. I will have no further association with her."

Jonathan gave a firm nod, then glanced at his wristwatch. "The birthday celebration commences in ten minutes. Take a moment to compose yourself. I will instruct the staff to conduct a final review of the proceedings."

"Of course. Thank you, Jonathan," Amelia replied, her voice sweet as honey. She watched his retreating figure, a satisfied smile gracing her lips. Just then, Miriam approached and whispered urgently, "Miss Sterling, regarding the surveillance footage—"

"Silence!" Amelia's expression instantly darkened as she cut her off.

Her eyes darted around the room, ensuring no one was within earshot, before she lowered her voice to a harsh whisper. "You fool. If you were going to frame Evelyn, why was the preparation so sloppy? Had I not foreseen this complication and arranged for a substitute video, how would you have possibly contained the situation?"

Miriam offered an uncomfortable, tight-lipped smile. "Your foresight saved us. I never anticipated Evelyn would be so perceptive. She didn't take the bait at all."

Amelia sniffed derisively. "Jonathan obviously recognized your incompetence and intervened directly. And see? He had Evelyn removed effortlessly. He has saved us considerable trouble."

A flicker of profound unease crossed Miriam's face.

She still couldn't comprehend why Jonathan had moved so decisively to eliminate Evelyn.

The only plausible explanation was his deep fondness for Amelia.

But Miriam alone knew a terrible truth Jonathan was unaware of—the woman he had so casually ordered apprehended was his own biological sister.

How would he react if that secret ever came to light?

The thought sent a cold shiver down her spine.

No. This secret must never be revealed. She would do anything, everything, to secure Amelia's position within the Sterling family.

Growing impatient with Miriam's silent contemplation, Amelia was about to reprimand her when a cool, clear voice cut through the air.

"Amelia, you were the one who fabricated that surveillance video, weren't you?"

Amelia's heart seized, a spike of pure fear lancing through her. She looked up to see Madeline approaching, her face an unreadable mask, flanked by two of her companions.

The sight of them made Amelia's expression turn to stone.

Not these meddlesome women again!

And Madeline had publicly defended Evelyn earlier.

What was her purpose now?

Amelia clenched her jaw, meeting Madeline's gaze with defiant hostility. "I have no idea what you are referring to," she stated coldly.
###Chapter 495

Madeline Fitzgerald paused briefly, then plastered on a strained, polite smile.

"Evelyn, why such animosity towards me?"

"Our families have been business partners for a long time."

"Logically, we should be friends..."

Evelyn cut her off sharply, her voice dripping with disdain.

"Spare me the act!"

"Do you really think I'd believe this performance?"

"Have you forgotten who spread those disgusting rumors about me in school?"

"Stop pretending to be nice."

"I see right through you."

Amelia Sterling's face tightened.

She fought to maintain her composed, elegant facade.

Her lips twitched slightly, a barely perceptible sign of her irritation.

Damn Evelyn!

"Evelyn, you're mistaken."

"I had nothing to do with those accusations back then."

"Didn't I provide evidence that cleared my name?" Amelia forced another smile.

Evelyn let out a derisive laugh.

"Evidence?"

"You mean the lies you manufactured?"

"Just like the fake surveillance footage you produced today?"

A flicker of unease crossed Amelia's eyes.

"That footage wasn't altered."

"This is your first time meeting Evelyn, correct?"

"You don't know her true character."

"This isn't the first stunt she's pulled."

Evelyn seethed, watching Amelia's innocent act.

The urge to wipe that smug expression off her face was overwhelming.

Initially, she had believed Evelyn stole Amelia's necklace.

But when Evelyn was forcibly removed, Madeline caught the defiant fire in her eyes.

That look resonated deeply.

It mirrored the expression she herself once wore, drowning under a tide of vicious, humiliating gossip.

Perhaps it was a silent understanding between those wronged.

In that moment, Madeline's heart raced.

A powerful impulse to step in and rescue Evelyn nearly took over.

She was now convinced.

Evelyn was innocent.

She had been framed!

But without solid proof, the Sterling family wouldn't release her.

So, Madeline confided in her friends.

She voiced her suspicions and outlined a plan to uncover the truth.

Fortunately, they supported her.

They agreed to help clear Evelyn's name.

Before they could act, however, Madeline spotted Amelia and Miriam lurking suspiciously.

She didn't hesitate to confront Amelia.

As expected, the woman was utterly insufferable!

Madeline eyed Amelia's arrogant posture and scoffed.

"I may not know Evelyn well."

"But I know exactly what kind of person you are."

"You'd better hope nothing ruins your precious birthday party tonight."

With that, Madeline turned abruptly.

She strode away, pulling her friends along with her.

Amelia clenched her jaw, fury simmering beneath the surface.

"That wretched girl," she muttered under her breath.

"Who does she think she is, challenging me?"

Soon, she would marry the founder of Aethelgard Industries.

Then, she would be the most influential woman in all of Crestwood.

Meanwhile, Evelyn was shoved into a dim storage room on the hotel's ground floor.

"Get in!" The bodyguard pushed her inside and barked, "Stay quiet."

Evelyn stumbled forward, grabbing a metal shelf to steady herself.

A moment later, the door slammed shut.

Complete darkness enveloped the room.
###Chapter 496

A sudden, sharp pain stabbed through Evelyn's heart.

She took a deep breath, forcing herself to remain calm.

Her hands reached out into the oppressive darkness surrounding her.

"Even a storage room must have a light switch," she whispered to herself.

Her fingers trailed along the cold wall, searching blindly.

Finally, they brushed against a small, plastic panel.

Relief washed over her as she flipped the switch.

Instantly, bright, harsh light flooded the small space.

Evelyn winced, her eyes struggling to adjust after the absolute blackness.

She blinked rapidly, taking in her new environment.

It was surprisingly tidy. Neatly stacked boxes lined the walls.

Not the damp, filthy dungeon she had feared the Sterlings would throw her into.

A bitter laugh escaped her lips. The Sterlings were certainly cruel enough for that.

But here she was, finding humor in the situation. Perhaps a little hope was necessary.

She had to think of the baby now. Her own safety was secondary.

She needed to stay calm, for the child's sake.

Evelyn found a sturdy-looking crate and sat down.

Her mind began working, plotting a way out of this nightmare.

Amelia's evidence was a complete fabrication. Evelyn had never been near any lounge.

She hadn't even seen the necklace she was accused of stealing.

The security footage from the banquet hall was her only hope.

A wave of frustration hit her. "I suggested checking the cameras, but I never got to see them," she muttered.

Now she was trapped. The Sterlings could easily erase the truth before she could prove it.

She sighed, regret twisting inside her. If only she had been more careful.

Her thoughts turned to Jonathan Sterling.

The man who had so coldly ordered her imprisonment.

10:56

496 What A Scoundrel!

"What a complete scoundrel! He bumps into me, blames me for it, and then condemns me without a second thought. The Sterling siblings are truly cut from the same cloth."

If they were determined to frame her, she couldn't fight this alone.

She pressed her lips together firmly and reached for her phone.

A small stroke of luck—she still had it. It wasn't in her purse.

Her gaze fell on her message thread with Alexander.

Should she call him? He was away on business. He might not be able to help in time.

She hesitated, her thumb hovering over Charlotte's contact.

Charlotte had her own company, but she was no match for the powerful Sterling family.

Dragging her best friend into this mess was not an option.

Evelyn racked her brain, searching for another solution.

Then, a name surfaced in her mind with sudden clarity.

It seemed she had only one viable choice left.

She couldn't wage this war alone anymore. Not while carrying a child.

The baby's safety was paramount.

A sense of resolve settled over her. The decision was made.

She quickly typed out a concise message.

Her finger pressed 'send'.

At that exact moment, the door was flung open with a violent crash.

Evelyn jolted, her entire body tensing in alarm.

A smug, taunting voice filled the small room. "Evelyn, how does it feel to be locked away like an animal?"

She looked up.

Her eyes met Amelia Sterling's mocking gaze, dripping with malicious amusement.
###Chapter 497

Amelia Sterling swept into the room with an air of absolute authority.

Miriam Croft trailed behind her, a cunning smile playing on her lips.

Two heavily built security guards flanked the doorway, their muscular frames blocking any possibility of escape.

Not that Evelyn had any intention of fleeing.

Even without the guards, attempting to run in an evening gown and high heels would have been pointless.

She resolved to conserve her energy, to remain calm, and to play for time.

This decision brought a certain steadiness to her frayed nerves.

Her eyes darted briefly to Miriam before locking onto Amelia. Her voice was remarkably composed. "This was all planned from the start, wasn't it? The party was a setup."

Amelia let out a light, mocking laugh, drawing out her words. "You're not entirely wrong."

She offered no further elaboration.

The initial idea had been simple—to humiliate Evelyn. Theft wasn't part of the original script.

But since Miriam had already set the scene, Amelia saw no reason not to go along with it—as long as the final outcome served her purpose.

Evelyn considered this for a moment before speaking again. "Your maid had no real evidence against me initially. She was just trying to create chaos and drag me away. If I had panicked, she might have succeeded. But I didn't. I held my ground, even when some of the guests began to doubt."

Miriam's smug expression wavered for an instant.

She hadn't anticipated Evelyn piecing the plot together so swiftly.

A flash of annoyance crossed Amelia's face before she concealed it with a smirk. "A pity you figured it out so late."

Evelyn remained unshaken. A faint, knowing smile touched her lips.

"When the accusation alone wasn't enough, you moved quickly. You produced a fake surveillance video and revealed it at the perfect moment." Evelyn's tone was analytical, almost detached, as if discussing a stranger's misfortune. "Once that footage played, everything I had said before became meaningless. The contrast made it easy for everyone to believe you. No one cared about my side of the story anymore. To them, I was just a liar wasting their time."

Amelia scoffed in exaggerated exasperation. "And? What's your point? Do you expect me to applaud your brilliant deduction?"

With theatrical slowness, she raised her hands and delivered a slow, sarcastic clap.

"Bravo! Evelyn, you're a genius. You've solved the entire mystery. I'm truly impressed." She leaned in slightly, her voice dripping with mockery. "Perhaps you should abandon fashion design and become a private investigator."

Before Evelyn could reply, Amelia straightened up, crossing her arms as she paced a short distance. "But honestly? I didn't plan every single detail. I do what I want, and no one stops me. You were simply unlucky. If you hadn't crossed my path, none of this would have happened."

Evelyn studied her intently, her gaze steady. "Amelia, I truly want to understand. What did I ever do to you? Why are you so determined to ruin my life?"
###Chapter 498

Evelyn drew a sharp breath, her forehead creasing with tension.

"From my perspective, we have no real quarrel. We exist in entirely separate spheres. Why resort to such drastic measures against me?"

Amelia remained silent.

But Evelyn didn't miss the flash of pure envy that crossed the other woman's impeccably made-up face.

Why? Amelia possessed everything—prestige, immense wealth, the undisputed status of a Sterling. What motive could she possibly have for tormenting a simple designer?

"Silence!" Amelia's voice cracked through the air like a whip. She spun around and shoved Evelyn violently.

Evelyn gasped, caught completely off guard. Her body was thrown sideways.

Pure, unadulterated panic surged through her, obliterating all other thought. Protect the baby.

Instinct took command. She twisted her body mid-fall, one arm flying to shield her abdomen while her foot scrambled for purchase against the wall's corner.

A sharp, burning pain erupted in her wrist and ankle. The color drained from her face.

Simultaneously, her shoulder collided with a metal shelving unit. The loud, jarring crash reverberated through the confined storage room.

Even Miriam froze in shock. She hadn't anticipated Amelia would become physically aggressive so abruptly.

For a fleeting second, her hand jerked forward as if to assist Evelyn. But she checked the impulse.

Sympathy for Evelyn was a luxury she could not afford.

Agony radiated through Evelyn's frame. A cold sweat broke out on her brow as she slid weakly to the cold floor. A trembling hand rose to touch her throbbing shoulder.

It hurt. Every part of her screamed in pain, except for one crucial area. Her abdomen felt undisturbed.

Amelia stood over her, eyes gleaming with anything but regret. If anything, she looked satisfied.

Had she known how gratifying it would feel to put Evelyn in her place, she would have acted sooner.

After all, the founder of Aethelgard Industries clearly held no affection for his so-called wife.

And as a daughter of the powerful Sterling family, she had every right to discipline a common upstart. The thought sent a thrill of pleasure through her. An involuntary, cruel smirk touched her lips.

She released a theatrical sigh, shaking her head in mock pity. "Oh, my. Look at you. How utterly pathetic."

10:56

The sharp, precise click of Amelia's heels echoed menacingly in the cramped space. Each step felt like a weight pressing down on Evelyn's chest.

Amelia's gaze dropped to Evelyn's trembling hand.

"Why is your hand shaking?" Amelia's tone feigned concern, but her voice was utterly devoid of warmth. "Did you sprain it? Or perhaps you hit it on something?" Without a moment's hesitation, she lifted her foot and pressed the ridged sole of her expensive designer shoe onto Evelyn's injured hand. Then she applied pressure, grinding down.

"Ah!" A ragged cry was torn from Evelyn's throat. The pain was excruciating, like white-hot lightning. Desperately, she tried to push the elegant heel away. "Get off!"

Searing agony shot up her arm. Her breathing became sharp, ragged gasps.

Her face was drenched in cold sweat within seconds.

The veins in her neck stood out against her pale skin as she strained against the crushing weight. Amelia did not yield.

Tears welled in Evelyn's eyes. Not from weakness. From sheer, overwhelming pain.

Miriam watched from across the room, her own heart hammering against her ribs.
###Chapter 499

A wave of dizziness washed over Evelyn.
Was it her illness, or the sheer horror of the scene?
She couldn't watch any longer.

"Miss Sterling..." Miriam's voice was strained. "The birthday celebration will begin soon. You should go and prepare."

Amelia withdrew her foot instantly.
Her interest had vanished.
She stood with a casual, almost bored posture.

Miriam's gaze fell upon Evelyn's injured hand.
She froze for a second.

Evelyn instinctively tried to pull her hand back.
But the wound on her arm made every movement agonizingly slow.
A fresh wave of pain shot through her.

Her fingers trembled uncontrollably.
Her vision blurred for a moment.
She cradled her injured hand against her chest.
Her breathing was ragged.
A single tear escaped, tracing a path through the grime and blood on her skin.

Amelia let out a soft, satisfied hum. "You're right. I shouldn't keep my guests waiting."

She turned away, humming a light tune as if she'd just finished a pleasant stroll.
"Stay here. Watch her. I'll deal with this after the party."

"Yes, Miss Sterling," Miriam murmured, her head bowed.

Amelia swept out of the storage room, her attendants following like a silent parade.
The heavy door clicked shut.
Thick, suffocating silence descended, leaving only Evelyn and Miriam.

Miriam stood rigidly by the door.
She forced herself to remember her mission.
Protect her daughter.
That singular thought was the only anchor against the tidal wave of guilt threatening to drown her.

Years ago, she had made a choice.
A choice that irrevocably altered Evelyn's destiny.
She had personally swapped the Sterling family's true heiress—Evelyn—with her own child.
After the switch, she hadn't even bothered to raise the girl she'd condemned.
She had abandoned her without a backward glance.
But fate had a vicious sense of humor.

Miriam was lost in the bitter past.
Evelyn's hoarse voice suddenly cut through the silence, sharp as broken glass. "At the last Sterling family banquet. You tried to kill me, didn't you?"

Miriam's breath caught in her throat.
Her heart hammered against her ribs, a frantic, panicked rhythm.
So she remembered...

Evelyn studied Miriam's horrified expression.
She then shifted her gaze to the dusty floor, her voice low but steady. "When I saw you tonight, it all came back. You're just a servant in the Sterling household. We should have no connection. And yet... you wanted me dead."

Miriam forced air into her lungs.
She had to remain calm. "You are mistaken. You are here because you offended Miss Sterling."

Evelyn turned her head, her piercing eyes locking onto Miriam's. "Then tell me, how did I offend Amelia?"

She didn't wait for a reply.
She continued, her voice slow and deliberate, like someone assembling clues. "The first time I met Amelia was at the Sterling banquet. Initially, it was just an accident—a simple collision. She's spoiled and volatile, so she lashed out. I understood that. But everything changed the moment she saw the invitation in my hand. That's when her dislike turned into pure hatred."

Her voice dropped to a whisper. "Is there something about the Sterling family's invitation that I don't know?"

Her mind flashed back to that night.
Because of that accident, she had sprained her ankle.
She never got the chance to ask Alexander about it.

But now, she was certain.
There was a secret tied to that invitation.
A secret that explained why Amelia's hostility had escalated into murderous intent.
###Chapter 500

Miriam Croft hesitated.
She struggled to follow Evelyn’s train of thought.

At the Sterling family’s event, she hadn’t even met Amelia Sterling.
Because of that, she had no idea what Amelia had been thinking back then.

Eventually, she did meet her biological daughter, Amelia.
To keep the truth about her and Evelyn’s origins hidden, Miriam had to weave a lie.
She claimed Evelyn had repeatedly sought out the founder of Aethelgard Industries with ulterior motives.
It was the only way to sway Amelia to her side.
She needed to turn Amelia against Evelyn, the rightful daughter of the Sterling family.
After all, Amelia’s interest in the founder wasn’t a secret within the Sterling household.

But now, after she watched Amelia lash out at Evelyn, doubt crept into Miriam’s mind.
Did Amelia know something?
Something deeper?
Was that why her hatred toward Evelyn ran so deep?

Still, Miriam was sure Amelia remained unaware of the real truth.
If Amelia knew Evelyn was the Sterling family’s true daughter, given her fiery temper, she would have already made Evelyn’s life unbearable.

"Evelyn, don’t expect to get anything out of me. I’m just a servant of the Sterling family," Miriam said coolly.
She drew a deep breath.

Evelyn met her gaze calmly.
After a brief pause, she spoke with conviction.
"So, you don’t know the real reason either."

Miriam’s heart skipped a beat.
Evelyn was sharp.
Too sharp.
If she kept piecing things together at this pace, it was only a matter of time before she uncovered the truth about her own origins.

A sudden wave of fear gripped Miriam.
She had made a mistake.
She shouldn’t have lingered here with Evelyn.
She should have left with Amelia.

As Miriam pulled herself together, her eyes landed on the deep, blood-red wound on Evelyn’s hand.
Pressing her lips together, she said dryly, "I’ll get you a first aid kit. Stay put."

Without waiting for a response, Miriam turned and walked swiftly toward the supply closet.
It almost seemed as if she were fleeing.

Evelyn’s gaze followed her.
As Miriam reached the door, Evelyn’s eyes flickered.
She had noticed something.

The hallway was empty.
Amelia’s bodyguards must have left with her.

A thought crossed Evelyn’s mind.
She spoke casually.
"I’m a little thirsty. Could you bring me some water?"

Miriam, still preoccupied, answered briskly.
"Fine."

Evelyn wasn’t in any condition to escape.
There was no harm in indulging her request.

Just as Miriam was about to shut the door, Evelyn suddenly asked, "Have we met before? Perhaps... At a hospital?"

Miriam froze.
Through the narrow gap in the door, Evelyn’s calm, unwavering gaze met hers.
It sent a jolt of panic through her chest.

How terrifying this woman was!
How could she remember something from so long ago?

Without thinking, Miriam slammed the door shut.
She cut off any further conversation.

Leaning against it, she inhaled deeply.
She tried to steady her racing heart.

For a moment, she closed her eyes.
When she opened them again, her expression was cold.
It was filled with quiet resentment.

She shouldn’t have wavered.
She shouldn’t have felt even a shred of sympathy for Evelyn.

With Amelia still holding the title of the Sterling family’s cherished daughter, now was the perfect time.
She could get rid of Evelyn once and for all.

Once Evelyn was out of the picture, no one could challenge Amelia’s place in the family.

Steeling herself, Miriam pulled a key from her pocket.
Without hesitation, she locked the storage room’s door from the outside.
